# Macro Desktop MFE — Full Repository Documentation

> **llms-full.txt** — single-file, LLM-consumable documentation of the `CapitalMarketsMacro/macro`
> monorepo, per the llms.txt convention (https://llmstxt.org/). It is generated by analyzing the
> repository source and is self-contained: an LLM (or engineer) without repo access should be able
> to answer detailed questions about the platform from this file alone. Regenerate it whenever a
> subsystem changes materially.

- **Generated:** 2026-07-11
- **Repo state:** branch `master`, commit `4881206` (post PR #81 "feat(prism): add AMPS Top N and ordering")
- **Platform:** NX 23 monorepo — Angular 22 (zoneless) + React 19 + Vite 8 + OpenFin Workspace 24.0.19
  (HERE Core UI, runtime 44.146.101.5) Capital Markets desktop platform with shared `@macro/*` libraries
  for real-time market data, enterprise messaging (AMPS / Solace / NATS+JetStream), FDC3 2.0 interop,
  AG Grid 36 Enterprise, and a unified multi-user / multi-environment Workspace Storage API.
- **Companion docs:** `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` (agent instructions), `docs/architecture.md` (Mermaid
  diagrams), `docs/api/workspace-storage-api.openapi.yaml` (storage contract), per-app `README.md`s.

## Table of Contents

 1. Repository Overview & Quick Reference

10. Lib: @macro/openfin — platform services (non-storage)
11. Lib: @macro/openfin — unified Workspace Storage subsystem
12. Lib: @macro/transports — unified messaging (AMPS, Solace, NATS/JetStream)
13. Lib: @macro/prism-core — framework-free blotter core
14. Lib: @macro/macro-design — design system & theming
15. Grid stack: @macro/macro-grid-format, @macro/macro-angular-grid, @macro/macro-react-grid
16. Libs: @macro/utils & @macro/logger
17. Repo-root asset trees: design-system/ & AgenticUI/
18. OpenFin platform configuration (apps/macro-workspace/public)
19. Workspace Storage API contract (docs/api) & other docs
20. Tooling, testing & CI
21. AI assistant & coding-agent configuration

---

## Repository Overview & Quick Reference

**macro** (npm package name `@macro/source`, version `0.0.0`, private, `license: MIT`) is an NX 23 monorepo implementing a Capital Markets desktop platform. It combines Angular 22 (all Angular apps zoneless), React 19 + Vite 8, and OpenFin Workspace 24.0.19 (HERE Core UI, runtime 44.146.101.5) into one desktop experience: an OpenFin platform shell (`macro-workspace`) hosts framework-mixed views (FX/Treasury market-data apps, an FDC3 instrument viewer, two "Prism" blotter apps) wired together through FDC3 2.0 context sharing, a Dock 3.0 bar, a Storefront app store, Home search, Snap docking, and a unified Workspace Storage API for all user persistence.

Shared capability lives in `libs/` and is imported via `@macro/*` path aliases from `tsconfig.base.json` (source-level imports — most libs have no build step for app consumption). The libraries cover design tokens/theming (`@macro/macro-design`), structured logging (`@macro/logger`), a unified messaging layer for AMPS / Solace / NATS+JetStream (`@macro/transports` with `/angular` and `/react` adapters), OpenFin platform services + the `WorkspaceStorageClient` (`@macro/openfin`), RxJS conflation (`@macro/utils`), a framework-free blotter core (`@macro/prism-core`), a framework-free column-format engine + AG Grid tool panels (`@macro/macro-grid-format`), AG Grid 36 Enterprise wrappers for both frameworks, and shared MCP server internals (`@macro/mcp-core`).

Real-time data is simulated by `market-data-server` (Node.js, port 3000): WebSocket FX/Treasury ticks, a Prism JSON table protocol (WS + REST mirror), and the phase‑1 reference implementation of the Workspace Storage REST API at `/workspace/v1`. Repo state documented here: branch `master`, commit `4881206` (post PR #81 "AMPS Top N and ordering"), 2026‑07‑11.

### Repository layout (top two levels)

```
macro/
├── apps/
│   ├── capital-markets-themes/   # React 19 theme/design-system showcase (Vite, :4206)
│   ├── macro-angular/            # Angular market-data app (:4200)
│   ├── macro-angular-fdc3/       # Angular FDC3 instrument viewer (:4203)
│   ├── macro-mcp/                # MCP server, stdio transport (no port)
│   ├── macro-mcp-agent/          # MCP server, HTTP/SSE via express (default :3100, env MCP_PORT)
│   ├── macro-react/              # React market-data app (:4201)
│   ├── macro-workspace/          # OpenFin Workspace platform shell (:4202)
│   ├── market-data-server/       # Node WS + REST server (:3000, env PORT)
│   ├── prism/                    # Prism blotter, Angular (:4204)
│   └── prism-react/              # Prism blotter, React (:4205)
├── libs/
│   ├── logger/  macro-angular-grid/  macro-design/  macro-grid-format/
│   ├── macro-react-grid/  mcp-core/  openfin/  prism-core/  transports/  utils/
├── docs/
│   ├── api/                      # workspace-storage-api.openapi.yaml + Postman collection
│   ├── architecture.md           # Mermaid system-context / dependency diagrams
│   ├── copilot/                  # ag-grid-36-format-panel-port.md (full port guide)
│   ├── prism/                    # RELEASE-NOTES.md
│   └── statusMay2026/            # capability deck (HTML/PDF)
├── scripts/                      # build-ci.mjs, test-ci.mjs, stop.mjs, dos.mjs,
│                                 # list-npm-packages.mjs, amps-sow-query.ts, amps-query-executions.ts
├── tools/
│   ├── prism-broker-lab/         # docker-compose.yml (NATS/Solace), feeder.mjs, nats.conf
│   ├── extract-dependencies.js
│   └── generate-favicons.ps1
├── AgenticUI/  design-system/    # tracked non-code assets: decks, icon system (SVG sets, showcases)
├── CLAUDE.md  AGENTS.md          # agent instructions (kept byte-identical to each other)
├── README.md                     # human quick start (NOTE: stale in places — says "NX 22.5", "Vite 7")
├── llms-full.txt                 # this file — single-file LLM documentation of the repo
├── nx.json  tsconfig.base.json  package.json  package-lock.json  .npmrc
├── eslint.config.mjs  jest.config.ts  jest.preset.js  vitest.config.ts
└── .github/                      # ci.yml workflow, copilot-instructions.md
```

`coverage/`, `dist/`, `reports/`, `tmp/`, and root `junit.xml` are git-ignored build/test outputs.

### Applications

| App | Port | Framework | Serve command | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `macro-angular` | 4200 | Angular 22 (zoneless), PrimeNG 21, AG Grid/AG Charts | `npm run start:angular` | FX Market Data + Treasury Microstructure views |
| `macro-react` | 4201 | React 19 + Vite 8, Shadcn/Radix, Tailwind 4, Recharts | `npm run start:react` | Treasury Market Data + Commodities Dashboard views |
| `macro-workspace` | 4202 | Angular 22 (zoneless) | `npm run start:workspace` | OpenFin platform provider: Dock 3.0, Storefront, Home, Snap, Analytics Dashboard, storage-env picker; serves per-env config under `public/{local,openshift}/` |
| `macro-angular-fdc3` | 4203 | Angular 22 (zoneless) | `npm run start:fdc3` | FDC3 2.0 instrument viewer (green context group) |
| `prism` | 4204 | Angular 22 (zoneless), PrimeNG | `npm run start:prism` | Prism "Blotter as a Service" — multi-source blotters (WS/REST/AMPS/NATS/Solace) with roll-ups |
| `prism-react` | 4205 | React 19 + Vite 8, Shadcn/Radix | `npm run start:prism-react` | React twin of the Prism blotter on shared `@macro/prism-core` |
| `capital-markets-themes` | 4206 | React 19 + Vite 8 | `npm run start:capital-markets-themes` | Theme / design-system showcase (Figma-derived), served under base `/capital-markets-themes/` |
| `market-data-server` | 3000 (`PORT` env) | Node.js (`ws` + `express`) | `npm run start:market-data-server` | Simulated FX (`/marketData/fx`) + Treasury (`/marketData/tsy`) ticks, Prism table protocol (`/prism` WS + `/prism/tables` REST), Workspace Storage reference API (`/workspace/v1`) |
| `macro-mcp` | — (stdio) | Node.js + `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` | `npm run build:mcp` then run from `dist/` | MCP server for repo scaffolding / Figma import; thin entrypoint over `@macro/mcp-core` |
| `macro-mcp-agent` | 3100 (`MCP_PORT` env) | Node.js + express (Streamable HTTP + SSE transports) | `npm run start:mcp-agent` | Same `@macro/mcp-core` tools exposed remotely for deployment |

`npm start` runs the core four concurrently (workspace, angular, react, fdc3 via `concurrently -n workspace,angular,react,fdc3`). Launch OpenFin afterwards with `npm run launch` (runs `node apps/macro-workspace/launch.mjs http://localhost:4202/local/manifest.fin.json`).

Gotcha: `apps/capital-markets-themes/` contains BOTH `vite.config.ts` (port 4206, base `/capital-markets-themes/`) and a leftover `vite.config.mts` (port 4200). Vite's config resolution picks `vite.config.ts` first, so 4206 is the effective port; its `project.json` declares no explicit targets (all inferred by the `@nx/vite` plugin).

### Shared libraries

| Alias (from `tsconfig.base.json`) | Directory | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `@macro/logger` | `libs/logger` | Pino-based structured logging (also npm-publishable: `npm run publish:logger`) |
| `@macro/transports` (+ `/angular`, `/react`) | `libs/transports` | Unified messaging: `AmpsTransport`, `SolaceTransport`, `NatsTransport` incl. JetStream; Angular services + React hooks |
| `@macro/openfin` (+ `/react`, `/theme-sync`) | `libs/openfin` | OpenFin Workspace services (dock/store/home/snap/theme/context/notifications/analytics), `WorkspaceStorageClient`, `ViewStateService`/`useViewState` |
| `@macro/macro-design` (+ `/angular`, `/react`) | `libs/macro-design` | Single source of truth for CSS variables, fonts, dark mode (`getInitialIsDark`/`applyDarkMode`/`onSystemThemeChange`), `buildAgGridTheme(isDark)`, OpenFin theme palettes |
| `@macro/utils` | `libs/utils` | RxJS conflation utilities (`ConflationSubject`, double-buffer) |
| `@macro/prism-core` | `libs/prism-core` | Framework-free blotter core: sources, feeds (`BlotterFeed`), `WsTableClient`, `RestSnapshotClient`, column inference, `RollupConfig`/`suggestRollup`, AMPS Top‑N/ordering |
| `@macro/macro-grid-format` (+ `/angular`, `/react`) | `libs/macro-grid-format` | Column-format engine + AG Grid Format/calc tool panels shared by both grid wrappers |
| `@macro/macro-angular-grid` | `libs/macro-angular-grid` | AG Grid 36 Enterprise Angular wrapper (`MacroAngularGrid`, `updateRows$`) |
| `@macro/macro-react-grid` | `libs/macro-react-grid` | AG Grid 36 Enterprise React wrapper (`MacroReactGrid`, `MacroReactGridRef` forwardRef) |
| `@macro/mcp-core` | `libs/mcp-core` | Shared MCP tools/resources/prompts (`registerTools`, `registerResources`, `registerPrompts`) used by both MCP server apps |

### npm scripts (complete, from `package.json`)

**Dev serve / stop**

| Script | Command / effect |
|---|---|
| `start` | `concurrently` serve of macro-workspace + macro-angular + macro-react + macro-angular-fdc3 |
| `start:workspace` / `start:angular` / `start:react` / `start:fdc3` / `start:prism` / `start:prism-react` / `start:market-data-server` / `start:capital-markets-themes` / `start:mcp-agent` | `nx serve <project>` |
| `serve:workspace` / `serve:angular` / `serve:react` / `serve:prism` / `serve:prism-react` / `serve:market-data-server` | aliases for the same `nx serve` targets |
| `stop` | `node scripts/stop.mjs` — kills this repo's dev servers via port scan + process-tree sweep (repo-scoped; deliberately excludes MCP/editor tooling) |
| `stop:all` | same `--with-openfin` (also kills OpenFin processes) |

**OpenFin**

| Script | Command / effect |
|---|---|
| `launch` | `node apps/macro-workspace/launch.mjs http://localhost:4202/local/manifest.fin.json` |
| `launch:openshift` | echoes instructions for launching against the OpenShift-hosted manifest |
| `dos` | `node scripts/dos.mjs` — sets HKCU `DesktopOwnerSettings` → `http://localhost:4202/local/dos.json` (pins the Beta-channel Workspace 24 UI; backs up prior value to `scripts/.dos-backup.json`; Windows registry, restart OpenFin after) |
| `dos:restore` | restores/removes the previous registry value |

**Prism broker lab**

| Script | Command / effect |
|---|---|
| `prism:brokers` | `docker compose -f tools/prism-broker-lab/docker-compose.yml up -d` (local NATS + Solace) |
| `prism:brokers:down` | tears the brokers down |
| `prism:feed` | `node tools/prism-broker-lab/feeder.mjs` (publishes blotter data into the brokers) |

**Build**

| Script | Command / effect |
|---|---|
| `build` | CI entry point: `node scripts/build-ci.mjs` — sets `CI=true` in-process (cross-platform, no cross-env) then runs `build:apps:ci`; exits non-zero on any failure |
| `build:apps:ci` | ordered production builds of macro-angular, macro-angular-fdc3, macro-react, macro-workspace, prism, prism-react, market-data-server, macro-mcp + `build:mcp-agent` — the single source of truth for the CI app list; new apps must be added here |
| `build:workspace` / `build:angular` / `build:react` / `build:fdc3` / `build:prism` / `build:prism-react` / `build:market-data-server` / `build:capital-markets-themes` / `build:logger` / `build:mcp` | `nx build <project>` |
| `build:mcp-agent` | `nx build macro-mcp-agent` then patches the exact installed `amps` version into `dist/apps/macro-mcp-agent/package.json` dependencies (inline node -e) |
| `build:apps` | `nx run-many` over macro-angular, macro-react, macro-workspace, macro-angular-fdc3, market-data-server, macro-mcp |
| `build:libs` | `nx run-many` over logger, transports, openfin, macro-design, macro-angular-grid, macro-react-grid, utils |
| `buildAll` | Windows-flavored (`set "CI=true"`) build+test combo chain (legacy; `build`+`test` are the CI entry points) |
| `exec:market-data-server` | builds then runs `node dist/apps/market-data-server/apps/market-data-server/src/main.js` |

**Test**

| Script | Command / effect |
|---|---|
| `test` | `node scripts/test-ci.mjs` — every project with coverage; merges LCOV → `coverage/lcov.info` and JUnit → root `junit.xml` (merges even on failure, then exits non-zero) |
| `test:ci` | alias for `npm run test` |
| `test:all` | `nx run-many --target=test --all` (no coverage/report merge) |
| `test:apps:ci` | the four core apps only, with coverage (`:test:ci` Jest configs + Vitest `--coverage`) |

**Publish**

| Script | Command / effect |
|---|---|
| `publish:logger` / `publish:logger:dry-run` | build `libs/logger` then `npm publish --prefix dist/libs/logger` (± `--dry-run`) |

### Key dependency versions (`package.json`, commit `4881206`)

| Area | Package(s) | Version |
|---|---|---|
| Monorepo | `nx`, `@nx/{angular,react,vite,vitest,jest,js,node,esbuild,eslint,web,workspace,devkit}` | `23.0.0` (exact) |
| Angular | `@angular/core` et al. | `~21.2.17` (build/cli `~21.2.16`) |
| React | `react`, `react-dom` | `^19.2.7` |
| Bundler | `vite` | `^8.0.16` (also pinned via root `overrides`) |
| Router | `react-router-dom` | `^7.16.0` |
| AG Grid | `ag-grid-{community,enterprise,angular,react}` | `^36.0.0` |
| AG Charts | `ag-charts-{enterprise,angular,react}` | `^14.0.0` |
| PrimeNG | `primeng` / `@primeuix/themes` / `primeicons` | `^21.1.8` / `^2.0.3` / `^7.0.0` |
| PrimeReact | `primereact` | `^11.0.0-alpha.10` (alpha — expect breaking changes) |
| Tailwind | `tailwindcss`, `@tailwindcss/vite`, `@tailwindcss/postcss` | `^4.3.0` |
| OpenFin | `@openfin/workspace`, `@openfin/workspace-platform` | `24.0.19` (exact) |
| OpenFin | `@openfin/core`, `@openfin/node-adapter` | `44.101.5` (exact peer pair; manifest runtime 44.146.101.5) |
| OpenFin | `@openfin/snap-sdk` / `@openfin/automation-helpers` | `^1.6.0` / `1.3.1` |
| FDC3 | `@finos/fdc3` | `2.2.3` |
| Messaging | `amps` | `^5.3.4-0.378635.60b2fcc` |
| Messaging | `@nats-io/nats-core`, `@nats-io/jetstream`, `@nats-io/kv` | `^3.4.0` |
| Messaging | `solclientjs` | `^10.18.2` |
| MCP | `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` | `^1.26.0` |
| Logging | `pino` / `pino-pretty` | `^10.1.0` / `^13.1.2` |
| Server | `express` / `ws` / `zod` | `^5.2.1` / `^8.18.3` / `^4.3.6` |
| Language | `typescript` | `~6.0.3` |
| RxJS | `rxjs` | `~7.8.2` |
| Charts (React) | `recharts` | `^3.4.1` |
| Test | `jest` / `jest-preset-angular` / `vitest` | `30.3.0` / `16.1.4` / `^4.1.7` |

Install note: root `.npmrc` sets `legacy-peer-deps=true` because TypeScript 6 is ahead of `@angular/build`'s declared peer range — plain `npm ci` / `npm install` work without extra flags on dev machines and CI. Root `overrides` additionally pin `shell-quote 1.8.4`, `esbuild ^0.28.1`, `form-data`, `hono`, `tmp`, `piscina`, and `undici ^7.28.0` under `@angular/build`.

### `nx.json` highlights

- `defaultBase: "master"`; `workspaceLayout: { appsDir: "apps", libsDir: "libs" }`; `analytics: false`.
- Cached target defaults: `@angular/build:application`, `@nx/js:tsc`, `@nx/esbuild:esbuild` (all `dependsOn: ["^build"]`, `production` inputs), `@nx/eslint:lint`, and `@nx/jest:jest` (with `passWithNoTests: true` and a `ci` configuration enabling `codeCoverage`).
- Plugins: `@nx/eslint/plugin` (target `lint`), `@nx/vite/plugin` (infers `build`/`serve`/`dev`/`preview`/`typecheck`… targets from vite configs — why Vite apps have near-empty `project.json`s), `@nx/vitest` (target `test`).
- Generator defaults: Angular apps → Jest, ESLint, CSS, `e2eTestRunner: none`; React apps → Vite bundler + Babel; React libs → Vitest.
- `production` named input excludes spec files, jest configs, and test setups from build cache keys.

### Companion documentation in the repo

- `llms-full.txt` (repo root) — this file: the single-file LLM-consumable documentation of the whole monorepo.
- `docs/architecture.md` — Mermaid system-context and dependency diagrams (platform shell, views, libs, data flows).
- `docs/api/workspace-storage-api.openapi.yaml` (OpenAPI 3.1, v1.2.0) + `docs/api/workspace-storage-api.postman_collection.json` — the Workspace Storage API contract.
- `CLAUDE.md` / `AGENTS.md` — agent instructions, maintained byte-identical.
- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` + `docs/copilot/ag-grid-36-format-panel-port.md` — condensed vs. full AG Grid 36 / format-panel porting rules.
- `README.md` — human quick start; partially stale (claims "NX 22.5" and "Vite 7" against actual NX 23.0.0 / Vite 8).

---

## App: macro-angular (port 4200)

Angular 22 standalone/zoneless demo app showing real-time capital-markets UI patterns: a streaming FX grid, AG Charts market-microstructure dashboards, and a grouped Risk/PnL blotter. Serve: `npm run start:angular` (or `npx nx serve macro-angular`), dev server on port 4200. Registered in the OpenFin workspace as FDC3-enabled views (below). All market data on every page is **simulated in-component with `setInterval`** — this app has no WebSocket connection and no `@macro/transports` usage (the `market-data-server` WS endpoints are consumed by other apps).

### Bootstrap and configuration

- `apps/macro-angular/src/main.ts` — `bootstrapApplication(App, appConfig)`; bootstrap failure logged via `Logger.getLogger('MacroAngularMain').error('Bootstrap error', err)`.
- `apps/macro-angular/src/app/app.config.ts` — providers:

```typescript
provideBrowserGlobalErrorListeners(),
provideAnimationsAsync(),
provideZonelessChangeDetection(),          // zoneless; project.json has "polyfills": [] (no zone.js)
provideRouter(appRoutes),
providePrimeNG({ theme: { preset: Aura, options: { darkModeSelector: '.dark' } } })
```

- `apps/macro-angular/project.json`:
  - `build`: `@angular/build:application` → `dist/apps/macro-angular`; production budgets initial 5 MB warn / 8 MB error, anyComponentStyle 4 kb / 8 kb; assets from `apps/macro-angular/public` (favicon only).
  - `serve`: `@angular/build:dev-server`, port 4200, default configuration `development`.
  - `serve-static`: `@nx/web:file-server` on 4200 serving `dist/apps/macro-angular/browser` with `spa: true`.
  - `test`: `@nx/jest:jest`; outputs include `{workspaceRoot}/reports/{projectName}-junit.xml` (required so NX cache replays keep the merged CI JUnit report complete).
- `apps/macro-angular/src/styles.css` — imports, in order: `libs/macro-design/src/lib/css/fonts.css`, `macro-etrading.css`, `macro-design.css` (a comment in `macro-design.css` requires `macro-etrading.css` be imported BEFORE it for the full token set), then `primeicons/primeicons.css`. `body` uses `font-family: 'Ubuntu'` and `var(--background)` / `var(--foreground)` with a 1 s color transition.
- `apps/macro-angular/src/index.html` — `<title>macro</title>`, `<app-root>`, SVG favicon.

### Shell and routing

`apps/macro-angular/src/app/app.routes.ts`:

| Path | Component | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `/fx-market-data` | `FxMarketDataComponent` | default (`''` redirects here, `pathMatch: 'full'`) |
| `/treasury-microstructure` | `TreasuryMicrostructureComponent` | AG Charts dashboards |
| `/risk-pnl` | `RiskPnlComponent` | grouped blotter |

Root component `App` (`apps/macro-angular/src/app/app.ts`, selector `app-root`, standalone, imports `RouterOutlet` + PrimeNG `Menubar`):

- `menuItems = signal<MenuItem[]>([])` — three `p-menubar` items (icons `pi pi-chart-line` / `pi-chart-bar` / `pi-chart-pie`). A `Router.events` subscription filtered to `NavigationEnd` rewrites the array with **fresh item objects** setting `styleClass: 'active-menu-item'` on the active route — signal + new references are both required because the router callback schedules no CD under zoneless and the OnPush menubar needs a new reference.
- Injects the shared `ThemeService` from `@macro/macro-design/angular` (default `macro` theme; syncs system + OpenFin). `get isDark()` reads `theme.isDark()`; `toggleTheme()` delegates to `theme.toggle()`. `app.html` renders a sun/moon SVG toggle button in the menubar `#end` template (uses `@if (isDark)` control flow).
- `ngOnInit` sets `Logger.setGlobalLevel(LogLevel.DEBUG)` and emits a battery of demo `@macro/logger` calls (structured objects at info/debug/warn/error) — intentional logger showcase, not incidental noise.

### Page: FX Market Data (`/fx-market-data`)

`apps/macro-angular/src/app/fx-market-data/fx-market-data.component.ts` — streaming grid of 15 hard-coded G10 crosses via `@macro/macro-angular-grid`:

```typescript
interface CurrencyPair { id: string; symbol: string; base: string; quote: string;
  bid: number; ask: number; mid: number; spread: number; change: number; changePercent: number; }
```

- Template binds `<lib-macro-angular-grid [columns] [rowData] [getRowId] [columnFormats] [gridOptions]>`. `rowData` stays `[]`; initial rows go through `gridComponent.setInitialRowData(initialData)` in `ngAfterViewInit` to avoid duplicate binding (the wrapper warns if initial data is set twice and tells callers to use `updateRows$`).
- 10 `ColDef`s: `symbol` pinned left; `bid`/`ask`/`mid`/`spread` right-aligned with per-symbol `valueFormatter` (JPY crosses → 2 decimals, others → 5; formatters return `''` for `null`/`undefined` value and tolerate missing `data` — group rows have neither); `change`/`changePercent` with green/red `cellStyle` functions and AG Grid 36 **absolute-value sorting** (`sortingOrder: [{ direction: 'desc', type: 'absolute' }, { direction: 'asc', type: 'absolute' }, null]`); plus a pre-defined AG Grid 36 **calculated column** `{ colId: 'calcSpreadPips', calculatedExpression: '[ask] - [bid]', cellDataType: 'number' }` — users can add/edit/remove their own from the column header menu and all calc columns persist in the saved view.
- Seeded `ColumnFormatMap` (`@macro/macro-grid-format`, user-editable via the grid's "Format" tool panel):

```typescript
{ symbol: { kind: 'text', weight: 'bold' },
  bid|ask|mid: { kind: 'fxRate', symbolField: 'symbol' },   // per-row pip/JPY precision
  calcSpreadPips: { kind: 'basisPoints', decimals: 1 } }
```

- Simulation: `baseRates` + per-pair `volatility` Maps (15 entries each; JPY-cross vol quoted in big figures, e.g. `USDJPY: 0.15` vs `EURUSD: 0.0005`). Every 1000 ms (`window.setInterval`) each pair takes a random-walk step `(Math.random() - 0.5) * 2 * vol`; spread is 1–5 pips (pip = `0.01` for JPY crosses else `0.0001`); bid/ask sit symmetrically ±half-spread around mid. `change`/`changePercent` are **tick-over-tick** (the base rate map is overwritten with the new mid each tick, so change is vs the previous tick, not session open). Updates pushed with `gridComponent.updateRows$.next(updatedRows)` — a `Subject<unknown[]>` on the wrapper that applies keyed AG Grid transactions (`getRowId` returns `data.id` = symbol).
- Workspace view-state (`ViewStateService` from `@macro/openfin`): `ngAfterViewInit` awaits `viewState.restoreState()` and, if the snapshot has an `agGrid` key, calls `gridComponent.applyGridState(saved['agGrid'] as GridState)`; then registers `viewState.setCollector(() => ({ agGrid: this.gridComponent.getGridState() }))` so column order/filters/sort/formats/calc columns are captured on workspace save. `ngOnDestroy` calls `viewState.destroy()` and clears the interval.
- FDC3: `gridOptions.onRowClicked` sets `selectedSymbol = signal<string | null>(null)` (a signal because AG Grid event callbacks schedule no CD under zoneless) and broadcasts `{ type: 'fdc3.instrument', id: { ticker: pair.symbol }, name: 'BASE/QUOTE' }` via `ContextService.broadcast` (green context group; no-ops with a log when `globalThis.fdc3` is absent).
- Notifications: `ngOnInit` fires one OpenFin notification via `NotificationsService.create({ title: 'FX Market Data', body: '15 G10 currency pairs streaming', indicator: { color: 'blue', text: 'Angular' }, stream: { id: 'macro-angular', … } })` — a no-op outside the OpenFin runtime (`typeof fin === 'undefined'` guard).

### Page: Treasury Microstructure (`/treasury-microstructure`)

`apps/macro-angular/src/app/treasury-microstructure/treasury-microstructure.component.ts` — four AG Charts panels rendered with `<ag-charts>` from `ag-charts-angular` directly (no grid). Data point shape: `{ timestamp: Date; tradeCount: number; orderToTradeRatio: number; quoteUpdates: number; timeBetweenTrades: number /* ms */ }`.

- Four chart-options **signals** (`signal<AgCartesianChartOptions<MicrostructureDataPoint>>({})`): Trade Frequency (bar, fill `#4f46e5`), Order-to-Trade Ratio (line `#10b981`), Quote Update Frequency (line `#f59e0b`), Time Between Trades (line `#ef4444`). All use a `time` x-axis with label format `%H:%M:%S`, `animation.enabled: false`, legend disabled.
- Rolling 50-point window (`maxDataPoints = 50`), one synthetic point per second: 5–20 trades/s, orders at 3–8× trades (ratio derived), 20–50 quote updates/s, 50–250 ms between trades, ±15% noise (a 0.3 volatility factor applied as `(Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.3`, i.e. a 0.85×–1.15× multiplier), floors at 0 (counts) and 10 ms.
- Update pattern (zoneless + AG Charts immutability): `options.update((o) => { const next = clone(o); next.data = chartData; return next; })` using the `clone` npm package — AG Charts wants a fresh options object per update and the signal write is what schedules the render.
- Theming here does **not** use the shared `ThemeService`: it reads the `.dark` class on `document.documentElement` (or `localStorage.theme === 'dark'`) and installs a `MutationObserver` on the root element's `class` attribute, swapping the AG Charts theme string `'ag-default'` ⇄ `'ag-default-dark'` across all four option signals. `isPlatformBrowser(PLATFORM_ID)` guards + `DOCUMENT` injection; observer disconnected in `ngOnDestroy`.
- No view-state, FDC3, or notifications on this page.

### Page: Risk / PnL (`/risk-pnl`)

`apps/macro-angular/src/app/risk-pnl/risk-pnl.component.ts` — positions grouped Desk → Book → Trader with summed aggregations and AG Grid 36 Enterprise "Show Values As" columns, all persisted via the workspace ViewState snapshot.

```typescript
interface RiskPosition { id: string; desk: string; book: string; trader: string;
  instrument: string; pnl: number; dayPnl: number; dv01: number; notional: number; }
```

- Fabricated book: `STRUCTURE` constant of 4 desks (Rates / FX / Credit / Commodities) → 2–3 books each → 1–2 traders, 2–4 positions per trader from a per-desk instrument list; ids are `desk|book|trader|instrument|i`.
- Columns: `desk`/`book`/`trader` as hidden `rowGroup` columns; `instrument`; `pnl`/`dayPnl`/`dv01`/`notional` with `aggFunc: 'sum'`, `enableValue: true`, `type: 'numericColumn'`; plus two Show Values As columns: `{ colId: 'pnlPctGrand', field: 'pnl', showValuesAs: 'percentOfGrandTotal', enableShowValuesAs: true }` and `{ colId: 'notionalPctParent', field: 'notional', showValuesAs: 'percentOfParentRowTotal', … }` (users can change the mode from the column menu).
- `gridOptions`: `rowGroupPanelShow: 'always'`, `groupDefaultExpanded: 1`, `grandTotalRow: 'bottom'`, `suppressAggFuncInHeader: true`, `autoGroupColumnDef` = "Desk / Book / Trader", pinned left, `minWidth: 260`.
- Seeded formats (absolute metrics only — % columns are left to Show Values As): `pnl`/`dayPnl` → `{ kind: 'currency', currency: 'USD', decimals: 0, negativeStyle: 'parentheses', colorMode: 'posneg' }`, `dv01` → USD 0 dp, `notional` → `{ kind: 'compact', notation: 'mmbn', decimals: 1, prefix: '$' }`.
- Live ticks: every 2500 ms roughly 18% of positions get `dayPnl`/`pnl` nudged by `rand(-1,1) * max(2000, |notional| * 0.0001)`; cloned rows are pushed through `updateRows$` and the grid re-aggregates group totals and % contributions in place.
- Same ViewState pattern as the FX page (restore + collector under the `agGrid` key; grouping, sort, Show Values As selections and formats persist with the workspace snapshot). No FDC3/notifications.

### OpenFin integration

View manifests in `apps/macro-workspace/public/{local,openshift}/` — one for the app root and one per route, all `{ "fdc3InteropApi": "2.0", "interop": { "currentContextGroup": "green" } }`:

| Manifest | Local URL |
| --- | --- |
| `macro-angular-view.fin.json` | `http://localhost:4200` |
| `macro-angular-fx-market-data.fin.json` | `http://localhost:4200/fx-market-data` |
| `macro-angular-treasury-microstructure.fin.json` | `http://localhost:4200/treasury-microstructure` |
| `macro-angular-risk-pnl.fin.json` | `http://localhost:4200/risk-pnl` |

`ViewStateService` persists snapshots into `fin.me.updateOptions({ customData: { viewState } })` and logs "fin.me not available — running outside OpenFin, skipping restore" in a plain browser; `NotificationsService` and `ContextService` likewise guard on `typeof fin === 'undefined'` / missing `globalThis.fdc3`, so the app runs standalone at `http://localhost:4200` with those features silently disabled.

### Tests

Jest 30 via `jest-preset-angular` (`apps/macro-angular/jest.config.ts`); `apps/macro-angular/src/test-setup.ts` calls `setupZonelessTestEnv({ errorOnUnknownElements: true, errorOnUnknownProperties: true })` and stubs `window.matchMedia` (JSDOM lacks it; theme detection needs it). No `fakeAsync` anywhere (zoneless). Run: `npx nx test macro-angular`.

- `fx-market-data.component.spec.ts` (~820 lines) — `jest.mock`s `@macro/logger`, `@macro/openfin` (avoids pulling `@openfin/workspace` ESM), `@macro/macro-angular-grid`, and `@macro/macro-design`; covers all 10 column defs (incl. the calculated column and absolute `sortingOrder` styles), JPY vs non-JPY formatter precision, `calculateBidAsk` symmetry, `round`, `generateInitialData` invariants (15 pairs, bid < mid < ask, decimal caps), `updateMarketData` via an injected fake grid with a spied `updateRows$`, and `ngOnDestroy` cleanup. Uses `jest.useFakeTimers()`.
- `risk-pnl.component.spec.ts` — grouping columns, pinned auto-group column, sum aggregations, both Show Values As columns, seeded formats, unique row ids, tick pushes through `updateRows$`, destroy cleanup.
- `treasury-microstructure.component.spec.ts` — theme detection (`.dark` class / localStorage), all four chart option signals (series types, disabled animation/legend, titles), `generateDataPoint` bounds/rounding, 50-point initial window.
- `app.spec.ts` — menu items, logger level setup, `toggleTheme` delegation to a mocked `ThemeService`; `app.config.spec.ts` + `app.routes.spec.ts` — provider count and the 4-route table incl. the default redirect.

---

## App: macro-react (port 4201)

React 19 + Vite 8 sample market-data application. Two pages: an AG Grid Enterprise Treasury blotter (via `@macro/macro-react-grid`) and a Recharts commodities dashboard. Demonstrates the React side of the shared platform: Shadcn/Radix UI, Tailwind CSS 4, PrimeReact 11 (alpha) provider config, the `@macro/macro-design` React theme adapter, `@macro/openfin/react` hooks, and `@macro/logger`. It generates all market data locally with in-component simulators — it does **not** connect to `market-data-server` and does **not** use any `@macro/transports` hook (no `useNatsTransport`/`useAmpsTransport`/`useSolaceTransport` usage anywhere in `apps/macro-react/src`).

Commands: `npm run start:react` (= `nx serve macro-react`), `npm run build:react` (= `nx build macro-react`), `npx nx test macro-react` (Vitest). Dev URL: `http://localhost:4201/macro-react/` (note the base path).

### Vite configuration — `apps/macro-react/vite.config.ts`

- `base: '/macro-react/'` — all assets and the SPA are served under this prefix; matches the router basename.
- `server` / `preview`: `port: 4201`, `host: 'localhost'`.
- Plugins: `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `nxViteTsPaths()`, `nxCopyAssetsPlugin(['*.md'])`.
- `resolve.alias`: `@` → `./src` (Shadcn convention), plus explicit source aliases for `@macro/logger`, `@macro/macro-react-grid`, `@macro/macro-design` + `/react`, `@macro/openfin` + `/react` + `/theme-sync` — the shared libs are consumed from source, not built artifacts.
- `build.outDir: '../../dist/apps/macro-react'`, `commonjsOptions.transformMixedEsModules: true`.
- Embedded Vitest config: `environment: 'jsdom'`, `globals: true`, JUnit reporter → `../../reports/macro-react-junit.xml`, v8 coverage → `../../coverage/apps/macro-react`. `apps/macro-react/project.json` declares both as NX `test` outputs (required so cache replays keep the merged CI reports complete).

`apps/macro-react/index.html`: `<base href="/" />`, `<div id="root">`, loads `/src/styles.css` and `/src/main.tsx`.

### Entry point — `apps/macro-react/src/main.tsx`

```tsx
import { PrimeReactProvider } from '@primereact/core/config'; // primereact 11 alpha split-package layout
import Aura from '@primeuix/themes/aura';
import { themeController } from '@macro/macro-design/react';

themeController.start(); // applies the macro theme + .dark/.light class BEFORE first paint

root.render(
  <PrimeReactProvider theme={{ preset: Aura, options: { darkModeSelector: '.dark' } }}>
    <App />
  </PrimeReactProvider>
);
```

`primereact` is pinned at `^11.0.0-alpha.10`; the provider comes from the transitive `@primereact/core` package (v11 package split). `darkModeSelector: '.dark'` aligns PrimeReact dark styling with the class-based dark mode that `themeController` toggles on `<html>`.

### Router & shell — `apps/macro-react/src/app/app.tsx`

- Exports both `App` (wraps `<BrowserRouter basename="/macro-react">`) and `AppContent` (routes + chrome; tests render `AppContent` inside `MemoryRouter`).
- Routes (React Router DOM 7):
  - `/treasury-market-data` → `TreasuryMarketDataComponent`
  - `/commodities-dashboard` → `CommoditiesDashboardComponent`
  - `/` → `<Navigate to="/treasury-market-data" replace />`
- Navigation is a Shadcn `Menubar`/`MenubarMenu`/`MenubarTrigger` bar; the trigger for the active route gets an extra `bg-accent` class keyed off `useLocation().pathname`.
- Theme toggle button uses `useTheme()` from `@macro/macro-design/react` (`useSyncExternalStore` wrapper over the shared `themeController`): reads `isDark`, calls `toggle()`; renders a sun/moon inline SVG + "Light"/"Dark" label. All dark-mode application and OS/OpenFin listeners live in the controller, not the component.
- `App`'s mount effect is a `@macro/logger` showcase: `Logger.setGlobalLevel(LogLevel.DEBUG)`, per-logger `setLevel`, and sample `info`/`error`/`warn`/`debug` calls with structured payloads.

### Page: Treasury Market Data — `src/app/treasury-market-data/treasury-market-data.component.tsx`

Simulated on-the-run UST blotter: 11 hardcoded securities (3 T-Bills, 6 T-Notes, 2 T-Bonds) with per-CUSIP `basePrice`/`baseYield`/`volatility`, random-walked every 1 s by a `setInterval`.

**Grid ref pattern** (`@macro/macro-react-grid`):

```tsx
const gridRef = useRef<MacroReactGridRef>(null);
<MacroReactGrid ref={gridRef} columns={columns} rowData={rowData}
                getRowId={getRowId} columnFormats={INITIAL_COLUMN_FORMATS} />
```

`MacroReactGridRef` (from the lib) exposes `applyTransaction`, `getGridApi()`, `getGridState()`, `applyGridState(state)`, and RxJS `Subject<unknown[]>` streams `addRows$` / `updateRows$` / `deleteRows$`. The component pushes the initial 11 rows via `gridRef.current.addRows$.next(rowData)` and every tick pushes full recomputed rows via `gridRef.current.updateRows$.next(updatedRows)`; `getRowId` returns `params.data.id` (= CUSIP) so updates patch in place.

**Column formats** are seeded declaratively through the shared `@macro/macro-grid-format` engine (`ColumnFormatMap` prop, user-editable afterwards in the grid's "Format" tool panel; saved view state overrides on restore):

```ts
const INITIAL_COLUMN_FORMATS: ColumnFormatMap = {
  cusip: { kind: 'text', weight: 'bold' },
  price: { kind: 'treasury', fraction: 32, plusTick: true },
  bid: { kind: 'treasury', fraction: 32, plusTick: true },
  ask: { kind: 'treasury', fraction: 32, plusTick: true },
  change: { kind: 'number', decimals: 4, signDisplay: 'always', colorMode: 'posneg' },
};
```

**Column defs** are a `useMemo` array (deliberately not a JSON string — a code comment notes arrays preserve function references for `valueFormatter`/`cellStyle`). Notable columns: `price`/`bid`/`ask` formatted via `formatTreasury32nd`; `change`/`changePercent` with sign-colored `cellStyle` and AG Grid 36 **absolute** `sortingOrder` (`{ direction: 'desc', type: 'absolute' }` etc.); a predefined AG Grid 36 calculated column `{ colId: 'calcMid', calculatedExpression: '([bid] + [ask]) / 2', cellDataType: 'number' }`. Most value formatters null-guard `params.value` (group rows have no value unless aggregated — a documented repo pitfall); exception: the `price`/`bid`/`ask` formatters pass `params.value` straight into `formatTreasury32nd`, which has no null guard (an undefined value renders as `NaN-NaN`).

**OpenFin hooks** (`@macro/openfin/react`):

- `const [viewState, savedState, isRestored] = useViewState()` — returns a stable `ViewStateService`, the restored `ViewStateData`, and a readiness flag. One effect applies `savedState['agGrid']` via `gridRef.current.applyGridState(...)` once `isRestored` is true; another registers auto-save with `viewState.enableAutoSave(() => ({ agGrid: gridRef.current?.getGridState() }))` (5-second cadence in the service) and calls `disableAutoSave()` on unmount.
- `useNotifications()` — returns a stable `NotificationsService`; on mount the page fires `notifications.create({ title: 'Treasury Market Data', body: '11 securities streaming', indicator: { color: 'blue', text: 'React' }, stream: { id: 'macro-react', ... } } as any)` (the `as any` cast covers indicator/stream fields beyond the typed surface). Registration itself happens in the workspace shell; app views only `create()`. Outside OpenFin these services no-op gracefully.

**Math helpers** — `src/app/treasury-market-data/treasury-utils.ts` exports pure functions `formatTreasury32nd(decimalPrice, useEighths = false)` (handle-dash-32nds, e.g. `99-16`, `+` suffix for a half-32nd at ≥ 0.4 threshold, optional eighths digit), `getYearsToMaturity(maturityDate)`, `calculateYield(price, coupon, yearsToMaturity)` (discount-yield formula under 0.5y, else current-yield + straight-line capital gain approximation), `calculateBidAsk(mid, spread)`, `calculateDuration(...)` (closed-form Macaulay-style approximation; returns `n` for zero-coupon), `calculateConvexity(...)`, `round(value, decimals)`.

### Page: Commodities Dashboard — `src/app/commodities-dashboard/commodities-dashboard.component.tsx`

Self-contained simulated futures dashboard for 7 commodities (CL, NG, GC, SI, HG, ZC, ZS) across Energy / Metals / Agriculture. All state is React `useState`/`useRef` — no grid, no OpenFin hooks.

- **Simulation**: `setInterval` at `1000 / playbackSpeed` ms; per tick each commodity random-walks (0.1% volatility × speed, floored at 0.01), recomputes bid/ask (0.01% spread), change vs. initial price, volume/open-interest drift, and a futures-curve state (`frontMonth`/`backMonth` → `curveType: 'contango' | 'backwardation'`).
- **Controls**: Shadcn `Switch` + `Label` LIVE/PAUSED toggle (with a pulsing dot), a plain `<select>` for playback speed (0.5x–4x), and per-category symbol buttons that set both `selectedCommodity` and `selectedCategory`.
- **Recharts** (v3): imports `LineChart/Line/BarChart/Bar/XAxis/YAxis/CartesianGrid/Tooltip/ResponsiveContainer/AreaChart/Area`, but renders only an `AreaChart` — price history (rolling 100 `PricePoint`s `{time, price, volume}`) as a gradient-filled monotone `Area` with `isAnimationActive={false}`, `domain={['dataMin - 1', 'dataMax + 1']}`, and a custom dark-styled tooltip component.
- **Order book**: 20 synthetic levels (10 bid below / 10 ask above at 0.01 increments); levels within 0.05 of the live price get their sizes re-randomized each tick, and the level nearest the current price is highlighted.
- **Live statistics** panel: total volume, average spread, cross-commodity volatility (stddev/mean of prices), 24h high/low — recomputed from the previous render's `commodityData` snapshot each tick.
- Styling is Tailwind-heavy with explicit `dark:` variants (e.g. `bg-gray-50 dark:bg-gray-900`) rather than the token classes used by the shell.

### Shadcn/Radix components & utilities

- `components.json`: Shadcn config — style `new-york`, `rsc: false`, Tailwind css `src/styles.css`, base color `neutral`, CSS variables on, aliases `@/components`, `@/components/ui`, `@/lib`, `@/lib/utils`, `@/hooks`, icon library `lucide`.
- `src/components/ui/menubar.tsx` — full Shadcn menubar over `@radix-ui/react-menubar` (exports `Menubar`, `MenubarMenu`, `MenubarTrigger`, `MenubarContent`, `MenubarItem`, `MenubarCheckboxItem`, `MenubarRadioGroup/Item`, `MenubarSub*`, `MenubarSeparator`, `MenubarLabel`, `MenubarShortcut`, …), all `React.forwardRef` with `cn()`-merged classes and lucide `Check`/`ChevronRight`/`Circle` icons.
- `src/components/ui/switch.tsx` — Shadcn `Switch` over `@radix-ui/react-switch`.
- `src/components/ui/label.tsx` — Shadcn `Label` over `@radix-ui/react-label` with a `cva` variant base.
- `src/lib/utils.ts` — `cn(...inputs: ClassValue[])` = `twMerge(clsx(inputs))`.

### Styling & theming — `src/styles.css`, `tailwind.config.js`

Import order in `src/styles.css` (shared tokens first, framework CSS after): `@macro/macro-design` `fonts.css` → `macro-etrading.css` → `macro-design.css` (relative paths into `libs/macro-design/src/lib/css/`), then `primeicons.css`, then Tailwind 4 (`@import "tailwindcss"; @import "tw-animate-css";`).

- Dark mode: `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark *))` — class-based, driven by the shared `themeController` toggling `.dark` on the root.
- An `@theme inline` block maps every Shadcn semantic token (`--color-background`, `--color-primary`, `--color-chart-1..5`, `--color-sidebar-*`, `--radius-*`) onto the `@macro/macro-design` CSS variables, so Tailwind utility classes (`bg-background`, `text-secondary-foreground`, `border-border`, …) resolve to the shared design system.
- `@layer base` applies `border-border outline-ring/50` to `*` and `bg-background text-foreground` + `'Noto Sans'` + a 2 s background/color transition to `body`.
- `tailwind.config.js` (legacy-format config kept alongside Tailwind 4): `darkMode: ['class']`, `content: ['./src/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}']`, and a `theme.extend.colors`/`borderRadius` block re-mapping the same semantic names to `var(--color-*)` / `var(--radius-*)`.

### OpenFin registration

Three per-env view manifests in `apps/macro-workspace/public/{local,openshift}/` point at this app's routes: `macro-react-view.fin.json` → `http://localhost:4201/macro-react/`, `macro-react-treasury-market-data.fin.json` → `/macro-react/treasury-market-data`, `macro-react-commodities-dashboard.fin.json` → `/macro-react/commodities-dashboard` (all with `fdc3InteropApi: "2.0"`, context group `green`). Matching `apps.json` registry entries: appIds `macro-react-view` ("Macro React App"), `macro-react-treasury-market-data` ("Treasury Market Data"), `macro-react-commodities-dashboard` ("Commodities Dashboard"), all `category: "Showcase"` with tags like `react`, `treasury`, `commodities`, `market-data`.

### Tests (Vitest 4, jsdom, ~1,800 lines total)

- `src/app/app.spec.tsx` (333 lines) — renders `AppContent` in `MemoryRouter` and full `App` under `BrowserRouter` (after `window.history.pushState({}, '', '/macro-react/')` so the basename matches). Mocks `@macro/logger`, `@macro/macro-design` + `/react` (controllable `themeState.isDark`, spied `toggle`), `@macro/macro-react-grid` (stub div), `@macro/openfin/react`, `ag-grid-community`, and `recharts`; stubs `window.matchMedia`. Asserts nav highlighting (`bg-accent` as a standalone class token, distinguishing it from `focus:bg-accent`), route rendering, `/` redirect, theme-button label per mode, toggle-on-click only (no toggle on initial render), and logger initialization on mount.
- `src/app/treasury-market-data/treasury-market-data.component.spec.tsx` (293 lines) and `treasury-utils.spec.ts` (308 lines — per-function suites for `formatTreasury32nd`, `getYearsToMaturity`, `calculateYield`, `calculateBidAsk`, `calculateDuration`, `calculateConvexity`, `round`).
- `src/app/commodities-dashboard/commodities-dashboard.component.spec.tsx` (378 lines), plus Shadcn component suites `menubar.spec.tsx` (301), `switch.spec.tsx` (85), `label.spec.tsx` (56) and `lib/utils.spec.ts` (62, `cn` merging).

---

## App: macro-workspace — OpenFin platform shell (port 4202)

Angular 22 zoneless application that hosts the OpenFin Workspace **platform provider window** plus a few auxiliary views served from the same origin. It is the app that OpenFin boots first: the platform manifest's `providerUrl` (e.g. `http://localhost:4202/provider?env=local` in `apps/macro-workspace/public/local/manifest.fin.json`) loads this app's `/provider` route, and that route drives the entire Workspace platform initialization (Dock, Home, Store, Notifications, Snap) through `@macro/openfin`. All per-environment OpenFin config lives under `apps/macro-workspace/public/{local,openshift}/` (covered in its own section); this section covers `apps/macro-workspace/src`.

- Serve: `npm run start:workspace` → `nx serve macro-workspace` (dev-server port **4202**, `@angular/build:dev-server`).
- Launch into OpenFin: `npm run launch` → `node apps/macro-workspace/launch.mjs http://localhost:4202/local/manifest.fin.json`.
- Build: `@angular/build:application`, `polyfills: []` (zoneless — no zone.js), assets = entire `apps/macro-workspace/public` folder, global styles = `src/styles.css` + `src/openfin.css`, budgets 3mb/5mb initial. Extra targets in `apps/macro-workspace/project.json`: `client` (runs `launch.mjs` — note it still points at the pre-env-split URL `http://localhost:4202/manifest.fin.json`) and `serve-static` (file-server over `dist/apps/macro-workspace/browser`, SPA mode, port 4202).

### Bootstrap flow

- `apps/macro-workspace/src/main.ts` — `bootstrapApplication(App, appConfig).catch(console.error)`.
- `apps/macro-workspace/src/app/app.config.ts` — exactly three providers: `provideZonelessChangeDetection()`, `provideRouter(routes)`, `provideHttpClient(withXhr())`.
- `apps/macro-workspace/src/app/app.ts` — root `App` component (`app-root`), inline template `<router-outlet />`, host style `display: contents`.
- `apps/macro-workspace/src/app/app.routes.ts` — 6 routes, all `loadComponent` lazy:

| Path | Component | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `provider` | `ProviderComponent` | Platform provider window (the shell) |
| `view1` | `View1Component` | Demo view: FDC3 broadcast + notifications |
| `view2` | `View2Component` | Demo view: FDC3/app-channel listener + IAB channel provider |
| `analytics` | `AnalyticsDashboardComponent` | Live analytics feed over NATS |
| `''` (full) / `**` | — | both redirect to `provider` |

`view1`/`view2`/`analytics` are opened as OpenFin views via the per-env manifests `view1.fin.json`, `view2.fin.json`, `analytics-dashboard.fin.json`.

Global ambient types: `src/types/fin.d.ts` declares `const fin: typeof fin` from `@openfin/core`; `src/types/fdc3.d.ts` declares `const fdc3: DesktopAgent` from `@finos/fdc3`. `tsconfig.app.json` sets `resolveJsonModule: true` (the provider imports `@openfin/workspace/package.json` to display the Workspace version) and includes `libs/openfin/src/openfin-globals.d.ts`.

Styling note: unlike the market-data apps, this shell does **not** import `@macro/macro-design` CSS. `src/styles.css` (82 lines) carries its own `:root` + `.dark` blocks of OpenFin theme variables (`--brand-primary`, `--background-1..6`, `--text-default`, `--status-*` …) whose values `ThemeService` overwrites at runtime from the active platform palette; `src/openfin.css` (929 lines) is the OpenFin-style chrome CSS keyed off `--theme-*` variables with a `.theme-light` override class.

### Provider component (`src/app/provider/provider.component.ts|html|css`)

`ProviderComponent` (`app-provider`, OnPush, standalone) injects the Angular wrappers `WorkspaceService`, `ThemeService`, `ThemePresetService`, `NotificationsService`, `SettingsService` from `@macro/openfin` (the Angular `@Injectable` subclasses in `libs/openfin/src/lib/angular/index.ts` are the lib's default exports; framework-agnostic classes are exported with a `Base` prefix).

`ngOnInit()` order:
1. `themeService.syncWithOpenFinTheme()` — starts OpenFin theme → CSS variable sync (stopped in `ngOnDestroy`).
2. `workspaceService.init().subscribe()` — kicks off the whole platform boot (pipeline below).
3. Subscribes to `workspaceService.getStatus$()` and on every status emission re-reads `getActiveStorageEnvironment()` and `themePresetService.getActivePresetId()` into signals — both resolve asynchronously during init (in REST storage mode the persisted preset arrives via hydration after the component is constructed), so the panel refreshes on each status transition.
4. `settingsService.getManifestSettings()` → `listStorageEnvironments(settings.storage)` populates the storage-environment picker; on failure logs a warning and keeps the default `[LOCAL_STORAGE_ENVIRONMENT]`.
5. If `typeof fin !== 'undefined'`: positions the window at the top-right of the primary monitor (`moveTo(primary.right - 292, primary.top + 12)`), reads `fin.System.getVersion()` into `runtimeVersion`, sets `platformVersion` from the installed `@openfin/workspace` package.json version (single source of truth — cannot drift from the bundled version), and sets `closeOnLastViewRemoved: false` on the window.

Provider window UI (`provider.component.html`):
- **Title bar** with expand/collapse chevron and minimize button (`fin.Window.getCurrentSync().minimize()`).
- **Status row**: green dot when the latest status string `includes('initialized')`; text bound to `message$ | async` (= `WorkspaceService.getStatus$()`).
- **Info rows**: OpenFin runtime version + Workspace package version.
- **Action buttons**: `launchAnalytics()` (creates a view from `${origin}/${env}/analytics-dashboard.fin.json`, `env` from the `?env=` query param, default `local`); `launchProcessManager()` (`fin.Application.startFromManifest('http://cdn.openfin.co/release/apps/openfin/processmanager/app.json')`); `uploadLogs()` (`fin.System.launchLogUploader` to `http://MontyUbuntu2604:8000` with `logs: ['debug:self','app','rvm']`, success/failure toast); `sendLogs()` (`app.sendApplicationLog()` — deliberately treats a thrown error as success with an info toast, because the API may throw even when logs were delivered, e.g. non-JSON server response).
- **Expandable settings panel** (`toggleExpand()` resizes the window: collapsed height 280px, expanded = measured `.provider` scrollHeight clamped to ≤600px, measured after a 50 ms `setTimeout` so Angular renders first):
  - **Theme preset pills** from `ThemePresetService.getAvailablePresets()`. `applyPreset()` **awaits** `setActivePresetId()` before restarting — in REST storage mode a fire-and-forget save could be killed by the restart before reaching the storage service; on write failure it does NOT restart (the platform would come back on the old preset) and shows an error notification. On success: `fin.Application.getCurrentSync().restart()`.
  - **Storage environment pills** (see below).
  - **Test notification pills**: levels `info|success|warning|error|critical` plus Notification Center 2.15 toast modes — `sticky` (stays until interacted with), `transient` (default, fades), `none` (Notification Center only) — passed as `{ toast }` to `NotificationsService[level](title, body, opts)`.
- **Quit flow**: `requestQuit()` → inline confirmation ("This will close all workspace windows and views.") → `confirmQuit()` calls `WorkspaceService.quit()`, which shuts down Dock3, deregisters notifications, stops Snap, publishes `Lifecycle/Quit` analytics, disconnects NATS, then `fin.Platform.getCurrentSync().quit()`.

### Storage-environment picker

Pills for each environment from `settings.json`'s `storage` block (always includes `LOCAL_STORAGE_ENVIRONMENT`, name `local`, `mode: 'localStorage'`). Pill tooltip shows the REST `baseUrl` or "Browser localStorage on this machine"; a hint line below shows the active backend.

- `storageEnvPinned` (computed): true only when `?storageEnv=` (`STORAGE_ENV_QUERY_PARAM`) is present **and its value actually won environment resolution** (`activeStorageEnv().name === queryValue`). An unknown/invalid query value is skipped with a fallback during resolution, and the picker must stay usable then — this exact edge is unit-tested. When pinned, pills are disabled and a "(pinned by ?storageEnv=)" hint renders.
- `applyStorageEnvironment(env)`: no-op when pinned or re-picking the active env. If the picked env equals `settings.storage.defaultEnvironment`, calls `saveStorageEnvironmentChoice(undefined)` to **clear** the machine-local override (localStorage key `macro:storage-env`) so `settings.json` stays in charge; otherwise persists the name. Then restarts the platform (`fin.Application.getCurrentSync().restart()`) — or `window.location.reload()` in a plain browser — so the whole platform re-reads workspaces/pages/dock/favorites/preferences/config from the new backend.

### Platform initialization pipeline (`WorkspaceService.init()`)

The provider only calls `init()`; the sequencing lives in `libs/openfin/src/lib/workspace.service.ts`. The Angular `WorkspaceService` (in `libs/openfin/src/lib/angular/index.ts`) wires the constructor graph via `inject()`: `PlatformService` (← `WorkspaceOverrideService` ← storage + snap, ← `LaunchService` ← `EntitlementsService` + `NotificationsService`), `DockService`, `Dock3Service` (← launch, apps, dock-config, storage — LOB dock apps), `HomeService`, `StoreService` (← apps, favorites, storefront-config, entitlements, launch, storage, dock3), `SettingsService`, `WorkspaceStorageService`, `ThemePresetService`, `NotificationsService`, `SnapService`, `AuthService`.

Not in OpenFin (`typeof fin === 'undefined'` or `!fin.me?.isOpenFin`): emits status `'Not running inside OpenFin'`, still resolves the storage environment (`initStorageContext()`, falling back to an undefined-settings init) so storage-backed features behave consistently in a plain browser, re-emits the status afterwards (the provider UI refreshes its storage display on status emissions), and returns `of(false)`.

Inside OpenFin, the RxJS pipeline (each step gated by `concatMap`):

1. `initStorageContext()` — `SettingsService.getManifestSettings()` (loads per-env `settings.json`), then `initWorkspaceStorage(settings.storage, { getUserId })`. Environment precedence: `?storageEnv=` → saved choice → `defaultEnvironment` → `local`. `getUserId` = `AuthService.getUser().id`, `'anonymous'` when auth is absent or throws.
2. `Promise.all([themePresetService.hydrate(), hydrateViewTitles()])` — storage-backed hydrations.
3. `themePresetService.loadActivePreset()` — resolves the theme palettes for platform init.
4. `platformService.initializeWorkspacePlatform(platformSettings, themePalettes, storeService.getStoreCustomActions(), browserSettings)`.
5. `awaitPlatformReady()` — listens for the one-shot `platform-api-ready` event but ALSO falls back to a 1000 ms timeout: the event may already have fired before subscription (WS 23.2.x resolves init() after it fires), and a missed event would otherwise silently keep Dock/Store/Home from ever registering.
6. `registerComponents()` — `forkJoin` of five **independent** registrations, each wrapped in `guarded(label, obs, ms = 12000)`: `dock` (`dock3Service.init`), `home`, `store`, `notifications`, `snap`. `guarded()` applies `timeout({ first: ms })` + `catchError` → `of(undefined)`, logging which component stalled — in WS 23.2.x several component promises resolve their UI but never settle, and without the guard the provider would sit on "Registering workspace components..." forever.
7. `showStartupComponents()` — fires `homeService.show()` and `storeService.show()` **without awaiting** (guards self-complete): `Storefront.show()`'s promise may never settle; this is a UX nicety, not a readiness gate. Dock3 auto-shows on init.
8. `restoreLastSavedWorkspace()` — deliberately does NOT use the platform's built-in `restoreLastSavedWorkspace()`, which restores the *currently active* workspace and on a fresh launch always returns `'not-saved-workspace'`. Instead reads a self-tracked last-saved id from `WorkspaceStorageService`, fetches that workspace, and calls `applyWorkspace(workspace, { skipPrompt: true, applySnapshotOptions: { closeExistingWindows: true } })`. A dangling id is cleared (`removeLastSavedWorkspaceId()`); all failures degrade to a status message, never abort init.
9. `delay(500)` → `platformService.updateToolbarButtons()` → status `'Platform initialized'` → `setAppLogUsername()` (reads `USERNAME` env var via `fin.System.getEnvironmentVariable`, retries `app.setAppLogUsername()` up to 5× with 2 s delay, skips when empty).

Any pipeline error is caught once at the end: logged, status set to `Error: <message>`, analytics `Lifecycle/Error` published, `of(false)` returned — the provider stays up showing the error. Status strings observed by the UI, in order: `Workspace platform initializing...` → `Platform API ready...` → `Registering workspace components...` → `Restoring last saved workspace...` → (`Restored last saved workspace` | `No saved workspace found` | `Error restoring workspace`) → `Platform initialized`.

### Entitlements / auth wiring

The shell contains no auth UI. Identity is config-driven: the Angular `AuthService` (`libs/openfin/src/lib/angular/index.ts`) loads `entitlements.json` from the per-env static folder via `resolveEnvConfigPath('entitlements.json')` (`?env=openshift` → `/openshift/…`, else `/local/…`; `libs/openfin/src/lib/config-path.ts`). `AuthService.getUser()` returns `entitlements.json#currentUser`, caching the config after one fetch; on load failure it logs and returns `{ id: 'anonymous', name: 'Anonymous', entitlements: [] }` — a pluggable seam for a real OIDC provider. The user id feeds user-scoped storage (`X-User-Id`) and `EntitlementsService` (injected into `LaunchService` and `StoreService`) gates app launches/visibility. `entitlements.json` and `settings.json` are bootstrap-tier and always load from static files, never through the storage API; the other config services (`AppsService`, `DockConfigService`, `StorefrontConfigService`, `SnapConfigService`) resolve through `resolveConfigUrl()` (storage-service `/config/*` in REST mode) with an automatic static-file fallback (`configHttpWithStaticFallback`) so a storage outage degrades to baseline config instead of an empty store/dock.

### Analytics

Two halves:
- **Publisher** (`libs/openfin/src/lib/analytics-nats.service.ts`, singleton `getAnalyticsNats()`): publishes platform lifecycle events over `NatsTransport` to `ws://MontyUbuntu2604:8224`, topic `macro.analytics.<user>.<source>.<type>.<action>` (user from the `USERNAME` env var). `WorkspaceService.init()` emits `Lifecycle` events `Starting`, `SettingsLoaded`, `PlatformCreated`, `PlatformReady`, `ComponentsRegistered`, `Initialized`, `Error`, plus `Workspace` events (`Restored`/`NoSavedWorkspace`/`RestoreError`) and `Quitting`/`Quit`. Every publish is `.catch(() => {})` — analytics can never break boot.
- **Consumer** — `src/app/analytics-dashboard/analytics-dashboard.component.ts` (route `/analytics`, launched from the provider's Analytics button or the dock's "Macro Tools" menu). Subscribes `NatsTransportService` (via the `TransportClient` interface) to `macro.analytics.>` on the same NATS URL. Signals-based: rolling buffer of the latest **200** events (`MAX_EVENTS`), per-user filter chips, per-source counts with fixed colors (`platform`/`browser`/`dock`/`home`/`store`), events-per-minute rate, pause toggle (drops messages while paused), click-to-expand JSON detail row. Connection failure just logs — the view shows "Connecting to NATS...".

### Demo views

- **View1** (`src/app/view1/view1.component.ts`): buttons to (a) create a rich OpenFin notification (`toast: 'transient'`, indicator, stream, CTA button with `customData`) — guarded by `typeof fin !== 'undefined'` with a warning otherwise; (b) `ContextService.broadcast()` an `fdc3.instrument` (MSFT) on the user channel; (c) `ChannelService.broadcast('CUSTOM-APP-CHANNEL', …)` (AAPL). Also subscribes `NotificationsService.observeNotificationActions()` and logs clicked notifications' `customData`.
- **View2** (`src/app/view2/view2.component.ts`): registers an `fdc3.instrument` context listener (`ContextService.registerContextListener` + `context$`) and an app-channel listener (`ChannelService.registerChannelListener('CUSTOM-APP-CHANNEL', 'fdc3.instrument')` + `channel$`); renders the last received context as pretty-printed JSON in a signal. Additionally creates a raw InterApplicationBus channel provider named `'channelName'` (`fin.InterApplicationBus.Channel.create`), logs client connections and `'example-topic'` dispatches, and republishes any app-channel context to `'example-topic'`. Channel creation no-ops outside OpenFin and failures are logged as warnings. Listeners are removed in `ngOnDestroy`.

Both views follow the repo-wide browser posture: every `fin.*` call is behind `typeof fin === 'undefined'` guards, and `@macro/openfin` services no-op gracefully outside the runtime, so all routes render in a plain browser.

### Launch script (`apps/macro-workspace/launch.mjs`)

Node ESM script. On macOS it converts the manifest URL to a `fins:` link and `open`s it (RVM handles the rest; quitting is manual via Process Manager / browser Quit menu). On Windows/Linux it uses `@openfin/node-adapter`: `launch({ manifestUrl })` → connect a `nonPersistent` dev connection over `ws://127.0.0.1:<port>`, wraps the platform UUID from the fetched manifest, wires `SIGINT`/`exit` to `platform.quit()`, and exits the Node process when the runtime disconnects. Calls `dns.setDefaultResultOrder('ipv4first')` to avoid interface-ordering issues.

### Tests

Jest 30 + `jest-preset-angular`; `src/test-setup.ts` uses `setupZonelessTestEnv` from `jest-preset-angular/setup-env/zoneless` (with `errorOnUnknownElements`/`errorOnUnknownProperties`) so tests run zoneless like production and avoid the NG0914 Zone.js warning. `jest.config.ts` is the standard Angular preset (html/svg/css stringified). The test target declares `{workspaceRoot}/reports/{projectName}-junit.xml` as an NX output (required for the merged CI JUnit report).

Spec inventory: `app.spec.ts` (4 — root renders `router-outlet`), `app.config.spec.ts` (3), `app.routes.spec.ts` (11 — route table shape, lazy-only), `provider.component.spec.ts` (20), `view1.component.spec.ts` (23), `view2.component.spec.ts` (30). All component specs mock `@macro/openfin` and `@macro/logger` at module level with `jest.mock()` and provide mock service instances through DI, with `provideZonelessChangeDetection()` + `NO_ERRORS_SCHEMA`. The provider spec's notable behavioral coverage: storage-environment picking (no-op on re-pick, persists non-default choice, passes `undefined` to clear the override when picking the settings default, no-op when pinned by an **effective** `?storageEnv=`, and — via `window.history.pushState('?storageEnv=bogus')` — that a query value which lost resolution does not lock the picker), plus the quit confirm/cancel/confirm flow.

---

## Apps: macro-angular-fdc3 (4203) & capital-markets-themes (4206)

Two small showcase apps. `macro-angular-fdc3` demonstrates FDC3 2.0 context linking between OpenFin views; `capital-markets-themes` is a React theme-palette showcase (a Figma Make export mounted inside an NX app). Both appear in the OpenFin store/dock with category `Showcase`.

### macro-angular-fdc3 — FDC3 Instrument Viewer (port 4203)

Angular 22, zoneless, standalone components. Serve: `npm run start:fdc3` (= `nx serve macro-angular-fdc3`). Built in CI via `build:apps:ci` (`macro-angular-fdc3:build:production`). Displays the most recent `fdc3.instrument` context broadcast on the view's color-linking channel, plus a rolling history.

Key files:

- `apps/macro-angular-fdc3/src/main.ts` — `bootstrapApplication(App, appConfig)`; bootstrap errors logged via `@macro/logger`.
- `apps/macro-angular-fdc3/src/app/app.config.ts` — providers: `provideBrowserGlobalErrorListeners()`, `provideAnimationsAsync()`, `provideZonelessChangeDetection()`, `provideRouter(appRoutes)`, `providePrimeNG({ theme: { preset: Aura, options: { darkModeSelector: '.dark' } } })`. Build target has `"polyfills": []` (no zone.js).
- `apps/macro-angular-fdc3/src/app/app.routes.ts` — `/instrument-viewer` → `InstrumentViewerComponent`; `''` redirects there (`pathMatch: 'full'`).
- `apps/macro-angular-fdc3/src/app/app.ts` — root `App` sets `Logger.setGlobalLevel(LogLevel.DEBUG)` and injects `ThemeService` from `@macro/macro-design/angular`; injecting it alone activates the shared theme controller (default theme `'macro'`, applies `.dark`/light, syncs with OS + OpenFin platform theme).
- `apps/macro-angular-fdc3/src/styles.css` — imports `fonts.css`, `macro-etrading.css`, `macro-design.css` from `libs/macro-design`; body styled with `var(--background)` / `var(--foreground)`.

`InstrumentViewerComponent` (`apps/macro-angular-fdc3/src/app/instrument-viewer/instrument-viewer.component.ts`):

```typescript
interface InstrumentContext extends Context {   // Context from @finos/fdc3
  type: 'fdc3.instrument';
  id: { ticker: string; [key: string]: string };
  name?: string;
}
interface ContextHistoryEntry { instrument: InstrumentContext; receivedAt: Date; }
```

- State is signals (`currentInstrument`, `contextHistory`, `channelColor`) — a code comment notes FDC3/interop callbacks and the channel poll fire outside Angular, so under zoneless only signal writes schedule change detection.
- `ngOnInit`: subscribes `ContextService.currentChannel$` → `channelColor` signal, and `ContextService.onContext<InstrumentContext>('fdc3.instrument')` → sets `currentInstrument` and prepends to `contextHistory`, capped at 20 entries (newest first).
- `ngOnDestroy`: unsubscribes both and calls `ContextService.removeListener()` to drop the FDC3 listener.
- Helpers assume 6-char FX tickers: `getBase(ticker)` = first 3 chars, `getQuote(ticker)` = rest. `getChannelDotColor()` maps channel ids (`green` → `#00CC88`, `red`, `orange`, `blue`, `purple`, `yellow`; unknown/none → `#888`). Template (`instrument-viewer.component.html`) shows a channel badge with pulsing "Listening" indicator, the current instrument card (type/ticker plus any extra `id` keys via `keyvalue` pipe), an empty state ("Select a currency pair in the FX Market Data view... Both views must be on the same color linking channel."), and the history list.

`ContextService` behavior (`libs/openfin/src/lib/context.service.ts`, exported from `@macro/openfin` — framework-agnostic, no Angular DI decorators of its own):

- Resolves the desktop agent from `globalThis.fdc3`; outside OpenFin every operation degrades: `broadcast()` and `registerContextListener()` log `'FDC3 desktop agent not available'` and no-op.
- `onContext<T>(contextType)` auto-registers an FDC3 context listener for that type and returns the shared `context$` (`shareReplay(1)`) filtered by `ctx.type === contextType`. `registerContextListener()` replaces any prior listener (only one FDC3 listener at a time).
- `currentChannel$: Observable<string | null>` emits the current context-group id (e.g. `'green'`) via a ref-counted 1000 ms poll of `fdc3.getCurrentChannel()` (`CHANNEL_POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 1000`), `distinctUntilChanged`, `null` when not joined; polling starts on first subscription, stops when the last unsubscribes, and poll errors are swallowed. Gotcha: if `fdc3` is absent when polling starts, it emits `null` once and never retries.

No intents: the app registers no intent handlers and its registry entry has `"intents": []` — it is context-listening only. The broadcasting counterpart is `macro-angular`'s FX Market Data grid (`apps/macro-angular/src/app/fx-market-data/fx-market-data.component.ts` `onRowClicked`), which calls `contextService.broadcast({ type: 'fdc3.instrument', id: { ticker: pair.symbol }, name: 'BASE/QUOTE' })`. Delivery requires both views on the same color channel; the view manifests default all views to green.

OpenFin registration (per env under `apps/macro-workspace/public/{local,openshift}/`):

```json
// macro-angular-fdc3-instrument-viewer.fin.json (local)
{ "url": "http://localhost:4203/instrument-viewer",
  "fdc3InteropApi": "2.0",
  "interop": { "currentContextGroup": "green" } }
```

Registry entry in `apps.json`: `appId`/`name` `macro-angular-fdc3-instrument-viewer`, title "FDC3 Instrument Viewer", `manifestType: "view"`, tags `view/angular/fdc3/instrument/showcase`, `category: "Showcase"`. Dock: content-menu item `cm-fdc3-instrument-viewer` inside the Showcase folder of `dock-config.json`.

Tests: Jest (`apps/macro-angular-fdc3/project.json` test target declares `{workspaceRoot}/reports/{projectName}-junit.xml` output); `instrument-viewer.component.spec.ts` has 11 specs (empty state, ticker parsing, channel display, context → signal/history updates). A `serve-static` target serves `dist/apps/macro-angular-fdc3/browser` on 4203 as an SPA.

### capital-markets-themes — Theme Showcase (port 4206)

React 19 + Vite. Serve: `npm run start:capital-markets-themes`. NOT in `build:apps:ci` (CI does not build it) and it has no inferred `test` target (no unit tests); `apps/capital-markets-themes/project.json` has `"targets": {}` — everything (build/serve/dev/preview/serve-static/typecheck/lint) is inferred by the `@nx/vite` plugin.

Two Vite configs exist side by side; `apps/capital-markets-themes/vite.config.ts` is the effective one (Vite resolves `.ts` before `.mts`): `base: '/capital-markets-themes/'`, port **4206**, `@tailwindcss/postcss` plugin, aliases `@` → `./src` and `@macro/macro-design[/react]` → the lib source under `libs/macro-design/src`. `vite.config.mts` is a stale NX-generated leftover (port 4200, no base).

Entry chain:

1. `apps/capital-markets-themes/index.html` → `src/main.tsx`, which imports `libs/macro-design` CSS (`fonts.css`, `macro-etrading.css`, `macro-design.css`) plus `./figma/styles/index.css`, then calls `themeController.start()` (from `@macro/macro-design/react`) **before** `ReactDOM.createRoot(...).render(<App />)` so the `.dark`/theme class is applied before first paint.
2. `src/app/app.tsx` — calls `useTheme()` (React adapter in `libs/macro-design/src/lib/react/index.ts`: wraps the framework-agnostic `themeController` via `useSyncExternalStore`; returns `ThemeState` + `toggle`/`setDark`/`setTheme`) and renders `<BrowserRouter basename="/capital-markets-themes/"><FigmaApp /></BrowserRouter>`.
3. `src/figma/app/App.tsx` — the actual showcase page (a Figma Make export).

What it demos (`src/figma/app/App.tsx`):

- Exports `interface Theme { name; description; light: ColorScheme; dark: ColorScheme }` and `interface ColorScheme` with 35 hex-color keys using the OpenFin Workspace palette vocabulary — `background1..6`, `contentBackground1..5`, `brandPrimary{,Active,Hover,Focused,Text}`, `brandSecondary{,Active,Hover,Focused,Text}`, `input{Background,Color,Placeholder,Disabled,Focused,Border}`, `text{Default,Help,Inactive}`, `status{Success,Warning,Critical,Active}`, `borderNeutral` — the same OpenFin Workspace palette vocabulary as `ThemePalette` in `libs/macro-design/src/lib/theme.config.ts`, but not the same key set: `ColorScheme` adds `contentBackground1..5` and `borderNeutral` (absent from `ThemePalette`), while `ThemePalette` has a `backgroundPrimary` key that `ColorScheme` lacks.
- 13 built-in themes, each with full light + dark palettes: Default OpenFin, Bloomberg Terminal, Midnight Trader, Carbon Graphite, Royal Purple, FX Dealer Pro, Commodities Desk, Risk & Analytics, Stagecoach Red, Executive Blue, Neo Quantum, Structured Credit Pro, Ubuntu Desktop. Initial selection is `themes[1]` (Bloomberg Terminal) in dark mode.
- UI: theme-selector cards (dark-palette swatch dots), Dark/Light mode toggle, and a "Download Theme JSON" button that serializes `{ light, dark }` to `<kebab-cased-name>-theme.json` via a Blob — a drop-in shape for OpenFin `customThemes` palettes (same shape as `src/figma/imports/theme-colors.json`).

Components (`src/figma/app/components/`):

- `ThemePreview.tsx` — a mock trading terminal rendered entirely from the selected `ColorScheme` via inline styles: toolbar with Settings/Execute Trade buttons (brandSecondary/brandPrimary), S&P 500 / NASDAQ / Portfolio stat tiles, an order book (bids in `statusSuccess`, asks in `statusCritical`), a quick-trade ticket whose Buy/Sell button text color comes from a YIQ luminance contrast helper, an "On The Run Treasuries" AG Grid, and a status bar. The grid deliberately uses plain `ag-grid-community` (`AllCommunityModule` + `themeQuartz.withParams({...palette-mapped params})`) rather than the shared Enterprise wrapper `@macro/macro-react-grid` or `buildAgGridTheme` — the point is theming AG Grid from an arbitrary palette. Code comment on the change column: "Yield up = price down = critical (red) for bonds" (inverted trend coloring).
- `ThemeEditor.tsx` — read-only palette browser: 8 groups (Backgrounds, Content Backgrounds, Text Colors, Brand Primary, Brand Secondary, Input Fields, Status Colors, Borders) with swatch + hex per token, plus a JSON export preview of `{ [mode]: colors }`.

Styling: `src/figma/styles/index.css` imports `fonts.css`, `tailwind.css` (Tailwind 4 — `@import 'tailwindcss' source(none)` + `@source '../**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}'` + `tw-animate-css`), and `theme.css` (Figma-exported shadcn token set with its own `:root`/`.dark` blocks and `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark *))` — a contained exception to the repo rule against per-app `:root` token blocks, since it is generated Figma output). The page chrome outside the preview is hard-coded dark (Tailwind slate gradient); `useTheme()` still keeps the `.dark` class synced with the shared controller (localStorage persistence + OS/OpenFin platform sync).

Unused Figma Make baggage shipped in the tree (nothing imports it from the live page): `src/figma/app/components/ui/*` (40+ shadcn/Radix components incl. `sidebar.tsx`, `chart.tsx`), `src/figma/app/components/figma/ImageWithFallback.tsx`, the alternate entry `src/figma/main.tsx`, `src/figma/imports/theme-colors.json`, and NX-generator leftovers `src/app/nx-welcome.tsx` / `src/app/app.module.css`.

OpenFin registration gotcha: the registry entry (`appId: "capital-markets-themes"`, title "Capital Markets Theme", tags `view/react/figma-make/showcase`, `category: "Showcase"`, screenshot `screenshots/capital-markets-themes.png`) points at `capital-markets-themes.fin.json`, whose URL is `http://localhost:4202/MacroThemeCondensed/workspace.html` — a static theme-sample page hosted from `apps/macro-workspace/public/MacroThemeCondensed/` (themes/ui-kit assets + `workspace.html`), **not** the Vite dev server on 4206. The live React app is a browser-only dev tool at `http://localhost:4206/capital-markets-themes/`. Dock: content-menu item `cm-capital-markets-themes` in the Showcase folder.

---

## App: prism — Blotter as a Service, Angular (port 4204)

Config-driven Capital Markets blotter at `apps/prism/` (Angular 22, standalone components, zoneless, PrimeNG 21 Aura). The user picks a data source (AMPS / NATS core / NATS JetStream / Solace / plain WebSocket / REST) from a seeded catalog — or defines an ad-hoc one at runtime — and gets a snapshot + real-time AG Grid 36 blotter. All framework-free logic (source model, feed, column inference, roll-ups, source store, table discovery) lives in `@macro/prism-core`; this app is the Angular/signals adapter over it. `apps/prism-react/` (port 4205) is the React twin over the same core.

- Serve: `npm run start:prism` → `nx serve prism` → `http://localhost:4204` (port set in `apps/prism/project.json` `serve.options.port`).
- Build: `@angular/build:application` → `dist/apps/prism`; `polyfills: []` (no zone.js); assets glob copies `apps/prism/public/` so the seed catalog is served at `/data-sources.json`.
- Bootstrap: `apps/prism/src/main.ts` logs via `Logger.getLogger('Prism')` (`@macro/logger`) and calls `bootstrapApplication(App, appConfig)`.
- `apps/prism/src/styles.css` imports, in order: `libs/macro-design/src/lib/css/fonts.css`, `macro-etrading.css`, `macro-design.css`, then `primeicons/primeicons.css`.

### App config, routes, shell

`apps/prism/src/app/app.config.ts` providers: `provideBrowserGlobalErrorListeners()`, `provideAnimationsAsync()`, `provideZonelessChangeDetection()`, `provideRouter(appRoutes)`, `provideHttpClient(withXhr())`, `providePrimeNG({ theme: { preset: Aura, options: { darkModeSelector: '.dark' } } })`, and `provideAppInitializer(() => inject(DataSourceRepository).loadCatalog())` — the catalog is loaded before the first route renders.

`apps/prism/src/app/app.routes.ts`:

```typescript
{ path: 'blotter', component: BlotterComponent }   // primary surface
{ path: 'sources', component: SourceCatalogComponent }
{ path: '', redirectTo: 'blotter', pathMatch: 'full' }
```

Shell (`apps/prism/src/app/app.ts`, selector `app-root`): PrimeNG `Menubar` with Blotter/Catalog items, a "Prism / Blotter as a Service" brand link, and a light/dark toggle backed by the shared `ThemeService` from `@macro/macro-design/angular`.

### Services

`apps/prism/src/app/services/data-source-repository.service.ts` — `DataSourceRepository` (`providedIn: 'root'`): Angular adapter over the framework-free `DataSourceStore` from `@macro/prism-core`. It instantiates a private store, subscribes to it, and bumps a private `version = signal(0)` on every store change so computeds invalidate. Surface:

- `readonly all: Signal<BlotterSource[]>` and `readonly adhocSources: Signal<BlotterSource[]>` — computeds that read `version()` then delegate to `store.getAll()` / `store.getAdhoc()`.
- `loadCatalog(): Promise<void>` — injects Angular `HttpClient` as the store's catalog fetcher (`firstValueFrom(http.get<BlotterSource[]>(url))`); the store's default URL is `/data-sources.json`. Catalog fetch failure degrades: the store logs a warning and starts with an empty catalog (ad-hoc sources still work).
