- `get(id)`, `byTransport(t)`, `byMode(m)`, `groupedByCategory()` (reads `version()` internally so computeds built on the method — e.g. the blotter's `pickerGroups` — re-evaluate after ad-hoc CRUD and the async catalog load), `addAdhoc(src)`, `updateAdhoc(src)`, `removeAdhoc(id)`.
- Ad-hoc persistence (store behavior): localStorage key `prism.adhoc-sources.v1`, generated ids `adhoc-<slug>-<rand>` (8 chars of `crypto.randomUUID()`); persistence failure logs a warning, never throws to the UI.

`apps/prism/src/app/services/active-source.service.ts` — `ActiveSourceService`: `readonly active = signal<BlotterSource | null>(null)` with `open(source)` / `clear()`. Holds the source being opened so catalog/dialog can hand it to the blotter route without serializing it through the URL; the URL only carries `?source=<id>` for deep links.

### Blotter page (`/blotter`)

`apps/prism/src/app/components/blotter/blotter.component.ts` (`BlotterComponent`, selector `app-blotter`). A fresh instance with no `?source=` shows an empty state ("No data source selected" + Select/New buttons).

**Source resolution & deep links.** `routeSourceId = toSignal(route.queryParamMap.pipe(map(pm => pm.get('source'))))`; `resolvedSource` is a computed that looks the id up in `repo.all()` (so it re-resolves once the async catalog load completes) and falls back to `activeSource.active()`. A constructor `effect` guarded by a `appliedId` field applies the source only when the id actually changes (avoids redundant feed restarts), calling `applySource(src)`: tear down the old feed, set `source`/`rollup` (from `src.rollup`)/`rollupOn` (from `src.rollup?.enabled`), adopt `src.columnMode`, remount. Because the source id lives in the URL, each blotter view persists its source through OpenFin layout save/restore.

**Feed lifecycle & GridOps adapter.** `restartGrid()` stops the feed, clears `columns` (so the new grid doesn't mount with stale defs — avoids a visible flash and a spurious group-removal event when rolled up), toggles `showGrid` false→true across two `setTimeout(0)`s (the wrapper's `setInitialRowData` is one-shot per grid instance, so a remount is required), then `startFeed()` adapts the `MacroAngularGrid` viewChild to prism-core's framework-free `GridOps`:

```typescript
const ops: GridOps = {
  addRows:    (rows) => grid.addRows$.next(rows),
  updateRows: (rows) => grid.updateRows$.next(rows),
  deleteRows: (rows) => grid.deleteRows$.next(rows),
  setInitialRowData: (rows) => grid.setInitialRowData(rows),
};
const feed = new BlotterFeed(src, ops, (rec) => this.onFirstRecord(rec));
```

Feed status is mirrored into a `feedState` signal (`{ status, rowCount, msgsPerSec, error }`, `FeedStatus = 'idle' | 'connecting' | 'snapshot-loading' | 'live' | 'error' | 'stopped'`) via `feed.subscribe(...)` so the zoneless template stays reactive. `getRowId`: append mode keys rows by the synthetic `__id`; keyed modes by `source.keyField` (fallback `__id`).

**Toolbar / status bar** (`blotter.component.html`): source-name trigger button (opens the picker popover) with transport + mode tags (`modeSeverity`: streaming→`danger`, append→`warn`, else `success`); a status `p-tag` (`statusSeverity`: live→success, error→danger, connecting/snapshot-loading→warn, else secondary); row count; msgs/sec — hidden for REST sources (comment: a frozen "0 msg/s" would read like a dead feed); feed errors render as a `p-message severity="error"`. Actions:

- **Infer / Auto (v36)** `p-selectButton` — `setColumnMode()` restarts the grid with the new column strategy.
- **Roll-up** button — disabled until `hasRollup()`; `toggleRollup()` flips `rollupOn` and remounts so grouping options settle at grid creation.
- **Refresh** (REST sources only, enabled when status is `live` or `error`) — `feed.refresh()` re-fetches the snapshot and diffs it into the grid in place.
- **Reconnect** — `restartGrid()`; **Disconnect** — `feed.stop()`.

**Column modes.** `onFirstRecord(rec)` runs `inferColumns(rec, keyField)` → `{ columns, formats }`. In `infer` mode the columns signal is set and `seedFormats` (a computed that returns `formats()` only on the infer path) seeds the wrapper's Format-panel store. In `auto-gen` mode, `gridOptions` adds `autoGenerateColumnDefs: { objectValues: 'flatten', nullishValues: 'include' }` plus a `processAutoGeneratedColumnDefs` callback that decorates the generated defs with `applyInferredFormats(this.formats())` and the active roll-up — the callback reads the signals at call time because v36 column generation runs synchronously with the first data batch, before change detection re-delivers the config completed in `onFirstRecord`.

**Roll-up view.** Signals: `rollup` (the source's own config, else a suggestion), `rollupOn`, `hasRollup = groupBy.length > 0`, `activeRollup` (non-null only when on AND usable). `effectiveColumns` applies `applyRollupToColumns`; `gridOptions` merges `rollupGridOptions(r)` plus an `onColumnRowGroupChanged` handler that turns the roll-up view off when the user clears every group inside the grid (status-bar Grouping toggle / row-group panel) — it filters `event.source`, ignoring `'gridOptionsChanged'` and `'gridInitializing'` so the component's own columnDefs pushes (e.g. the transient empty-columns push during a feed restart) never flip the view. In `onFirstRecord`, an explicit source hierarchy wins via `completeRollup(src.rollup, columns)` (fills inferred aggregations for uncovered fields); otherwise — including "open rolled up" saved with a blank hierarchy — `suggestRollup(columns, { keyField, mode })` supplies one so the toggle works on any payload with plausible grouping fields.

**Source picker popover.** A `p-popover` shared by the toolbar trigger and the empty state; autofocuses a search input. `pickerGroups` filters `repo.groupedByCategory()` by name/category/topic/transport label (case-insensitive). Each row: `switchSource(src)` hides the popover, calls `activeSource.open(src)` and navigates to `/blotter?source=<id>` (same-id switches no-op). Ad-hoc entries get inline **edit** and **delete** icon buttons; catalog entries (read-only) get a **Duplicate as ad-hoc** button — both open the shared dialog. `removeSource` on the currently-open source clears `ActiveSourceService` and navigates back to the empty `/blotter`. Footer: **New data source** + a **Browse all** link to `/sources`. `onSourceSaved(src)`: if the saved source IS the active one (same id after an in-place edit), it calls `applySource(src)` directly — `switchSource` would no-op on the same id and leave the old connection settings and roll-up running.

### Ad-hoc source dialog

`apps/prism/src/app/components/adhoc-source-dialog/adhoc-source-dialog.component.ts` (`AdHocSourceDialogComponent`, selector `app-adhoc-source-dialog`, `saved = output<BlotterSource>()`). One dialog serves three flows via `show(source?)`:

- no arg → **create** blank (`form.reset` defaults: category `Custom`, transport `nats`, mode `streaming`, columnMode `infer`);
- an `origin: 'adhoc'` source → **edit in place** (`editingId` set; save calls `repo.updateAdhoc`);
- a catalog source → **duplicate** (form pre-filled via `patchFrom`, name suffixed `" (copy)"`, save calls `repo.addAdhoc` — the catalog itself is read-only).

Reactive form controls: `name`*, `category`*, `transport`*, `mode`*, `topic`*, `filter`, `topN` (`Validators.min(1)`), `orderBy`, `keyField`, `conflationMs`, `maxRows`, `expandArrays` (default true), `columnMode`*, `rollupGroupBy` (comma-separated text), `rollupEnabled`; per-transport connection fields `url`/`logon`/`heartbeat` (AMPS), `servers`/`clientName`/`stream` (NATS / JetStream), `hostUrl`/`vpnName`/`userName`/`password` (Solace), `wsUrl`, `restUrl`.

**AMPS "Initial SOW Top N" + "Order by" (PR #81).** Rendered only when transport = AMPS, alongside the AMPS Filter field: `topN` is a `p-inputnumber` (min 1, step 1, no grouping/fractions; hint: "Limits rows returned by the initial SOW query; matching live updates continue") and `orderBy` is free text (placeholder `/orderDate DESC, /customerName ASC`, hint "Comma-separated: /field [ASC | DESC] [TEXT]"). Behavior:

- `canSave()` rejects an AMPS `topN` that is set but not a positive integer (`!Number.isInteger(v.topN) || v.topN < 1`); `null` is fine (optional).
- `save()` includes `topN` only when `transport === 'amps' && topN != null`, and `orderBy` only when AMPS and non-blank after `trim()` (the trimmed value is persisted) — values typed then abandoned by switching to another transport are dropped from the payload.
- `patchFrom` restores both on edit and on catalog duplicate, so the settings round-trip.
- Downstream (prism-core): `BlotterSource.topN?: number` / `orderBy?: string`; `BlotterFeed.wireAmps()` builds `AmpsSowOptions { topN?, orderBy? }` and passes them to `AmpsTransport.sowAndSubscribe(topic, filter, options)` — `top_n` bounds only the initial SOW result (no `skip_n`; live updates keep flowing), `orderBy` becomes the AMPS `OrderBy` header. No seeded catalog entry sets them; they are dialog/ad-hoc features (also documented in `apps/prism/README.md` § AMPS).

**Transport switching & table discovery.** `onTransportChange` distinguishes transport families — `'table'` (websocket/rest) vs `'subject'` (brokers) — and clears `topic` on a family change (a broker subject is not a table name); switching to REST pins `mode: 'snapshot-update'` (REST is snapshot-only; the Behavior select is replaced by a hint). WebSocket/REST panels have a **Load tables** button → `discoverTables()` calls `discoverWsTables(url)` / `discoverRestTables(url)` from `@macro/prism-core`, guarded by a `discoverEpoch` counter (bumped on dialog open, URL edits, and transport change) so a stale in-flight discovery cannot mutate a newer session; errors land in `discoverError`. The first discovered table is auto-picked only when `topic` is blank. The Table field is an editable `p-select`; `onTableChange` ignores `originalEvent.type === 'input'` (typing fires onChange per keystroke and a half-typed name must not adopt another table's key field/mode); a real pick runs `applyTableSuggestions` — adopts the table name plus the server's suggested `keyField` and `mode` (mode never adopted for REST). Opening the dialog from a saved ws/rest source seeds the table list with `[{ name: source.topic }]` so the select shows it without re-discovery.

**Validation (`canSave`)**: name required; `topic` required except REST (the URL itself may be the rows endpoint); `keyField` required unless mode is `append`; AMPS topN rule above; connection completeness per transport — AMPS `url`, NATS/JetStream `servers`, Solace `hostUrl`+`vpnName`+`userName`, WebSocket `wsUrl`, REST `restUrl`.

**Save payload assembly**: `filter` only for AMPS; `conflationMs` dropped for REST (no stream to conflate); `maxRows` only for `append`; `expandArrays` persisted only when `false` (true is the default; the checkbox is hidden for ws/rest table sources, where arrays are protocol framing and always expand); roll-up via `buildRollup(groupByText, enabled)` — a blank hierarchy with "Open rolled up" checked still persists (`{ groupBy: [], enabled: true }` → blotter opens on the suggested hierarchy), and a private `rollupExtras` field preserves `RollupConfig` members the form doesn't surface (`aggregations`, `expandLevels`, `grandTotal`) across an edit/duplicate round-trip. Footer buttons: Cancel / **Open** (saves, closes, emits `saved`).

### Source catalog page (`/sources`)

`apps/prism/src/app/components/source-catalog/source-catalog.component.ts` (`SourceCatalogComponent`): the full card grid of every source. A `p-selectButton` picks the grouping — **Category** (delegates to `repo.groupedByCategory()`), **Type** (by `transport`, titled via `TRANSPORT_LABELS`), **Behavior** (by `mode`, via `MODE_LABELS`). Each `p-card` shows transport/mode tags (+ an `ad-hoc` info tag), name, description, category + topic meta, and a footer: **Open** (→ `ActiveSourceService.open` + navigate `/blotter?source=<id>`), then edit/delete for ad-hoc sources or **Duplicate as ad-hoc** for catalog sources (same shared dialog; `onSaved` opens the result). Header has **Add ad-hoc source**; empty catalog shows "No data sources yet — add an ad-hoc source to get started."

### Seeded catalog — `apps/prism/public/data-sources.json`

11 read-only entries (all pre-wired to the local broker lab / market-data-server; entries omit `origin` — the store stamps catalog provenance):

| id | name | transport | mode | topic | key / extras |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `amps-fx-spot` | AMPS FX Spot (SOW) | amps `ws://localhost:9008/amps/json` | snapshot-update | `fx_spot` | `symbol`, conflation 150ms |
| `amps-orders-blotter` | AMPS Orders (append) | amps | append | `orders` | filter `/status <> 'DONE'`, maxRows 5000, key `orderId` |
| `nats-analytics` | NATS Analytics Stream | nats `ws://localhost:9222` | append | `macro.analytics.>` | maxRows 1000 |
| `nats-fx-stream` | NATS FX Quotes (streaming) | nats | streaming | `macro.fx.quotes.>` | `symbol`, conflation 250ms |
| `natsjs-positions` | JetStream Positions (snapshot) | nats-js, stream `POSITIONS` | snapshot-update | `macro.positions.>` | `bookId` |
| `solace-treasury` | Solace UST Prices (streaming) | solace `ws://localhost:8008` (vpn/user/pass `default`) | streaming | `ust/prices/>` | `cusip`, conflation 300ms, **columnMode `auto-gen`** |
| `ws-ust-market-data` | WS UST Market Data (snapshot) | websocket `ws://localhost:3000/prism` | snapshot-update | `ust_market_data` | `symbol`, conflation 250ms |
| `ws-ust-trades` | WS UST Trades (append) | websocket | append | `ust_trades` | `tradeId`, maxRows 1000, rollup `["instrumentType","symbol","side"]` (size sum / price avg, `enabled`) |
| `ws-irs-risk-pnl` | WS IRS Risk / PnL | websocket | snapshot-update | `irs_risk_pnl` | `tradeId`, conflation 250ms, rollup `["desk","book","trader"]` enabled, `aggregations: { notional: "none" }` (mixed-ccy notionals must not sum) |
| `rest-irs-risk-pnl` | REST IRS Risk / PnL (snapshot) | rest `http://localhost:3000/prism/tables` | snapshot-update | `irs_risk_pnl` | `tradeId`, same Desk→Book→Trader roll-up + `notional: "none"` |
| `rest-ust-market-data` | REST UST Market Data (snapshot) | rest | snapshot-update | `ust_market_data` | `symbol` |

All entries use `columnMode: "infer"` except `solace-treasury` (`auto-gen`). None set `topN`/`orderBy`.

### Tests

Jest 30 (`@nx/jest`, `jest-preset-angular`); run with `npx nx test prism` (JUnit output declared: `{workspaceRoot}/reports/{projectName}-junit.xml`).

- `apps/prism/src/app/app.spec.ts` — shell creation with a stubbed `ThemeService`; asserts a "Catalog" menu item exists.
- `apps/prism/src/app/components/adhoc-source-dialog/adhoc-source-dialog.component.spec.ts` — PR #81 coverage of the AMPS result controls, with the component template overridden to empty (logic-only) and a mocked `DataSourceRepository`: (1) saving persists a positive `topN` and a trimmed `orderBy`; (2) `show()` on an ad-hoc AMPS source restores `topN`/`orderBy` and saves via `updateAdhoc`, while `show()` on the same source with `origin: 'catalog'` renames to `"AMPS Orders (copy)"` and saves the settings through `addAdhoc`; (3) a non-AMPS save omits `topN`/`orderBy` even when the controls hold values; (4) `canSave()` accepts `null`/`1`, rejects `0` and `1.5`.

---

## App: prism-react — Blotter as a Service, React (port 4205)

React 19 + Vite mirror of the Angular `prism` app (port 4204): a generic real-time blotter that connects to any of six transports (AMPS, core NATS, NATS JetStream, Solace, plain-WebSocket table server, snapshot-only REST) via the framework-free `@macro/prism-core` engine and renders rows in `MacroReactGrid` (AG Grid 36 Enterprise). UI chrome is Shadcn-style components (Radix primitives) under `apps/prism-react/src/components/ui/` (badge, button, card, dialog, input, label, popover, select) plus lucide-react icons and Tailwind CSS 4.

- Serve: `npm run start:prism-react` (alias `serve:prism-react`) → `nx serve prism-react` at `http://localhost:4205/prism-react/`. Build: `npm run build:prism-react` → `dist/apps/prism-react`; included in the CI list (`build:apps:ci` in `package.json`).
- OpenFin: registered as appId `prism-react-blotter` in `apps/macro-workspace/public/{local,openshift}/apps.json` via view manifest `prism-react.fin.json` (category `Blotters`).
- The two blotter apps share the exact same seed catalog: `apps/prism-react/public/data-sources.json` is byte-identical to the Angular prism's copy (verified by diff) — 11 sources: 2 AMPS (`amps-fx-spot` snapshot-update, `amps-orders-blotter` append with filter `/status <> 'DONE'` + `maxRows: 5000`), 2 core NATS (`nats-analytics` append, `nats-fx-stream` streaming conflated 250 ms), `natsjs-positions` (JetStream snapshot-update, stream `POSITIONS`), `solace-treasury` (streaming, `columnMode: "auto-gen"`), 3 WebSocket table sources against `ws://localhost:3000/prism` (`ust_market_data`, `ust_trades` with a `rollup` `["instrumentType","symbol","side"]` opened enabled, `irs_risk_pnl` with rollup `["desk","book","trader"]` + `aggregations: { notional: "none" }`), and 2 REST sources against `http://localhost:3000/prism/tables` (`irs_risk_pnl` with the same rollup, `ust_market_data`).

### File layout

```
apps/prism-react/
├── index.html                      # <div id="root">, title "Prism — Blotter as a Service"
├── vite.config.mts                 # base /prism-react/, port 4205, aliases, embedded Vitest config
├── project.json                    # test outputs: coverage/{projectRoot} + reports/prism-react-junit.xml
├── public/data-sources.json        # seed catalog (identical to apps/prism)
└── src/
    ├── main.tsx                    # bootstrap: theme, PrimeReactProvider, logger
    ├── styles.css                  # macro-design imports + Tailwind 4 + Shadcn token bridge
    ├── lib/utils.ts                # cn() class-merge helper
    ├── components/ui/*.tsx         # Shadcn/Radix primitives
    └── app/
        ├── app.tsx                 # BrowserRouter basename="/prism-react", routes, header shell
        ├── data-source-context.tsx # DataSourceStore provider + useSyncExternalStore adapter
        └── components/
            ├── blotter.tsx         # feed lifecycle, GridOps adapter, roll-up, toolbar
            ├── source-picker.tsx   # popover source switcher
            ├── source-catalog.tsx  # /sources card gallery
            └── adhoc-source-dialog.tsx  # create/edit/duplicate dialog (incl. AMPS Top N / Order by)
```

### Bootstrap & shell

`src/main.tsx`: creates `Logger.getLogger('PrismReact')`, calls `themeController.start()` from `@macro/macro-design/react` BEFORE first paint (applies the 'macro' theme + `.dark` class), then renders `<App/>` inside `<PrimeReactProvider theme={{ preset: Aura, options: { darkModeSelector: '.dark' } }}>`.

`src/app/app.tsx`: `<BrowserRouter basename="/prism-react">` wrapping `<DataSourceProvider>`. Routes: `/blotter` → `Blotter`, `/sources` → `SourceCatalog`, `*` → `<Navigate to="/blotter" replace/>`. The header nav uses `useTheme()` (`{ isDark, toggle }`) from `@macro/macro-design/react` for the light/dark toggle button.

`src/styles.css` imports, in order: `libs/macro-design/src/lib/css/fonts.css`, `macro-etrading.css`, `macro-design.css`, then `primeicons/primeicons.css`, `tailwindcss`, `tw-animate-css`; declares `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark *))` (class-based dark mode) and an `@theme inline` block mapping Shadcn tokens (`--color-background: var(--background)` etc.) onto the macro-design CSS variables.

### `vite.config.mts`

- `base: '/prism-react/'`; `server`/`preview` on `port: 4205, host: 'localhost'`; plugins `react()`, `nxViteTsPaths()`, `nxCopyAssetsPlugin(['*.md'])`.
- Explicit `resolve.alias` entries (in addition to nxViteTsPaths): `'@'` → `./src`, plus source-file aliases for `@macro/logger`, `@macro/prism-core`, `@macro/macro-react-grid`, `@macro/macro-grid-format` **and its `/react` sub-path** (which must be listed BEFORE the bare alias to match), `@macro/macro-design` + `/react`, `@macro/transports`, `@macro/utils` — all pointing at `libs/*/src/...` source, not build output.
- `build.outDir: '../../dist/apps/prism-react'`, `commonjsOptions: { transformMixedEsModules: true }`.
- Embedded Vitest config: `environment: 'jsdom'`, `globals: true`, junit reporter → `../../reports/prism-react-junit.xml`, v8 coverage → `../../coverage/apps/prism-react`. `project.json` declares both as NX `test` outputs so cache replays keep the merged CI reports complete.

### Data-source context — `useSyncExternalStore` over prism-core

`src/app/data-source-context.tsx` exports three things:

```tsx
export function DataSourceProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode });
export function useDataSourceStore(): DataSourceStore;   // throws outside the provider
export function useAllSources(): BlotterSource[];        // useSyncExternalStore(store.subscribe, store.getAll)
```

`DataSourceProvider` creates ONE `DataSourceStore` (from `@macro/prism-core`) via `useMemo` and on mount calls `store.loadCatalog((url) => fetch(url).then(r => r.json()), \`${import.meta.env.BASE_URL}data-sources.json\`)` — the fetch function is injected because prism-core has no HttpClient/fetch dependency; with `base: '/prism-react/'` the catalog resolves to `/prism-react/data-sources.json`. `DataSourceStore.subscribe`/`.getAll` are useSyncExternalStore-shaped (stable arrow-function identities; `getAll` returns a memoized `[...catalog, ...adhoc]` snapshot whose reference only changes on mutation, so React identity checks work). Ad-hoc sources persist in localStorage under `prism.adhoc-sources.v1`; catalog entries carry `origin: 'catalog'` (read-only), ad-hoc `origin: 'adhoc'` with generated ids `adhoc-<slug>-<n>`.

### Blotter (`src/app/components/blotter.tsx`)

Reads the active source from the `?source=` query param (`useSearchParams`) and resolves it against `useAllSources()` — so the blotter reactively follows the async catalog load and ad-hoc edits/deletes. With no/unknown source it renders an empty state (source picker + "New data source").

**Source-switch adoption during render (not an effect):** when `source.id !== modeSourceId`, the component calls `setModeSourceId/setColumnMode/setRollup/setRollupOn` directly in the render body so state is settled before the feed effect runs — avoiding a double feed restart. Editing the same source in place keeps the user's current toggles (handled separately in the dialog's `onSaved`).

**Remount-on-reconnect via `key`:** the grid is rendered as `<MacroReactGrid key={gridKey} .../>` with

```ts
const gridKey = `${source?.id ?? 'none'}:${columnMode}:${rollupOn ? 'rollup' : 'flat'}:${reconnectNonce}`;
```

so every feed (re)start remounts AG Grid — required because `getRowId`, `autoGenerateColumnDefs`, and grouping options are init-only. The Reconnect button and dialog `onSaved` bump `reconnectNonce`; switching column mode or toggling roll-up also changes the key.

**GridOps adapter over `MacroReactGridRef`:** `BlotterFeed` writes to the minimal `GridOps` interface (`addRows/updateRows/deleteRows/setInitialRowData`, all `(rows: unknown[]) => void`). The React adapter maps the first three onto the ref's RxJS Subjects and — because `MacroReactGridRef` has no `setInitialRowData` method (unlike the Angular wrapper) — seeds snapshots through the `rowData` prop state:

```ts
const ops: GridOps = {
  addRows: (rows) => grid.addRows$.next(rows),
  updateRows: (rows) => grid.updateRows$.next(rows),
  deleteRows: (rows) => grid.deleteRows$.next(rows),
  setInitialRowData: (rows) => setRowData(rows),
};
```

**Feed lifecycle effect** (deps `[source, columnMode, rollupOn, reconnectNonce]`): resets `columns/rowData/feedState`, constructs `new BlotterFeed(source, ops, firstRecordCb)`, subscribes (`feed.subscribe(() => setFeedState(feed.getState()))` — plain listener into `useState`, not useSyncExternalStore), then `void feed.start()`; cleanup unsubscribes, `void feed.stop()`, nulls `feedRef`. `FeedState` is `{ status: 'idle'|'connecting'|'snapshot-loading'|'live'|'error'|'stopped'; rowCount; msgsPerSec; error: string|null }`.

**First-record callback:** runs `inferColumns(rec, source.keyField)`; stores the inferred `ColumnFormatMap`; sets `columns` unless in `auto-gen` mode; then resolves the roll-up — an explicit `source.rollup.groupBy` is completed with inferred aggregations (`completeRollup`), otherwise (including "open rolled up" saved with a blank hierarchy) `suggestRollup(inferred.columns, { keyField, mode })`.

**Roll-up wiring:** `effectiveColumns = applyRollupToColumns(columns, activeRollup)` when the view is on; `gridOptions` memo merges `rollupGridOptions(r)` plus an `onColumnRowGroupChanged` handler that ignores `event.source === 'gridOptionsChanged' | 'gridInitializing'` (the wrapper's own columnDefs pushes, including the transient empty-columns push during feed restart) and flips `rollupOn` off via `setTimeout(..., 0)` only when a USER gesture cleared every group (status-bar toggle → `'api'`, panel drag → `'uiColumnDragged'`). In `auto-gen` column mode it also sets `autoGenerateColumnDefs: { objectValues: 'flatten', nullishValues: 'include' }` and a `processAutoGeneratedColumnDefs` that applies `applyInferredFormats(formats)` then decorates via `activeRollupRef.current` — a live-read `useRef` mirror, because column generation fires when the first data batch hits the grid, potentially before React re-renders with the completed roll-up config (a plain closure would capture the stale partial config). `seedFormats` (the `columnFormats` prop) is memoized and only populated in `infer` mode so the wrapper's restore-on-change effect fires only on real changes.

**`getRowId`:** append mode → the feed's synthetic `__id`; otherwise `String(data[source.keyField ?? '__id'])`.

**Toolbar:** SourcePicker (trigger = source name + transport/mode badges); status badge (`live`→success, `error`→destructive, `connecting`/`snapshot-loading`→warning, else secondary); row count; `msgsPerSec` hidden for REST sources (snapshot-only — a frozen "0 msg/s" would read like a dead feed); Infer / "Auto (v36)" column-mode segmented toggle (mode is in `gridKey`, so switching remounts); Roll-up toggle (disabled until a usable hierarchy exists — `rollup.groupBy.length > 0`); a Refresh button for REST only, enabled when status is `live` or `error`, calling `feedRef.current?.refresh()` (in-place snapshot diff); Reconnect (nonce bump); Disconnect (`feed.stop()`). `feedState.error` renders as a destructive banner above the grid.

**Dialog `onSaved`:** closes, bumps `reconnectNonce` (remount even when editing the ACTIVE source in place — same id means the render-time adoption block will not fire), and if `src.id === sourceId` explicitly re-adopts `src.rollup`/`enabled`, then navigates to `/blotter?source=<id>`.

### SourcePicker (`src/app/components/source-picker.tsx`)

Radix Popover whose trigger is caller-supplied (`asChild`). Search input filters on name, category, topic, or transport label (case-insensitive substring); results grouped by category; the active source row gets a `border-primary` ring. Ad-hoc rows expose Edit and Delete (delete calls `store.removeAdhoc(id)` directly); catalog rows expose "Duplicate as ad-hoc". Footer: "New data source" button + "Browse all" link to `/sources`. Props: `{ trigger, activeId?, onSelect, onNew, onEdit, onDuplicate }` — the blotter passes `onDuplicate={openEdit}` because the dialog itself decides duplicate-vs-edit from `source.origin`.

### SourceCatalog (`src/app/components/source-catalog.tsx`)

`/sources` page: card gallery with a group-by segmented toggle (`'category' | 'transport' | 'mode'`; the latter two title groups via `TRANSPORT_LABELS`/`MODE_LABELS`). Each card: transport badge, mode badge (`streaming`→destructive, `append`→warning, else success — same `modeVariant` mapping as the blotter/picker), optional `ad-hoc` badge, name, description, category, topic. Actions: Open (→ `/blotter?source=<id>`), ad-hoc Edit/Delete, catalog Duplicate-as-ad-hoc. Hosts its own `AdHocSourceDialog`; `onSaved` navigates to the blotter.

### Ad-hoc source dialog (`src/app/components/adhoc-source-dialog.tsx`)

Props `{ open, source: BlotterSource | null, onOpenChange, onSaved }`. Open semantics: `source === null` → blank form (`EMPTY`: transport `nats`, mode `streaming`, category `Custom`, `expandArrays: true`); an ad-hoc source → edit in place; a catalog source → duplicate (form pre-filled via `fromSource`, name suffixed " (copy)", saved as a NEW ad-hoc — the catalog is read-only). Form state is one flat string-typed `FormState` covering all transports; `fromSource` maps per-transport connection fields and restores `topN`/`orderBy` **only when `s.transport === 'amps'`**.

**Table discovery (websocket/rest):** "Load tables" calls `discoverWsTables(url, timeoutMs = 5_000)` / `discoverRestTables(url, timeoutMs = 10_000)` from prism-core. A `discoverSeq` ref is bumped on dialog open, transport switch, and URL edit so a stale in-flight discovery can never mutate a newer session (every await checks `seq !== discoverSeq.current`). Discovered results are filtered to non-empty string names; the first table is auto-picked ONLY when the topic is still blank (functional `setForm` — the user may have typed a topic while discovery was in flight). Picking a table adopts the server's suggested `keyField` and `mode` (mode never for REST). On open from an existing ws/rest source, the saved topic seeds the quick-pick list so it shows before re-discovery.

**Transport switching:** a `family()` helper classifies transports as `'table'` (websocket/rest) vs `'subject'` (brokers); switching across families clears `topic` (a broker subject is not a table name), and switching to REST pins `mode: 'snapshot-update'` (the Behavior select is replaced by "Snapshot only" text). WebSocket/REST sources never show the "Expand array payloads into rows" checkbox — for table protocols arrays are protocol framing, always expanded.

**AMPS Top N / Order by (PR #81):** AMPS sources get, besides URL/logon/heartbeat and Filter, two extra fields feeding `BlotterSource.topN?: number` ("Maximum number of records in the initial AMPS SOW result") and `orderBy?: string` (e.g. `/updatedAt DESC, /symbol ASC` — comma-separated AMPS fields with optional `ASC`/`DESC`/`TEXT` directives). "Initial SOW Top N" is validated by `positiveInt()` (must parse to an integer > 0); a non-empty invalid value renders "Enter a whole number greater than zero." and disables Open via `canSave()`'s amps branch: `!!form.url && (form.topN.trim() === '' || positiveInt(form.topN) != null)`. On save, `topN` is included only when valid and `orderBy` only when non-blank after `trim()`; both (and `filter`) are emitted only for `transport === 'amps'`. `BlotterFeed` forwards them into the AMPS SOW-and-subscribe options; matching live updates continue past the Top N cap.

**Other save rules (`canSave` / `save`):** name required; topic required except REST (the URL itself may be the rows endpoint); `keyField` required unless mode is `append`; per-transport required connection fields (`servers` for nats/nats-js; `hostUrl`+`vpnName`+`userName` for solace; URLs for ws/rest). `conflationMs` is dropped for REST (no stream to conflate); `maxRows` only for append; `expandArrays` serialized only when explicitly `false`. `buildRollup()` parses the comma-separated group-by, preserves extras the form does not surface (`aggregations`/`expandLevels`/`grandTotal` from the opened source, so edit/duplicate round-trips do not drop them), and persists a blank hierarchy when "Open rolled up" is checked (the blotter then opens on the suggested hierarchy). Editing an ad-hoc source calls `store.updateAdhoc({...payload, id, origin: 'adhoc'})`; anything else calls `store.addAdhoc(payload)`; the result is passed to `onSaved`.

### Tests

Vitest 4 + Testing Library, jsdom (`npx nx test prism-react`):

- `src/app/app.spec.tsx` — smoke: `SourceCatalog` inside `MemoryRouter` + `DataSourceProvider` renders the "Data Sources" header and the "Add ad-hoc source" action.
- `src/app/components/adhoc-source-dialog.spec.tsx` — PR #81 coverage: opening the dialog on an AMPS catalog source with `topN: 25, orderBy: '/updatedAt DESC'` restores both inputs; typing `2.5` into Top N disables the Open button and shows the whole-number validation message; `100` plus a padded `'  /updatedAt DESC, /orderId TEXT  '` re-enables it, and `onSaved` receives `objectContaining({ topN: 100, orderBy: '/updatedAt DESC, /orderId TEXT' })`. The test stubs global `fetch` (the provider's catalog load) and clears localStorage per test.

---

## App: market-data-server (port 3000)

Node.js simulation/reference server. One HTTP server (`http.createServer`) on `PORT` (env var, default **3000**) hosts a single `ws` `WebSocketServer` (`perMessageDeflate: false` — avoids RSV1 frame errors — and `clientTracking: false`) plus plain-HTTP REST routing. Entry: `apps/market-data-server/src/main.ts`. Not a real market data system: all prices are seeded random walks; the value is realistic shapes + protocols for the front-end apps.

Source files:

| File | Role |
| --- | --- |
| `apps/market-data-server/src/main.ts` | Server bootstrap, WS path routing, FX/Treasury connection handlers, SIGINT/SIGTERM graceful shutdown |
| `apps/market-data-server/src/fx-market-data.service.ts` | `FxMarketDataService` — G10 FX random walk |
| `apps/market-data-server/src/tsy-market-data.service.ts` | `TsyMarketDataService` — US Treasury prices/yields/duration/convexity |
| `apps/market-data-server/src/prism-table-hub.ts` | `PrismTableHub` — WS table protocol at `/prism` + REST mirror `/prism/tables` |
| `apps/market-data-server/src/prism-tables.service.ts` | `UstMarketDataTable`, `UstTradesTable`, `to32nds()` — UST universe engines |
| `apps/market-data-server/src/irs-risk-pnl.service.ts` | `IrsRiskPnlTable` — IRS desk risk/P&L simulation |
| `apps/market-data-server/src/workspace-storage-api.service.ts` | `WorkspaceStorageApi` — phase-1 Workspace Storage REST reference at `/workspace/v1` |

Routing: WS upgrades are dispatched by pathname — `/marketData/fx`, `/marketData/tsy`, `/prism`; any other path is closed with code `1008` ("Invalid path"). HTTP requests are tried against `PrismTableHub.handleRest()` then `WorkspaceStorageApi.handleRest()` (each returns `false` when the path prefix is not theirs); anything else gets a `404` JSON body with `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` (CORS on errors too, so browser clients can read the message). A top-level try/catch around HTTP handling returns `500` — one bad request never kills the process.

Run: `npm run start:market-data-server` (= `nx serve market-data-server`; `@nx/js:node` executor over an `@nx/esbuild` build — CJS, `bundle: false`, `generatePackageJson: true`, output `dist/apps/market-data-server`). Production: `npm run exec:market-data-server` → builds then `node dist/apps/market-data-server/apps/market-data-server/src/main.js`. Env vars: `PORT`, `WORKSPACE_STORE_FILE`, `WORKSPACE_CONFIG_DIR` (latter two below).

### FX endpoint — `ws://localhost:3000/marketData/fx`

15 currency pairs across the G10 currencies (USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, CAD, CHF, NZD, SEK, NOK): `EURUSD GBPUSD USDJPY AUDUSD USDCAD USDCHF NZDUSD USDSEK USDNOK EURGBP EURJPY GBPJPY AUDJPY EURCHF GBPCHF`, each with its own seed mid and volatility (e.g. `EURUSD` 1.0850 / 0.0005, `USDJPY` 149.50 / 0.15).

On connect the client receives `{ type: 'connected', message, currencies: [10 G10 codes], timestamp }`, then a **per-client** 1-second `setInterval` publishes:

```json
{ "type": "marketData",
  "data": { "pairs": [ { "base": "EUR", "quote": "USD", "symbol": "EURUSD",
      "bid": 1.08495, "ask": 1.08505, "mid": 1.085, "spread": 0.0001,
      "change": 0.00015, "changePercent": 0.0138 } ], "timestamp": "ISO" },
  "timestamp": "ISO" }
```

Behavior (`FxMarketDataService.getMarketData()`): each call random-walks every pair's stored mid (`±volatility` uniform), draws a fresh spread of 1–5 pips (pip = `0.01` for `*JPY` pairs, else `0.0001`), splits it symmetrically into bid/ask, and rounds to 2 decimals (JPY pairs) or 5. Gotcha: `change`/`changePercent` are freshly drawn random numbers (`(Math.random()-0.5)*vol*2`), NOT the actual tick-over-tick delta — the code comments this as "simplified". `base`/`quote` are `symbol.substring(0,3)` / `substring(3)`. A client message `{ "type": "subscribe" }` gets `{ type: 'subscribed', message, timestamp }` back (data flows regardless of subscribing). Since the mutable rate map lives in one shared service instance but each connection ticks it on its own interval, more connected clients = faster market movement.

### Treasury endpoint — `ws://localhost:3000/marketData/tsy`

11 securities: 3 T-Bills (zero-coupon, CUSIPs `912797XZ8/YF5/YG3`, maturities 2025-12-15 → 2026-06-15), 6 T-Notes (`91282CJX8/JY6/JZ3/KA0/KB8/KC6`, 2026 → 2034 maturities, coupons 4.00–4.75), 2 T-Bonds (`912810QZ8`, `912810RA5`, 2044/2054). On connect: `{ type: 'connected', message, securities: [{ cusip, securityType, maturity }], timestamp }` (from `getSecurities()`), then per-client 1-second publishes of:

```json
{ "type": "marketData",
  "data": { "securities": [ { "cusip", "securityType": "T-Bill|T-Note|T-Bond",
      "maturity", "yearsToMaturity", "coupon", "price", "yield", "bid", "ask",
      "spread", "change", "changePercent", "volume", "duration", "convexity" } ],
    "benchmarkRates": { "2Y": 4.30, "5Y": 4.45, "10Y": 4.55, "30Y": 4.65 },
    "timestamp": "ISO" },
  "timestamp": "ISO" }
```

`TsyMarketDataService.getMarketData()` random-walks each price by its per-security volatility (0.05 bills → 0.35 for the 30Y bond), recomputes yield from price (T-Bills < 0.5y: simple discount yield; else current yield + annualized capital gain — deliberately simplified, not true YTM), spread = 1–3 ticks of `0.03125` (1/32 point), `change` here IS the real price delta, volume is random 50–550 ($MM), duration = approximate Macaulay formula (zero-coupon → `n`), convexity ≈ `n(n+1)/(1+y)²`. `benchmarkRates` are populated from whichever security's `yearsToMaturity` falls in the 2Y/5Y/10Y/30Y windows (±0.5y), falling back to constants 4.30/4.45/4.55/4.65 when none matches. Same `{ type: 'subscribe' }` → `{ type: 'subscribed' }` echo as FX.

### Prism table hub — `ws://localhost:3000/prism` + REST mirror

`PrismTableHub` implements the JSON "table protocol" consumed by the Prism blotters' WebSocket source (`WsTableClient` in `@macro/prism-core`). All frames are JSON text:

```
server → on connect:    { "type": "tables", "tables": [{ name, title, description, keyField, mode }], "timestamp" }
client →                { "type": "listTables" } | { "type": "subscribe", "table": "<name>" } | { "type": "unsubscribe", "table": "<name>" }
server → on subscribe:  { "type": "subscribed", "table", "keyField", "mode" }
                        { "type": "snapshot", "table", "rows": [ ... ] }          // always a JSON array
server → streaming:     { "type": "update", "table", "row": { ... } }             // single row…
                        { "type": "update", "table", "rows": [ ... ] }            // …or a batch
server → on problems:   { "type": "error", "message" }
```

Error posture: non-JSON frames, valid-JSON non-objects (`null`, `42`, arrays — explicitly guarded), unknown `type`, and unknown table names all produce `{ type: 'error', message }` without dropping the connection. Close/error removes the socket from every subscriber set.

Catalog (`TABLES` in `prism-table-hub.ts` — `mode` is the suggested blotter mode):

| `name` | `mode` | `keyField` | Tick cadence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `ust_trades` | `append` | `tradeId` | one print every 600–1800 ms (`TRADE_MIN_GAP_MS`/`TRADE_MAX_GAP_MS`, re-randomized per print) |
| `ust_market_data` | `snapshot-update` | `symbol` | every 500 ms (`MARKET_DATA_TICK_MS`) |
| `irs_risk_pnl` | `snapshot-update` | `tradeId` | every 1000 ms (`IRS_RISK_TICK_MS`) |

Tickers are **global and unconditional** — one engine instance per table, ticked even with zero WS subscribers, so every subscriber sees identical state and REST snapshots keep evolving. Batching: market-data ticks update 1–4 instruments and are sent as single-`row` frames, except a 25% chance of one `rows` batch when >1 changed; IRS reprices are always one `rows` batch (a curve-point move hits a set of positions at once); trades are a single `row` ~80% of the time, else a burst `rows` array of 2–4.

REST mirror (snapshot-only, CORS `*`, consumed by the blotters' `RestSnapshotClient`):

- `GET http://localhost:3000/prism/tables` → `{ "tables": [ ...same catalog... ] }`
- `GET http://localhost:3000/prism/tables/<name>` → **bare JSON array** of rows; `404` for unknown table names, `400` for malformed percent-encoding, `405` for non-GET, `OPTIONS` → `204`.

#### Table `ust_market_data` (keyed by `symbol`)

Universe of 15 US-rates instruments shared with `ust_trades`: 7 cash on-the-run Treasuries (`UST 2Y` … `UST 30Y`, real-format CUSIPs, coupons, maturities out to 2056) + 8 CME Treasury futures for Sep-2026 (`ZTU6 Z3NU6 ZFU6 ZNU6 TNU6 TWEU6 ZBU6 UBU6`). Tick sizes follow contract spec: cash trades in 1/8 of a 32nd; ZT/Z3N/ZF quarter-32nds; ZN/TN half-32nds; TWE/ZB/UB full 32nds — all prices are rounded to instrument tick.

`UstMarketDataRow` (from `prism-tables.service.ts`): `symbol, instrumentType: 'CASH'|'FUTURE', tenor, description, cusip (cash only, else null), exchange (futures only), coupon, maturity, bid, ask, mid, last, bidDisplay, askDisplay, bidSize, askSize, yield, change, changePercent, volume, openInterest (futures only), updated`. Both tables emit a **homogeneous field set** (nulls where not applicable) so the blotter's first-record column inference sees every column. `bidDisplay`/`askDisplay` are dealer 32nds strings via exported `to32nds(price)` — handle, dash, two-digit 32nds, eighths as trailing digit with `+` for a half (e.g. `99.515625` → `"99-16+"`); rounding overflow rolls to the next handle (`"100-00"`). `yield`, `coupon`, and `changePercent` are emitted as **fractions** (0.0397 = 3.97%) because the blotter's inferred `percent` format multiplies by 100. Sizes/volume: $MM for cash, contracts for futures. `tick()` moves 1–4 random instruments per 500 ms with a gentle mean-reversion drift toward `basePrice` (`(base − mid) × 0.02` per tick); spread is 1–2 ticks wide.

#### Table `ust_trades` (append, key `tradeId`)

`UstTradeRow`: `tradeId ("T-100000"+seq), time, symbol, instrumentType, tenor, cusip, side: 'BUY'|'SELL', price, priceDisplay (32nds), size, sizeUnit: 'MM'|'CT', yield (cash only, fraction), venue, counterparty`. Prints land within ±2 ticks of the market-data engine's current mid (`UstMarketDataTable.midOf()` — the two tables are coupled). Venues: cash → `BrokerTec` / `Dealerweb` / `Fenics UST`; futures → `CME Globex`. Counterparties from a fixed 10-name list (`JPM`, `GS`, …, `JANE ST`, `SIG`). History is bounded at 200 rows and pre-seeded with 40 so a late subscriber's snapshot is non-empty.

#### Table `irs_risk_pnl` (keyed by `tradeId`) — the IRS desk simulation

`IrsRiskPnlTable` in `irs-risk-pnl.service.ts`. One row per live vanilla OIS position (SOFR / ESTR / SONIA / TONA — `INDEX_BY_CCY`), booked into a fixed **desk → book → trader** hierarchy (`STRUCTURE`): `USD Swaps` (books `SOFR Outright`, `SOFR Curve`, `UST Swap Spreads`), `EUR Swaps` (`ESTR Outright`, `EUR Basis`), `Sterling & Yen` (`SONIA Outright`, `TONA Outright`) — 11 traders total, 3–5 swaps each minted at startup (≈33–55 rows). Tenors `2Y…30Y` (8 points); clearing house drawn per currency (`LCH SwapClear`/`CME`/`EUREX`/`JSCC`); `strategy` derived from the book name (Curve / Basis / Outright).

`IrsRiskPnlRow` fields: identity (`tradeId "IRS-700000"+`, `desk`, `book`, `trader`, `strategy`, `product: 'IRS OIS'`, `ccy`, `index`, `tenor`, `payReceive: 'PAY'|'RCV'`, `counterparty`, `clearingHouse`, `effectiveDate`, `maturityDate`), economics (`notional` in trade ccy, `notionalUsd`, `fixedRate` and `parRate` as fractions), risk in USD (`npv`, `dv01`, `gamma`, `kr01_2y`, `kr01_5y`, `kr01_10y`, `kr01_30y`), P&L in USD (`dayPnl`, `mtdPnl`, `ytdPnl`, `carryPnl`, `rollDownPnl`, `curvePnl`, `newTradePnl`, `feesPnl`, `residualPnl`), `updated`.

Invariants the sim maintains (and that downstream roll-ups rely on):

- **P&L explain ties out**: `dayPnl === carryPnl + rollDownPnl + curvePnl + newTradePnl + feesPnl + residualPnl` at mint, after every reprice, and after day roll (all six zeroed).
- **Bond-style DV01 sign**: receive-fixed = long duration = positive `dv01` (pay-fixed negative). `dv01 ≈ notionalUsd × tenorYears × 0.92 × 0.0001`.
- **KR01 buckets sum to `dv01`**: 88–96% in the tenor's own key-rate bucket (2Y for ≤3y, 5Y for ≤7y, 10Y for ≤15y, else 30Y), the exact remainder in ONE adjacent bucket only — key-rate bumps are local, so a 2Y swap never carries 30Y sensitivity.
- **Cross-currency aggregation**: all risk and P&L fields are USD; `notionalUsd = notional × FX_TO_USD` (fixed rates: EUR 1.09, GBP 1.27, JPY 0.0064; JPY notionals are minted ×150 larger). `notional` stays in trade currency and must **never be summed across currencies** — `notionalUsd` is the aggregatable field (the seeded blotter roll-ups give `notional` the `"none"` aggregation).

Engine dynamics: live par curves are a `Map` keyed `"${ccy}:${tenor}"` seeded from `BASE_CURVES` (rough mid-2026 levels, e.g. USD 10Y 3.81%, JPY 2Y 0.81%). Each 1 s `tick()` moves 1–2 randomly-picked curve points by ±0.4 bp plus a mean-reversion drift toward base (`(base − current) × 0.02`); deltas are **accumulated in a Map** because `pick()` samples with replacement — repricing must see the NET move of a twice-picked point. Every position on a moved `(ccy, tenor)` point is repriced: `curveMove = −Δbp × dv01 + 0.5 × gamma × Δbp²`; carry accrues as `direction × (fixedRate − overnight) × notionalUsd × 1e-4 × rand(0.6,1.4)` where the compounded-overnight proxy is that currency's live 2Y par minus 15 bp and `direction` is +1 for RCV / −1 for PAY (a flat pay/receive book carries flat, not uniformly positive); roll-down accrues `dv01 × rand(0, 0.0015)`; residual is ±1% of the curve move; `dayPnl` is recomputed from the six components and `npv`/`mtdPnl`/`ytdPnl` each drift by the day-P&L increment. **Day roll**: `DAY_ROLL_TICKS = 1200` ticks at 1 s ≈ a ~20-minute simulated trading day — on roll, every position's six day components and `dayPnl` are zeroed (MTD/YTD keep their accumulated values) and ALL rows are returned as one batch update, keeping `dayPnl` bounded on long-running demos. At mint, `npv` is set off-market by `(par − fixed)` bp × DV01 with receiver-gains-when-par<fixed sign, ~20% of positions are flagged new-today (positive `newTradePnl`, negative `feesPnl`; both 0 otherwise, and `feesPnl` is always ≤ 0).

### Workspace Storage API — `http://localhost:3000/workspace/v1`

`WorkspaceStorageApi` is the **phase-1 reference implementation** of the contract in `docs/api/workspace-storage-api.openapi.yaml`, consumed by `RestWorkspaceStorageClient` in `@macro/openfin`; phase 2 replaces it with Java Spring Boot + MongoDB speaking the same wire contract. Conventions (fixed — the client already implements them):

- **User scoping**: every user-scoped route reads the `X-User-Id` request header, defaulting to `anonymous` (interim scheme; phase 2 = OAuth2/JWT). Per-user state is an in-memory `UserStore` (`workspaces`/`pages`/`dock` Maps, `favorites: string[]`, `preferences` Map). `MAX_USERS = 500`: since `X-User-Id` is unauthenticated, the user map is capped — on overflow the **oldest-inserted** user is evicted with a console warning.
- **Errors** are RFC 9457 `application/problem+json`: `{ "type": "about:blank", "title", "status", "detail" }`.
- **ETag/If-Match**: single-resource GETs (and PUT responses) carry a strong ETag = quoted SHA-1 of the resource's JSON GET representation. `If-Match` on PUT/DELETE is optional; mismatch → `412`; per RFC 9110, `If-Match` (including `*`) on a resource with no current representation also → `412`; comma-separated tag lists are accepted.
- **PUT semantics**: `201` when created, `200` when replaced; the body's id field must equal the path id or → `400`. GET of a missing single resource → `404` (client maps to `undefined`); DELETE of a missing resource → `404` (client treats as success). Workspace snapshots / page layouts / dock configs are **opaque JSON** — the server never inspects them.
- **CORS** wide open on every response including errors: `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`, methods `GET,PUT,DELETE,OPTIONS`, allowed headers `Content-Type,X-User-Id,If-Match`, exposed header `ETag`; `OPTIONS` → `204`.
- **Bodies**: JSON, capped at 5 MB (`413`, remaining body drained); invalid JSON → `400`. A `BODY_HANDLED` sentinel Symbol signals "error response already sent" to route handlers. Malformed percent-encoding anywhere in the path → `400` rather than a crash.

Routes:

| Route | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| `GET /health` | `{ status:'ok', service:'workspace-storage-api', uptimeSec, users, dockApps, storeApps, persistedTo }` — no user scope |
| `GET /workspaces?query=` | List; optional case-insensitive substring filter on `title` OR `workspaceId` |
| `GET/PUT/DELETE /workspaces/{id}` | Single-document semantics above; id field `workspaceId` |
| `GET /pages`, `GET/PUT/DELETE /pages/{id}` | Same; id field `pageId` |
| `GET/PUT/DELETE /dock/{dockProviderId}` | Same; id field `id` |
| `GET /favorites` | `{ appIds: string[] }` — **never 404**; unsaved = empty list (with ETag) |
| `PUT /favorites` | Body must be `{ "appIds": string[] }` (else `400`); honors If-Match; → `200` |
| `GET /preferences` | All prefs as `[ { key, value } ]` |
| `GET/PUT/DELETE /preferences/{key}` | GET representation (and ETag basis) is `{ key, value }`; PUT body `{ "value": <any JSON> }` → `201`/`200` |
| `GET /config/{name}` | Platform config passthrough, **not user-scoped**; names: `settings`, `apps`, `dock-config`, `storefront-config`, `snap-config`, `entitlements` |
| `PUT /config/{name}` | `501 Not Implemented` — admin operation reserved for phase 2 |
| `GET /dock-apps`, `GET/PUT/DELETE /dock-apps/{id}` | Shared LOB dock-app registry (below); id field `id` |
| `GET /store-apps`, `GET/PUT/DELETE /store-apps/{appId}` | Shared LOB store-app registry (below); id field `appId` |

`/config/{name}` reads the file **fresh from disk per request** out of `WORKSPACE_CONFIG_DIR` (env var; default `<cwd>/apps/macro-workspace/public/local`): unknown name or missing file → `404`, file that fails `JSON.parse` → `500`, success → raw file text with an ETag of the text. The name→file lookup uses `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty` so inherited keys like `constructor`/`__proto__` cannot bypass the 404 guard.

`/dock-apps` and `/store-apps` are **NOT user-scoped** — publisher-facing registries shared across all users like `/config` (`X-User-Id` ignored; LOBs PUT, the platform GETs at dock/storefront init). List GETs never 404 (empty array when none) and sort by `sortOrder` ascending with undefined-`sortOrder` entries last; the comparator returns 0 on ties so `Array.sort` stability preserves insertion order. PUT bodies are **strictly validated** against the `LobDockApp` / `LobStoreApp` contracts (`validateLobDockApp` / `validateLobStoreApp`) with `additionalProperties: false` semantics — any unknown field, at top level or nested, → `400` with a human-readable detail, because these shared unauthenticated resources feed every user's dock/storefront:

- **Dock app**: `id`/`label`/`iconUrl` required non-empty strings; `type` must be `"icon"` (then `url` required) or `"dropdown"` (then `children` a non-empty array, each child requiring `id`/`label`/`url`, optional `iconUrl`); optional `tooltip`/`lob` non-empty strings, `sortOrder` a finite number. Allowed keys only: `id,label,iconUrl,type,url,children,tooltip,lob,sortOrder` (children: `id,label,url,iconUrl`).
- **Store app**: `appId`/`title`/`manifest` required non-empty; `manifestType` ∈ `"view" | "manifest"`; `icons` a non-empty array of exactly-`{ src: string }` objects; optional `description`/`publisher`/`contactEmail`/`supportEmail`/`category`/`lob` non-empty strings, `sortOrder` finite number, `tags` array of non-empty strings, `images` array of `{ src }`.

**Persistence**: the whole store (per-user maps + the two shared registries) lives in memory and is persisted to `WORKSPACE_STORE_FILE` (env var; default `<cwd>/.workspace-store.json`, gitignored) as `{ version: 1, savedAt, dockApps, storeApps, users }`. Writes are debounced 500 ms (`SAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS`) and atomic (write `<file>.tmp`, then `renameSync` over the target); a pending dirty save is flushed on `process.on('exit')` so Ctrl-C loses nothing. On startup: missing file → warn + start empty; corrupt file → warn + start empty (all maps cleared); older store files without `dockApps`/`storeApps` keys are tolerated. Serialization writes users into a **null-prototype object** so a user id of `"__proto__"` or `"constructor"` round-trips as data instead of touching the prototype. A failed persist only logs — in-memory state stays authoritative (demo-grade posture; durability is a phase-2 concern).

Postman coverage: `docs/api/workspace-storage-api.postman_collection.json` exercises every endpoint plus scenario flows (ETag/If-Match 412, per-user isolation) with per-request assertions; run top-to-bottom (`npx newman run …`) since later requests reuse captured ETags. Variables: `baseUrl`, `userId`.

---

## MCP Servers: macro-mcp, macro-mcp-agent & @macro/mcp-core

Custom Model Context Protocol servers that let AI coding agents scaffold apps, explore live AMPS data, import Figma Make exports, and query `@macro/*` library APIs. All tools/resources/prompts live in one shared library, `@macro/mcp-core` (`libs/mcp-core/src`, path alias `"@macro/mcp-core": ["libs/mcp-core/src/index.ts"]` in `tsconfig.base.json`), consumed by two thin transport entrypoints so the surfaces can never drift:

- `apps/macro-mcp` — **stdio** transport, registered in the repo's `.mcp.json`
- `apps/macro-mcp-agent` — **HTTP (Streamable) + legacy SSE** transport for remote deployment

`libs/mcp-core/src/index.ts` exports exactly three functions: `registerTools(server: McpServer)`, `registerResources(server)`, `registerPrompts(server)`. Both entrypoints call all three against a `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` `McpServer` (SDK `^1.26.0`; tool/prompt param schemas use `zod`).

### Entrypoints & build

**`apps/macro-mcp/src/main.ts`** (~30 lines): creates `McpServer({ name: 'macro-mcp', version: '1.0.0' })` with `resources/tools/prompts` capabilities, registers everything, connects a `StdioServerTransport`. Fatal errors log to stderr and `process.exit(1)`.

**`apps/macro-mcp-agent/src/main.ts`**: Express 5 app on `MCP_HOST` (default `0.0.0.0`) / `MCP_PORT` (default `3100`), CORS `*` (exposes `Mcp-Session-Id`), request logging. Endpoints:

- `GET /health` → `{ status: 'ok', name: 'macro-mcp-agent', version, uptime }`
- `ALL /mcp` — stateless Streamable HTTP (`StreamableHTTPServerTransport` with `sessionIdGenerator: undefined`); a single server instance is reused, and on an `Already connected` error a fresh `McpServer` is created per request (fallback path)
- `GET /sse` + `POST /messages?sessionId=` — legacy `SSEServerTransport` sessions kept in an in-memory map, cleaned up on response close; unknown session → HTTP 400 `{ error: 'Invalid session' }`

Both apps build with `@nx/esbuild:esbuild` (`platform: node`, `format: ['cjs']`, `bundle: true`, `generatePackageJson: true`) into `dist/apps/<name>/main.js`. **Build is required before first use**: `npm run build:mcp` (`nx build macro-mcp`). `.mcp.json` wires the stdio server as:

```json
"macro-mcp": { "command": "node", "args": ["dist/apps/macro-mcp/main.js"] }
```

The agent build marks `amps` as `external` and `npm run build:mcp-agent` post-patches the generated `dist/apps/macro-mcp-agent/package.json` to pin the `amps` dependency version (esbuild cannot detect it — see below). `npm run start:mcp-agent` = `nx serve macro-mcp-agent`. Both builds are part of CI via `build:apps:ci`.

The `amps` npm client is loaded lazily in `libs/mcp-core/src/lib/tools/amps-explore.ts` via a dynamic `_require(mod)` with a variable module name, deliberately hidden from esbuild's static analysis, so neither bundle embeds it; at runtime `require('amps')` resolves from `node_modules` (works when run from the repo root; the agent's patched package.json covers standalone deploys).

### Tools (11) — `libs/mcp-core/src/lib/tools/`

Registered by `registerTools` in `tools/index.ts`. Three are **pure text generators** (no filesystem writes): `scaffold_angular_app`, `scaffold_react_app`, `scaffold_library`. Three are **filesystem writers**: `register_openfin_app`, `import_figma_app`, `amps_create_mfe`. The rest are read-only lookups (`list_libraries`, `get_library_api`, `get_commands`, `list_icons`) plus the live-data explorer `amps_explore`.

| Tool | Params (z-schema) | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `scaffold_angular_app` | `appName`, `description`, `port?` (default 4204), `features?: ('ag-grid'\|'ag-charts'\|'amps'\|'solace'\|'nats'\|'openfin'\|'conflation')[]` | Returns a step-by-step markdown guide (NX generate command, `main.ts`/`app.config.ts`/`app.routes.ts`/`app.ts`/`styles.css` code) mirroring `apps/macro-angular`: zoneless, standalone, PrimeNG Aura, `ThemeService` from `@macro/macro-design/angular`, 3-file CSS chain. Feature flags append AG Grid / AMPS / Solace / NATS / conflation wiring snippets and a pointer to `register_openfin_app` |
| `scaffold_react_app` | same params | Same idea mirroring `apps/macro-react`: React 19 + Vite, `themeController.start()` before `createRoot`, `useTheme()`, Tailwind 4 `@theme inline` bridge, full `vite.config.ts` with `@macro/*` aliases (longer subpath keys before parents) |
| `scaffold_library` | `libName`, `description`, `type?: 'ts'\|'angular'\|'react'` (default `ts`) | Markdown guide: NX generator command per type, `tsconfig.base.json` alias step, `index.ts` public-API stub, per-type conventions |
| `list_libraries` | — | JSON array of 7 hard-coded `@macro/*` library records (`name`, `importPath`, `description`, `keyExports`) |
| `get_library_api` | `library: enum('logger','transports','nats','amps','solace','utils','openfin','macro-design','macro-angular-grid','macro-react-grid')` | Full hand-written API doc for the library; `amps`/`solace`/`nats` alias to the unified `transports` doc |
| `get_commands` | `category?: 'dev'\|'build'\|'test'\|'publish'\|'nx'` | Markdown tables of npm/NX commands, optionally filtered |
| `list_icons` | `category?`, `search?` | Lists the Capital Markets icon set (dark+light SVG variants) from `apps/macro-workspace/public/icons/capital-markets/index.json` (fallback: scan `dark/`), grouped into 10 hard-coded categories plus an exhaustive "Other" bucket; prints the served URLs `http://localhost:<wsPort>/icons/capital-markets/{dark,light}/{name}.svg` |
| `register_openfin_app` | `appId`, `title`, `description`, `port`, `route?`, `framework: 'angular'\|'react'`, `icon?`, `tags?` | Writes OpenFin registration files (below). Validates kebab-case `appId` (`/^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$/`), resolves icon against the Capital Markets set (default `dashboard`, warns-but-uses unknown names), appends a per-framework FDC3 `ContextService` snippet to the response. Idempotent |
| `import_figma_app` | `appName`, `title=''`, `description=''`, `port?`, `sourcePath` (folder or `.zip`), `icon?`, `dryRun=false`, `preserveFigmaTheme=false`, `verifyBuild=false` | Full Figma Make → workspace import pipeline (below) |
| `amps_explore` | `ampsUrl?`, `topic?`, `filter?`, `configPath?` (file path or HTTP URL), `sampleSize?=10`, `timeout?=5000` | Connects to AMPS, optionally SOWs a topic, returns schema/keys/sample-data analysis (below) |
| `amps_create_mfe` | `ampsUrl?`, `topic`, `framework`, `appName`, `title`, `port`, `description?`, `filter?`, `compositeKey?` (e.g. `"/MarketId+/Id"`), `conflationMs?=100`, `configUrl?` | Introspects AMPS then generates a complete Angular or React app (files written directly) with auto-generated `ColDef`s, `sowAndSubscribe` + `ConflationSubject` + grid wiring, and OpenFin registration |

#### OpenFin registration helpers — `tools/openfin-registration.ts`

Shared by `register_openfin_app` and `import_figma_app` (`registerWorkspaceApp(root, opts: RegisterWorkspaceAppOptions)`), so their registration shape cannot drift. `findWorkspaceRoot()` walks up from `__dirname` (max 10 levels) looking for a `package.json` with `name === '@macro/source'`; throws if not found. `getWorkspacePort(root)` reads `apps/macro-workspace/project.json` serve port (fallback 4202). `registerWorkspaceApp` writes, for **both** `apps/macro-workspace/public/{local,openshift}/`:

1. View manifests `<env>/<appId>.fin.json` — `{ url, fdc3InteropApi: '2.0', interop: { currentContextGroup: 'green' } }` (openshift URL uses an `{{OPENSHIFT_<APPID>_HOST}}` token)
2. App entries appended to `manifest.fin.json` + `settings.json` under `customSettings.apps` (idempotent by `appId`)
3. Dock favorites appended to `settings.json` under `customSettings.dock3.favorites` — icon deliberately a plain string URL (code comment: the `{dark,light}` object variant hangs the dock)

**Staleness gotcha (verified against current config):** since the PR #78/#80 registry split, the per-env `manifest.fin.json`/`settings.json` no longer contain `customSettings` at all — the app registry moved to `apps.json` and the dock to `dock-config.json`. `addAppToJsonFile`/`addDockFavorite` only push when a `customSettings.apps`/`customSettings.dock3.favorites` array already exists, so steps 2–3 currently **silently no-op**; only the view manifests are actually written. These tools have not been updated to target `apps.json`/`dock-config.json`.

`tools/mcp-utils.ts` contains near-duplicate `findWorkspaceRoot`/`addAppToJsonFile`/`addDockFavorite` (used only by `amps_create_mfe`; its variants rewrite the file unconditionally and return `void`), plus `toHeaderName(field)` — camelCase→"Header Name" with an acronym whitelist (`id, cusip, isin, url, uuid, pnl, fx, ir, dv01, cds, etf` → uppercased).

#### `import_figma_app` pipeline — `tools/import-figma-app.ts` (~1170 lines)

1. Validates kebab-case name, rejects existing `apps/<name>`, missing `sourcePath`
2. Auto-detects a free port from 4204 upward by scanning all apps' `vite.config.*` (`port:` regex) and `project.json` serve targets, always reserving 4200–4203, 3000, and the workspace port
3. Extracts `.zip` via `unzip` → PowerShell `Expand-Archive` → `python3`/`python zipfile` (first that works; nested single-root folders unwrapped; `__MACOSX` skipped) into `<root>/tmp/figma-<app>-<ts>`
4. Rejects non-React exports up front: `angular.json` → "use scaffold_angular_app"; `next.config.*` → "eject to Vite first"; zero `.tsx/.jsx` files → error
5. Auto-detects title/description from the export's `package.json`, filtering generic Figma placeholders (`@Figma/...`, "My Make File", etc.); auto-suggests an icon from a large keyword→icon map (longest keyword match wins, fallback `dashboard`), auto-copying icons from `CapitalMarketsIcons/{dark,light}/svg` into the workspace public folder if missing
6. `dryRun: true` reports the plan (port, icon, root component from `ROOT_CANDIDATES` like `app/App.tsx`/`App.tsx`/`pages/index.tsx`, CSS imports, Tailwind detection, new deps, macro-design token conflicts) and stops before any write
7. Writes `project.json` (Vite build/serve/preview targets), tsconfigs, `eslint.config.mjs` (ignores generated `src/figma/**`); copies the Figma `src/` into `src/figma/` (renaming its own `main.tsx`/`index.tsx` to `*.figma-original`), `public/` into `src/assets/`
8. Rewrites import specifiers Vite can't resolve: strips `pkg@x.y.z` version suffixes and rewrites `figma:asset/<file>` → `@/assets/<file>`
9. Installs only genuinely-new deps (`npm install <bare names> --legacy-peer-deps`, skipping the root package.json's deps/devDeps and a `FIGMA_TOOLCHAIN` set); failure downgrades to a warning step
10. Generates `src/app/app.tsx` (BrowserRouter `basename="/<app>/"` + `useTheme()`) and `src/main.tsx` (`themeController.start()`; macro CSS imported **after** Figma CSS by default so macro tokens win the cascade — `preserveFigmaTheme: true` reverses the order), `vite.config.mts`, `index.html`
11. Calls `registerWorkspaceApp` (tags `['view','react','figma-make']`), adds `start:<app>`/`build:<app>` scripts to the root `package.json`
12. `verifyBuild: true` runs `npx nx build <app> --skip-nx-cache` and reports pass/fail with the log tail

Error posture: any thrown error removes the temp extraction dir, the partial `apps/<name>` directory, and partially-written `.fin.json` manifests, so re-runs can reuse the name.

#### `amps_explore` — `tools/amps-explore.ts`

Exports `exploreAmps(ampsUrl, topic?, filter?, configPath?, sampleSize=10, timeout=5000): Promise<AmpsExploreResult>` plus types `AmpsSchemaField { field; type: 'string'|'number'|'boolean'|'object'|'array'; nullable; sampleValues; decimals }`, `AmpsTopicInfo { name; keys; compositeKey; messageType }`, `AmpsTransportInfo`. Behavior:

- `configPath` (local file or HTTP/HTTPS URL, redirects followed, self-signed certs accepted) is regex-parsed as AMPS XML: instance `<Name>`, `<Transport>` blocks (name/type/port/protocol), and `<SOW><Topic>` blocks with multi-`<Key>` composite keys. A `websocket` transport auto-derives `detectedWsUrl = ws://<host>:<port>/amps/json`
- With a `topic`: connects (`wss://` temporarily sets `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0`, restored afterwards even on failure), runs `sowAndSubscribe`, collects rows until `group_end` or timeout, then analyzes: per-field type inference, nullability, max decimal places, up to 3 unique sample values; key detection prefers the config's composite key, else common key names (`id`, `symbol`, `cusip`, `isin`, `tradeId`, …) or fields whose values are unique across the sample
- Without `topic`: config-only mode, no connection. Errors are returned in the `error` field of the result (never thrown); the tool renders everything as a markdown report with a schema table and sample JSON

#### `amps_create_mfe` — `tools/amps-create-mfe.ts`

Calls `exploreAmps` (20 samples, 8 s timeout) to derive the schema/keys, then writes a complete app: attempts the NX generator first (`--e2eTestRunner=none --skipFormat`) but proceeds manually if it fails, always overwriting with its own generated files. Generated column defs: first string field pinned left; numeric fields right-aligned with `toFixed(decimals)`; fields containing "change" get +/- formatting, green/red `cellStyle`, and absolute-value `sortingOrder`. Generated component uses `sowAndSubscribe` (SOW snapshot → `setInitialRowData`, then live updates through `ConflationSubject(conflationMs)` batched into `grid.updateRows$` on an interval). Angular output is zoneless with signals; React output uses `AmpsTransport` + refs. OpenFin registration here uses the older `mcp-utils` helpers and hard-codes `http://localhost:4202` + `icons/platform.svg` (same `customSettings` staleness caveat applies; view manifests are still written). Also patches root `package.json` scripts. Note: the generated code predates the theme-controller era (uses `applyDarkMode`/`onSystemThemeChange` directly) — functional but not the current recommended pattern.

### Resources (12) — `libs/mcp-core/src/lib/resources/`

All are static markdown docs (`mimeType: 'text/markdown'`) served under `macro://` URIs; each file holds one large template-literal doc:

| Resource name | URI | Content |
|---|---|---|
| `architecture` | `macro://architecture` | Monorepo layout, tech-stack table, data-flow, port map |
| `angular-template` | `macro://templates/angular` | Full Angular LOB app pattern |
| `react-template` | `macro://templates/react` | Full React LOB app pattern |
| `theming` | `macro://theming` | `@macro/macro-design` single-source-of-truth theming architecture |
| `data-connectivity` | `macro://data-connectivity` | Unified `@macro/transports` (AMPS/Solace/NATS) + WebSocket + conflation patterns |
| `openfin` | `macro://openfin` | OpenFin Workspace 24.0.19 integration, FDC3, manifests |
| `libraries` | `macro://libraries` | `@macro/*` library catalog |
| `market-data-server` | `macro://market-data-server` | Port-3000 WS server endpoints and message shapes |
| `testing` | `macro://testing` | Jest/Vitest split, commands |
| `tech-stack` | `macro://tech-stack` | Version matrix |
| `lob-guide` | `macro://lob-guide` | 7-step LOB team onboarding guide |
| `figma-workflow` | `macro://figma-workflow` | Figma Make → OpenFin import workflow narrative |

Some resource docs predate recent refactors (e.g. `architecture` still lists only 7 apps and pre-Top-N Prism state) — they are conventions docs, not generated from source.

### Prompts (6) — `libs/mcp-core/src/lib/prompts/`

Each returns a single user-role message containing generated instructions/code:

- `create-angular-app` / `create-react-app` — args `appName`, `businessDomain`, `dataSource?: 'amps'|'solace'|'websocket'|'rest'`; complete new-LOB-app walkthrough (assumes port 4204)
- `add-grid-component` — args `framework: 'angular'|'react'`, `componentName` (PascalCase), `dataFields` (comma-separated); emits ColDefs + `getRowId` + `updateRows$` wiring
- `add-fdc3-context` — args `framework`, `contextType` (e.g. `fdc3.instrument`), `componentName`; broadcast/listen code via `ContextService`
- `add-theme-support` — args `framework`, `componentName`; `ThemeService`/`useTheme` + 3-file CSS chain
- `add-data-connectivity` — args `framework`, `transport: 'amps'|'solace'|'nats'|'websocket'`, `topic`, `componentName`; transport + conflation + grid wiring

### Build/consumption notes

- `libs/mcp-core/project.json` declares no targets — the lib is consumed **from source** through the esbuild bundling of the two apps; its `package.json` (`@macro/mcp-core`, private, `main: ./src/index.ts`) exists only for editor resolution
- `apps/macro-mcp/tsconfig.app.json` compiles CJS (`module: commonjs`); the `.js` extensions on relative imports inside `mcp-core` are ESM-style but resolved fine by esbuild/TS
- `apps/macro-mcp/README.md` documents the stdio server; its `.mcp.json` example shows an absolute Windows path, but the actual repo `.mcp.json` uses the relative `dist/apps/macro-mcp/main.js` (resolved against the MCP client's cwd, i.e. the repo root)

---

## Lib: @macro/openfin — platform services (non-storage)

Framework-agnostic OpenFin Workspace platform services with an Angular DI layer on top and two React hooks. Source: `libs/openfin/src`. Path aliases (in `tsconfig.base.json`): `@macro/openfin` → `libs/openfin/src/index.ts`, `@macro/openfin/react` → `libs/openfin/src/lib/react.ts`, `@macro/openfin/theme-sync` → `libs/openfin/src/lib/theme-sync.ts`. Storage internals (`lib/storage/` — `WorkspaceStorageClient`, local/REST clients, storage context) are covered in their own section; this section covers everything else, including how services consume storage.

**Export pattern** (`src/index.ts`): every framework-agnostic class is re-exported with a `Base` prefix (`BaseWorkspaceService`, `BaseStoreService`, `BaseDock3Service`, …); the un-prefixed names (`WorkspaceService`, `StoreService`, …) are the Angular `@Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' })` wrappers from `lib/angular/index.ts` (re-exported via `export * from './lib/angular'`). Also exported: `launchApp`, `onOpenFinThemeChange`, `buildMacroGranularTheme`, `resolveEnvConfigPath`, `hydrateViewTitles`/`setViewTitle`/`getViewTitles`, `LocalStorageFavoritesStore`, `FavoritesStore` (type), plus `themeConfig`/`ThemePalette`/`ThemeConfig` re-exports from `@macro/macro-design` and the storage-API surface.

**Browser no-op posture**: every service guards `typeof fin === 'undefined'` (or `fin.me?.isOpenFin`, or `globalThis.fdc3` for FDC3) and degrades gracefully outside the OpenFin runtime — no-op writes, empty observables, log-and-continue. `@openfin/workspace/notifications` is dynamically `import()`ed because it reads `fin.me.uuid` at module-import time and would crash a plain browser tab.

### Core types (`lib/types.ts`)

```ts
export type MacroApp = App & { category?: string };   // App = @openfin/workspace App
export interface AppsConfig { apps: App[] }            // shape of apps.json

export type Dock3Icon = string | { light: string; dark: string };
export type Dock3FavoriteEntry =
  | { type: 'item'; id: string; icon: Dock3Icon; label: string; appId?: string }
  | { type: 'folder'; id: string; label: string; icon?: Dock3Icon };  // folder = dock DROPDOWN
export interface Dock3ContentFolder { type: 'folder'; id: string; label: string; children: Dock3ContentEntry[] }
export interface Dock3ContentItem { type: 'item'; id: string; icon: Dock3Icon; label: string; appId?: string }
export interface Dock3Settings { favorites?: Dock3FavoriteEntry[]; contentMenu?: Dock3ContentEntry[] } // dock-config.json

export interface PlatformSettings { id: string; title: string; icon: string }
export interface BrowserSettings {          // settings.json → browserSettings (Workspace v24 options)
  allowDuplicatePageTitles?: boolean;
  indicators?: { suppressWorkspaceSwitched?: boolean; suppressWorkspaceSaved?: boolean };
  tabSearchButton?: { pageTabs?: { enabled: boolean }; viewTabs?: { enabled: boolean } };
}
export interface SettingsResponse { platformSettings: PlatformSettings; browserSettings?: BrowserSettings; storage?: StorageSettings }

export interface StorefrontNavItemConfig { id: string; title: string; category?: string; tags?: string[] } // '*' = all apps
export interface StorefrontNavSectionConfig { id: string; title: string; items: StorefrontNavItemConfig[] }
export interface StorefrontNavigationConfig { favoritesTitle?: string; sections: StorefrontNavSectionConfig[] }
export interface StorefrontRowConfig { title: string; appIds?: string[]; category?: string }
export interface StorefrontConfig {
  navigation: StorefrontNavigationConfig;
  landingPage?: { hero?: {...}; topRow?: StorefrontRowConfig; middleRow?: ...; bottomRow?: ... };
  footer?: { logo?: {...}; text?: string; links?: { title: string; url: string }[] };
  cardClickBehavior?: 'show-app-details' | 'perform-primary-button-action';
}

export interface User { id: string; name: string; groups?: string[]; entitlements: string[] }
export interface EntitlementsConfig { currentUser: User; apps: Record<string, string[]> } // appId → any-of entitlements
```

### WorkspaceService (`lib/workspace.service.ts`)

Orchestrates the full platform boot. `init()` returns `Observable<boolean>` and emits status strings on `getStatus$()`.

- Outside OpenFin (`fin.me?.isOpenFin` false): emits `'Not running inside OpenFin'`, still resolves the storage environment (so favorites/preferences behave consistently on the provider page in a plain browser), re-emits the status after (the provider UI refreshes its storage-env display on status emissions), returns `of(false)`.
- In OpenFin, sequential pipeline: load `settings.json` → `initWorkspaceStorage(settings.storage, { getUserId })` → hydrate theme preset + view titles → load active theme preset palettes → `PlatformService.initializeWorkspacePlatform(...)` → `awaitPlatformReady()` → `registerComponents()` → `showStartupComponents()` → `restoreLastSavedWorkspace()` → 500 ms delay → `PlatformService.updateToolbarButtons()` → status `'Platform initialized'`. Every stage publishes a lifecycle analytics event via `getAnalyticsNats()` (fire-and-forget, `.catch(() => {})`). Terminal `catchError` emits `Error: <message>` status and returns `of(false)`.
- `userIdSupplier()`: resolves `AuthService.getUser().id`, `'anonymous'` on any failure or when no AuthService injected.
- `awaitPlatformReady()`: listens for the one-shot `platform-api-ready` platform event but ALSO resolves after a 1000 ms `setTimeout` — the event may have already fired before subscription (WS 23.2.x resolves `init()` after it fires), and a missed event would otherwise silently prevent Dock/Store/Home registration.
- `registerComponents()`: `forkJoin` of dock (`Dock3Service.init`), home, store, notifications, snap — each wrapped in `guarded(label, source, ms = 12000)` which applies `timeout({ first: ms })` + `catchError` → `of(undefined)` with a warning naming the stalled component. Rationale (code comment): in WS 23.2.x several component promises resolve their UI but never settle.
- `showStartupComponents()`: fires `homeService.show()` and `storeService.show()` WITHOUT awaiting (guarded subscriptions) — `Storefront.show()`'s promise may never settle, and blocking would stall init.
- `restoreLastSavedWorkspace()`: does NOT use the platform's built-in `restoreLastSavedWorkspace()` (which restores the *currently active* workspace and always returns `'not-saved-workspace'` on fresh launch). Instead reads the self-tracked last-saved id from `WorkspaceStorageService.getLastSavedWorkspaceId()` (a well-known preference, written in the override's create/update hooks), loads that workspace, and `applyWorkspace(workspace, { skipPrompt: true, applySnapshotOptions: { closeExistingWindows: true } })`. A dangling id is cleared. All failures are non-fatal (status + analytics event only).
- `setAppLogUsername()`: after full init, reads env var `USERNAME` and calls `fin.Application.getCurrent().setAppLogUsername(username)` with 5 retries × 2 s.
- `quit()`: publishes `Quitting`, then `Promise.all([dock3Service.shutdown(), notificationsService.deregister(), snapService.stop()])`, then `Quit` analytics + NATS disconnect + `fin.Platform.getCurrentSync().quit()`.

### WorkspaceOverrideService (`lib/workspace-override.service.ts`)

`createOverrideCallback(): WorkspacePlatformOverrideCallback` returns a subclass of `WorkspacePlatformProvider` passed to `init({ overrideCallback })`. Constructor takes `(WorkspaceStorageService, SnapService)`. `setOnThemeChanged(cb)` registers a post-theme-change hook (used by PlatformService to swap toolbar icons).

Overridden provider methods:
- `handleAnalytics(req)`: logs each event + republishes to NATS, then `super.handleAnalytics`.
- **Workspace CRUD** → unified storage: `getSavedWorkspaces(query?)` (title/id substring filter, lowercased), `getSavedWorkspacesMetadata`, `getSavedWorkspace(id)`, `createSavedWorkspace`/`updateSavedWorkspace` (both: broadcast IAB topic `workspace:flush-view-state`, wait 200 ms, re-capture `platform.getSnapshot()` into the workspace so views persist freshest state; patch snapshot with custom view titles; save; set last-saved-workspace pointer), `deleteSavedWorkspace(id)` (also clears the last-saved pointer if it matches).
- **Page storage** → unified storage with one-time legacy migration: `getSavedPages()` reads storage; if empty, `migrateLegacyPagesOnce()` imports pages from the platform's DEFAULT storage (per-machine IndexedDB) — guarded per boot AND by a persisted `pages-migrated` preference read via the RAW storage client (which throws): an unreadable flag means migration state is UNKNOWN and importing could resurrect deleted pages, so the attempt aborts. `createSavedPage`/`updateSavedPage` call `markPagesMigrated()` so a later empty list never re-imports stale legacy pages over user intent. `getSavedPage(id)` falls back through `getSavedPages()` to cover the not-yet-migrated case.
- **Dock customization**: `getDockProviderConfig(id)` → storage, falling back to `super` (platform default storage) so a pre-migration customization still applies; `saveDockProviderConfig` → storage.
