- `applySnapshot(payload, identity?)`: extracts `customData.viewTitle` entries from the snapshot, runs `snapService.prepareToApplySnapshot` → `super.applySnapshot` → `snapService.applySnapshot`, then after 3 s matches titles to the NEW live view names by tree-walk position and re-applies them via `executeJavaScript('document.title = …')` + `setViewTitle`.
- `getSnapshot()`: patches the snapshot's golden-layout tree with live/stored view titles (OpenFin snapshots may miss runtime `updateOptions` customData), then `snapService.decorateSnapshot(snapshot)`.
- `setSelectedScheme(scheme)`: `super`, analytics, the registered theme-changed callback, then IAB broadcast on `THEME_CHANGED_TOPIC = 'workspace:theme-changed'` (exported constant) with `{ isDark }`.
- `createView(payload, callerIdentity)`: restores `customData.viewTitle` on recreated views (retries `document.title` set at `page-title-updated`+50 ms, 1 s, 3 s).
- `openViewTabContextMenu`: prepends a "Rename View" item (custom action id `rename-view`) + separator to the tab context menu.

**View titles side-channel** (module-level, exported): titles are read synchronously deep inside snapshot capture, so they live in an in-memory cache backed by the storage preference `WELL_KNOWN_PREFERENCES.viewTitles`, with legacy localStorage key `macro:view-titles` as the synchronous fallback for never-hydrated hosts. `hydrateViewTitles()` (called during boot): on failure seeds the cache EMPTY, not from the legacy key — in REST mode legacy titles belong to a different backend and persisting a legacy-seeded map would overwrite the environment's titles; the failure is remembered so the next `persistViewTitles()` merge-reads the backend first. `setViewTitle(viewName, title)` writes through fire-and-forget; `getViewTitles()` returns the cache (seeding from legacy localStorage if null).

### PlatformService (`lib/platform.service.ts`)

`initializeWorkspacePlatform(platformSettings, themePalettes?, additionalCustomActions?, browserSettings?): Observable<void>` — calls `@openfin/workspace-platform` `init()` with:
- `overrideCallback` from WorkspaceOverrideService.
- `browser`: `allowDuplicatePageTitles` (default false), `indicators` passthrough, `defaultWindowOptions` with `icon`, `taskbarIcon: toTaskbarIcon(icon)`, toolbar buttons, `navigationButtons: { enabled: true }`, `viewTabDimensions: { minWidth: '100px', maxWidth: '240px' }`, `pageTabDimensions: { minWidth: '80px', maxWidth: '200px' }`, `tabSearchButton` defaulting BOTH tab strips enabled.
- `theme: [buildMacroGranularTheme(dark, light)]` — HERE Core UI granular token theme (see below); palettes come from ThemePresetService or fall back to compiled `themeConfig`.
- `customActions`: merges `additionalCustomActions` (the StoreService card actions) with: `launch-app` (dock dropdown/custom button → `LaunchService.launch`), `toggle-page-tabs` (flips `disableMultiplePages` on all browser windows + swaps icon), `toggle-theme` (flips `Theme.setSelectedScheme`), `upload-logs` (`fin.System.launchLogUploader({ endpoint: 'http://MontyUbuntu2604:8000', logs: ['debug:self','app','rvm'], ui: { show: true } })`), `rename-view` (opens popup `http://localhost:4202/rename-view.html?title=…&dark=…` via `showPopupWindow`, then `setViewTitle` + `document.title`).
- After init, broadcasts the initial scheme on `workspace:theme-changed` so views calling `syncWithOpenFinTheme()` later get the current scheme without Theme API access.

`updateToolbarButtons(scheme?)` replaces toolbar options on all browser windows: built-ins `ShowHideTabs`, `ColorLinking`, `PresetLayouts`, `LockUnlockPage`, `SaveMenu` + three custom buttons (page-tabs toggle, theme toggle sun/moon, upload logs) with inline base64 SVG icons in blue `#3b82f6` (custom buttons don't get theme-adaptive icons). Registered as the `setOnThemeChanged` callback so icons flip with the scheme.

### Theming

- **`ThemeService` (`lib/theme.service.ts`)**: applies a `ThemePalette` from `@macro/macro-design` to `document.documentElement` — every palette key as BOTH `--kebab-case` and `--theme-kebab-case` CSS variables, plus classes `dark`/`theme-dark` vs `theme-light`. Constructor applies dark immediately (skipping the callback — subclass fields aren't initialized yet). `toggleTheme()`: outside OpenFin flips locally; inside, flips `Theme.setSelectedScheme` and lets the listener update. `syncWithOpenFinTheme()`: initial `getSelectedScheme()` (isolated try — the Theme API may throw in view contexts, falls back to IAB-only) + IAB subscribe on `workspace:theme-changed`; `stopSyncing()` unsubscribes. Observables: `getTheme$()`, `getPalette$()`; sync: `getCurrentTheme()`, `getCurrentPalette()`; `getGeneratedPalettes()` proxies `Theme.getGeneratedPalettes()` (null outside OpenFin).
- **`ThemePresetService` (`lib/theme-preset.service.ts`)**: 6 named presets (`macro-etrading` default, `default`, `carbon-graphite`, `midnight-trader`, `executive-blue`, `stagecoach-red`) loaded by `fetch('/<file>.json')` from the app's public dir. Active id = `theme-preset` storage preference, read through a boot-hydrated sync cache (`hydrate()`), falling back to legacy localStorage key `workspace-theme-preset`. `setActivePresetId(id)` follows the writes-throw posture: rolls back the cache and rethrows on persist failure (restarting on an unpersisted choice would silently revert the theme). Preset change requires a platform restart (palettes are set once at `init()`). `loadPreset`/`loadActivePreset` fall back to compiled `themeConfig` on any failure.
- **`buildMacroGranularTheme(dark, light): ThemeOptions` (`lib/granular-theme.ts`)**: builds the Workspace 24 "Granular Color Theming" token theme (label `Macro`, default dark) — flat dotted `seed` keys (`'brand.base.dark'`, `'brand.accent.light'`, …), per-scheme `overrides` maps (~40 tokens: `color.role.background.1..6`, buttons, forms, `color.enterprise.viewTab.*`, notification/modal chrome), and `notificationIndicatorColors` for blue/green/yellow/red/magenta matching the NotificationsService level colors. Gotcha (code comment): `@openfin/theme-engine` is bundled untyped, so a misspelled token key silently no-ops — every key was verified against the bundled engine and official docs.
- **`onOpenFinThemeChange(cb): () => void` (`lib/theme-sync.ts`)**: standalone IAB subscription to `workspace:theme-changed` for any view; the topic constant is deliberately duplicated there because importing the override service pulls in `@openfin/workspace-platform`, which crashes outside OpenFin. Returns an unsubscribe function; no-op outside OpenFin.

### FDC3: ContextService + ChannelService

- **`ContextService` (`lib/context.service.ts`)**: user-channel FDC3. `broadcast(context)` (warn if no `globalThis.fdc3`), `registerContextListener(contextType?)`, `onContext<T>(contextType)` → filtered `context$` (`shareReplay(1)`), `removeListener()`. `currentChannel$: Observable<string | null>` emits the joined context-group id via 1000 ms polling of `fdc3.getCurrentChannel()` — ref-counted: polling starts on first subscriber, stops on last unsubscribe; emits `null` when unjoined or FDC3 missing; `distinctUntilChanged`.
- **`ChannelService` (`lib/channel.service.ts`)**: named app-channel FDC3 via `getOrCreateChannel`. `broadcast(channelName, context)`, `broadcastMyChannel(context)` / `registerMyChannelListener(contextType?)` against a lazily-created shared `'my-channel'` (memoized promise, reset on failure), `registerChannelListener(channelName, contextType?)`, `channel$` (`shareReplay(1)`). Only one listener at a time — re-registering removes the previous.

### NotificationsService (`lib/notifications.service.ts`)

Wraps `@openfin/workspace/notifications` (lazy `import()`). `register(platformSettings)` registers the notifications platform (id/icon — rasterized via `toTaskbarIcon` — /title) and marks the client environment initialized. Key subtlety: the client environment is per-window (per JS realm) — views that only SEND notifications must call `ensureClientInitialized()` (idempotent; calls the SDK `register()` with no platform options) or `create()` throws "Environment is not initialized". `create(config)` is fire-and-forget with retry: on that specific error it re-inits and retries up to 5 attempts × 200 ms. Level API: `notify(level, options)` + shortcuts `info/success/warning/error/critical(title, body, options?)` — sets `indicator` (blue/green/yellow/red/magenta + label), a `stream` keyed by platform id, optional `toast: 'sticky' | 'transient' | 'none'`, `priority: 1..4`, `sound: 'default' | 'silent'`. `observeNotificationActions(): Observable<NotificationActionEvent>` (completes immediately outside OpenFin). `setReminder(notificationId, date)` / `cancelReminder(notificationId)` → boolean, false on error. `deregister()` on quit. Errors are serialized explicitly (`{name, message, stack}`) because `Error` has no enumerable props and would log as `{}`.

### HomeService (`lib/home.service.ts`)

`register(platformSettings)` → `from(Home.register(homeProvider))`. Provider: `onUserInput` responds `[]` immediately, then returns all registry apps (via `AppsService.ensureLoaded()`/`getApps()`) mapped to `HomeSearchResult`s and title-substring-filtered by the lowercased query; non-empty queries publish a `Home/Search/Query` analytics event. `onResultDispatch` → analytics + `LaunchService.launch(app)`. `mapAppEntriesToSearchEntries` labels by `manifestType` (`view` → "View"/"Launch View", `snapshot`, `external` → "Native App", else "App"), `CLITemplate.SimpleText`, first icon. Registration also installs a `window-created` listener that re-applies the current theme scheme when a Home window appears (Home windows are created lazily and would otherwise miss the platform theme). `show()` → `Home.show()` + analytics.

### StoreService (`lib/store.service.ts`)

Builds and registers the Storefront provider. Constructor: `(AppsService, FavoritesService, StorefrontConfigService, EntitlementsService, LaunchService, storageService?: WorkspaceStorageService, dock3Service?: Dock3Service)` — the last two enable the "Add to Dock" card button.

- `register(platformSettings)`: installs a `window-created` theme-enforcement listener for lazily-created store windows, then `hydrateDockPins() → buildProvider() → Storefront.register(provider)`; keeps the `StoreRegistration` for later `updateAppCardButtons` calls.
- Provider: `cardClickBehavior` from config (repo config uses `'show-app-details'` — card body opens the app-details page while the Launch button launches), `getNavigation`, `getLandingPage`, `getFooter`, `getApps`, and `launchApp` → analytics + `LaunchService.launch` (entitlement gate). Storefront window icon is `toTaskbarIcon(platformSettings.icon)`.
- `buildDecoratedApps()`: all registry+LOB apps, each decorated with a tooltip (entitled → normal; not entitled → `🔒 Requires entitlement: <list>` — apps stay VISIBLE, only launch is blocked) and `secondaryButtons` = `☆ Favorite`/`★ Unfavorite` (action `toggle-store-favorite`) plus, when storage is wired, `📌 Add to Dock`/`📌 Remove from Dock` (action `toggle-dock-pin`).
- **Nav build** (`buildNavigation`): hard limits `MAX_NAV_SECTIONS = 3`, `MAX_NAV_ITEMS = 5` (verified against `@openfin/workspace` `store.d.ts`). A dynamic Favorites section is prepended first (only when the user has ≥ 1 favorite that maps to a known app; title from `navigation.favoritesTitle`, default `'Favorites'`), then configured sections with items capped at 5; sections beyond 3 are dropped with a warning.
- **`filterForItem(apps, item)`**: category-OR-tag union, case-insensitive — apps matching `item.category` (`'*'` = all) first, then apps carrying ANY of `item.tags` (lowercased comparison) that didn't already match, de-duplicated by `appId`. This is what lets LOB apps published via `/store-apps` route into business-area nav items by either field.
- **Dock pins**: per-user preference `WELL_KNOWN_PREFERENCES.dockPinnedApps` (`dock-pinned-apps`), persisted as the FULL array. `hydrateDockPins()` reads via the RAW storage client (which throws) — an outage must be distinguishable from "no pins" or the next full-array write would wipe stored pins; on failure `pinsHydrated` stays false and toggles are refused (after one re-hydration retry). `toggle-dock-pin` toggles are serialized through a promise queue (`pinQueue = pinQueue.then(...)`) so overlapping clicks compose instead of last-write-wins. Order inside `toggleDockPin`: persist FIRST (writes throw; failure leaves dock unchanged) → update cache → analytics → `updateCardButtons` → `dock3Service.refreshPinnedApps([...pinned])` (live dock update; failure is non-fatal — "pin applies on next platform start").
- `updateCardButtons` calls `storeRegistration.updateAppCardButtons({ appId, primaryButton, secondaryButtons })` — the ONLY stable live update; re-registering the provider to refresh the left-nav Favorites section corrupts the store window's platform connection ("Target is not a Workspace Platform"), so the Favorites nav section only refreshes when OpenFin re-calls `getNavigation()` (navigation/reopen).
- Landing page: `topRow` (max 4, falls back to all apps), `middleRow` (max 6), `bottomRow` (max 3), rows resolved by explicit `appIds` else `category`; optional hero with an AppGrid CTA. Footer: max 3 links, logo/text default to platform icon/title.

### AppsService (`lib/apps.service.ts`)

Loads `apps.json` (shape `{ apps: App[] }`) and optionally merges LOB store apps from the storage API. Constructor: `(httpClient: { get<T>(url): Promise<T> }, resolvePath: () => string, storageService?: WorkspaceStorageService)`. `load()` is idempotent (memoized promise + `loaded` flag), runs registry fetch and `getLobStoreApps()` concurrently, and has a TERMINAL catch: `load()` must never stick as a rejected cached promise — a malformed payload degrades to an empty registry, not a dead platform. Sync access after load: `getApps(): App[]`, `getApps$(): Observable<App[]>` (BehaviorSubject), `ensureLoaded()`.

LOB merge rules (`merge`): registry first; LOB apps (pre-sorted by `sortOrder` ascending, undefined last, stable for ties) appended with a per-entry validation guard — `appId`/`title`/`manifest` non-empty strings, `manifestType` ∈ `'view' | 'manifest'`, `icons` non-empty with `icons[0].src` a string — malformed entries are skipped with a warning so one bad row never hides the rest; `appId` collisions with the registry are skipped (**registry wins** — publishers can't shadow platform apps). `toApp(LobStoreApp)` maps onto the OpenFin `App` shape: `publisher` falls back to `lob` then `'Line of Business'` (the details page requires one), a `lob` tag is force-added (the "LOB Apps" nav item finds them by it), `category` passes through when present, `intents: []`.

### Dock3Service (`lib/dock3.service.ts`) and legacy DockService

**Dock3Service** — Dock 3.0 provider. Constructor: `(LaunchService, AppsService, DockConfigService, storageService?: WorkspaceStorageService)`.

`init(platformSettings, appsOverride?, dockOverride?)` (overrides exist for tests): fetches apps, `dock-config.json`, LOB dock apps, and pinned appIds concurrently (the LOB fetch must not serially extend dock registration when the storage host is slow), then builds:
- **Favorites** from `dock-config.json` `favorites[]`: `item` → dock launch button (icon falls back to the referenced app's first icon, then the platform icon; `itemData: { appId, url: app.manifest }`); `folder` → a dock DROPDOWN whose children come from the content-menu folder with the SAME id (Dock 3.0 folder merging — the `DockEntry` folder variant has no `children`; define both halves).
- **Content menu** from `contentMenu[]` (recursive folders/items, same icon fallback).
- **LOB dock apps** appended via `appendLobApps` — wrapped in its own try/catch ("merge failed — dock renders config-driven entries only") plus per-entry guards: required `id`/`label`/`iconUrl` strings, duplicates skipped. All LOB-derived ids are NAMESPACED `lob:<id>` — publishers can't know the platform's config ids and Dock 3.0 keys folder-merging and bookmarking by entry id, so an un-namespaced collision would open the wrong dropdown. `type: 'dropdown'` → favorites folder + same-id content-menu folder with children `lob:<id>:<childId|index>` (children without a `url` filtered; empty → entry skipped); `type: 'icon'` (requires `url`) → favorites item, ALSO catalogued under a content-menu folder `lob-apps` ("LOB Apps") grouped by `lob` into sub-folders `lob:group:<slug>` (items `lob:catalog:<id>`; non-string `lob` = ungrouped; slug collisions get `-2` suffixes).
- **Pinned favorites** appended LAST via `buildPinnedFavorites`: per-user `dock-pinned-apps` ids → items `pin:<appId>` (unknown/duplicate ids skipped with warning).

Config details: `defaultDockButtons: ['home','store','notifications','switchWorkspace','contentMenu']`; `uiConfig.providerIconContentMenu: true` (the provider icon doubles as a content-menu dropdown button instead of a plain drag region); `uiConfig.contentMenu.enableBookmarking: true`; more-menu custom option group **"Macro Tools"** with options Analytics Dashboard (`launch-analytics-dashboard` — launches registry app `macro-analytics-dashboard` via LaunchService) and Process Manager (`launch-process-manager` — `fin.Application.startFromManifest('http://cdn.openfin.co/release/apps/openfin/processmanager/app.json')`). Note: more-menu option text is the `tooltip` field (there is no label field). `Dock.init` `windowOptions`: `taskbarIconGroup: 'macro-workspace'`, experimental snap zone (threshold 40, bottom/top), context menu `['snapToTop','snapToBottom']`.

Provider override subclass: `loadConfig()` always returns the freshly built config (never the stale IndexedDB cache, so icon/config changes apply immediately); `launchEntry(payload)` routes `itemData.appId` through `LaunchService.launch(app)` so the entry honours its `manifestType` (previously always `createView`, which collapsed platform apps into a single view), else `itemData.url` → `platform.createView({ url })`; `moreMenuCustomOptionClicked` → analytics + action dispatch. After `provider.ready`, the dock window's `icon` is overridden with `toTaskbarIcon(...)` — the dock window runs under the OpenFin Workspace app uuid and `DockAllowedWindowOptions` exposes no `taskbarIcon`, so the runtime-mutable window `icon` is the only branding hook.

`refreshPinnedApps(pinnedAppIds)`: live-applies a changed pin set by replacing only the `pin:`-prefixed favorites on the ACTIVE config object (preserving in-session dock edits) and pushing it through `provider.updateConfig(...)`; the active config object is mutated too so the `loadConfig` override keeps returning pinned state on internal dock reloads. No-op with a warning before dock init. `shutdown()` tears down the provider.

**DockService** (`lib/dock.service.ts`) — legacy pre-3.0 dock: `Dock.register` with `workspaceComponents` + a single "Apps" DropdownButton over the registry (action `launch-app`). Still exported and injected into `WorkspaceService`, but `registerComponents()` only registers Dock3; the legacy service is effectively unused at runtime.

### FavoritesService + ClientFavoritesStore

`FavoritesService` (`lib/favorites.service.ts`): per-user store favorites with a synchronous cache (`BehaviorSubject<Set<string>>`) and pluggable persistence:

```ts
export interface FavoritesStore { load(key: string): Promise<string[]>; save(key: string, ids: string[]): Promise<void>; }
```

`hydrate()` resolves the user via optional `AuthService` (default `'anonymous'`) and loads key `workspace-store-favorites:<userId>`. `toggleFavorite(appId)` updates the cache synchronously and writes through fire-and-forget. Sync reads: `isFavorite`, `getFavoriteIds`, `getFavoriteIds$`. `LocalStorageFavoritesStore` is the default (graceful no-op without `localStorage`).

`ClientFavoritesStore` (exported from `lib/storage/storage-context.ts`) adapts the store onto the unified storage client: `load()` awaits `whenWorkspaceStorageReady()` (bounded, 5 s fail-open) then `getFavorites()`; the `key` argument is ignored — user scoping is the client's job. **Write gating**: favorites persist as the FULL list, so `save()` is refused (warn) until a load has SUCCEEDED against the initialized backend — saving from a never-hydrated cache (outage, or the fail-open pre-init window) would silently wipe stored favorites; the in-memory toggle survives and the next hydration re-syncs. The Angular `FavoritesService` wires `ClientFavoritesStore` + re-hydrates on every `onWorkspaceStorageInitialized` callback (DI-time hydrate can race environment selection during boot).

### LaunchService + launchApp

`LaunchService` (`lib/launch.service.ts`) is the central launch guard — Storefront, Home, Dock3, and the `launch-app` custom action all route through `launch(app): Promise<boolean>`: `EntitlementsService.ensureLoaded()`; if `canLaunch(app.appId)` fails, logs, raises a warning notification ("Access restricted … Required: <list>") and returns false; else `launchApp(app)` and returns true.

`launchApp(app)` (`lib/launch.ts`, exported): dispatch on `manifestType` — `view` → `platform.createView({ manifestUrl })`; `snapshot` → `platform.applySnapshot(manifest)`; `external` → `fin.System.launchExternalProcess({ path: manifest, uuid: appId })`; `manifest` (and default) → `fin.Application.startFromManifest(manifest)` (a manifest with a `platform` block boots a separate Workspace Platform with its own provider/windows — how nested platforms launch instead of collapsing into a view). No `app.manifest` → error log, returns undefined.

### SnapService (`lib/snap.service.ts`)

Wraps `@openfin/snap-sdk` `SnapServer`. `init(platformId)`: reads `snap-config.json` via `SnapConfigService`; `enabled: false` skips entirely; otherwise starts `new SnapServer(platformId)` with only explicitly-set `serverOptions` (SDK defaults win) and enables auto window registration; init failure clears the server (non-fatal). `decorateSnapshot`/`prepareToApplySnapshot`/`applySnapshot` are called from the workspace override's snapshot paths and no-op unless `getSnapServerStatus() === 'connected'`; all failures return/continue gracefully. `stop()` disables auto-registration and stops the server. `isRunning` getter.

### Analytics (`lib/analytics-nats.service.ts`)

`AnalyticsNatsService`, singleton via `getAnalyticsNats()`. Publishes workspace analytics to NATS over `@macro/transports` `NatsTransport('macro-workspace-analytics')` at hardcoded `ws://MontyUbuntu2604:8224`, topic `macro.analytics.<user>.<source>.<type>.<action>` (all segments sanitized `[^a-zA-Z0-9_-] → _`, lowercased; user from env `USERNAME`, `'unknown'` fallback). `publish(event)` lazily connects; if the connect fails it silently drops the event (callers additionally `.catch(() => {})` everywhere, so analytics can never break platform flow). Payload adds ISO `timestamp` + `user`. `disconnect()` on quit.

### ViewStateService + React hooks

`ViewStateService` (`lib/view-state.service.ts`): persists namespaced view state in `fin.me.updateOptions({ customData: { viewState } })`, which workspace snapshots capture automatically. API: `restoreState(): Promise<ViewStateData>` (call once on view init; logs + returns `{}` outside OpenFin), `saveState(namespace, data)` (merges namespaces so multiple providers coexist), `getState(namespace)` (in-memory), `flushState()`, `setCollector(fn)` (flush only on workspace save — subscribes to IAB topic `workspace:flush-view-state`, the same topic the override broadcasts 200 ms before snapshot capture), `enableAutoSave(fn, intervalMs = 5000)` (periodic + save-time flush), `disableAutoSave()`, `destroy()`. `ViewStateData = { [namespace: string]: unknown }`.

React (`@macro/openfin/react` → `lib/react.ts`): `useViewState(): [ViewStateService, ViewStateData, boolean]` — stable service instance, restored state object, `isRestored` flag; destroys the service on unmount. `useNotifications(): NotificationsService` — stable instance for views that only `create()` (registration is the shell's job).

### Auth + entitlements

`AuthService` (`lib/auth.service.ts`): config/mock identity — loads `entitlements.json` (`EntitlementsConfig`) once (memoized promise), fallback on failure is `{ currentUser: { id: 'anonymous', name: 'Anonymous', entitlements: [] }, apps: {} }`. `getUser()`, `getEntitlementsConfig()`, `getCurrentUserId()` (sync; `'anonymous'` until loaded). Designed as a pluggable seam for a real IdP. `EntitlementsService` (`lib/entitlements.service.ts`): launch authorization only — apps stay visible to everyone. `ensureLoaded()` (idempotent), `getRequiredEntitlements(appId)` (empty = open to all), `canLaunch(appId)` (any-of match against the user's entitlement set), `getEntitlements()` (copy). `entitlements.json` is bootstrap-tier: always static per-env, never via the storage API.

### Config loader services

All follow the same pattern: injected minimal `httpClient` (`{ get<T>(url): Promise<T> }`) + lazy `resolvePath()` lambda, memoized load, error → logged fallback (never throws):

| Service | File loaded | Fallback |
|---|---|---|
| `SettingsService` (`lib/settings.service.ts`) | `settings.json` (always static per-env via `resolveEnvConfigPath` — it DEFINES the storage environments) | none (throws propagate) |
| `StorefrontConfigService` | `storefront-config.json` | `{ navigation: { sections: [] } }`; `getCardClickBehavior()` default `'perform-primary-button-action'` |
| `DockConfigService` | `dock-config.json` | `{ favorites: [], contentMenu: [] }` |
| `SnapConfigService` | `snap-config.json` | `{ enabled: true }` |
| `AppsService` | `apps.json` | `[]` (terminal catch) |

`resolveEnvConfigPath(fileName)` (`lib/config-path.ts`): `?env=openshift` → `/openshift/<file>`, else `/local/<file>` (also outside a window). In the Angular layer, content-config paths instead go through the storage context's `resolveConfigUrl(fileName)`: REST-mode environments load from `<baseUrl>/config/<name-without-.json>`, local mode from the static folder.

### Angular DI layer (`lib/angular/index.ts`)

Every base service gets an `@Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' })` subclass wiring dependencies via `inject()`:
- `SettingsService`/`AuthService` use Angular `HttpClient` adapted to the promise interface; `AuthService` resolves `entitlements.json` via `resolveEnvConfigPath` (static, bootstrap-tier).
- **`configHttpWithStaticFallback(http, fileName)`** wraps HttpClient for `StorefrontConfigService`, `AppsService`, `DockConfigService`, `SnapConfigService`: the primary URL is the lazily-evaluated `resolveConfigUrl(fileName)` (evaluated at `load()` time, AFTER storage-environment init during boot); on fetch failure it falls back to the static `resolveEnvConfigPath(fileName)` (skipped when the primary already IS the static URL — then the error rethrows). This matters because the config services memoize failures — without the fallback, one storage-service outage would stick an empty store/dock for the whole session.
- `AppsService` and `Dock3Service` get `WorkspaceStorageService` injected (enables LOB `/store-apps` and `/dock-apps` merging); `StoreService` gets `WorkspaceStorageService` + `Dock3Service` (enables Add to Dock).
- `FavoritesService` is constructed with `ClientFavoritesStore` + `AuthService`, hydrates immediately, and re-hydrates on `onWorkspaceStorageInitialized` (only a load against the real backend unlocks write-through).
- Angular `ThemeService` adds signals `isDark` and `currentPalette`, updated through the base callback — zoneless-safe template binding.
- `WorkspaceService` receives the full dependency set (platform, dock legacy, dock3, home, store, settings, storage facade, theme preset, notifications, snap, auth).

### Storage facade + misc

`WorkspaceStorageService` (`lib/workspace-storage.service.ts`, outside `lib/storage/`) is the facade every platform service persists through — delegates to `getWorkspaceStorage()` (the active `WorkspaceStorageClient`; see the storage section). Error posture encoded per method: **reads degrade** (try/catch → log + `[]`/`undefined`) so an outage never bricks boot; **writes throw** (`saveWorkspace`, `savePage`, `saveDockConfig`, `setPreference`, `deleteWorkspace`, `deletePage` pass rejections through) so a failed save is never silently "successful". Exception: the last-saved-workspace pointer helpers (`setLastSavedWorkspaceId`/`removeLastSavedWorkspaceId`) deliberately degrade — it's a convenience pointer. Also exposes shared read-only LOB registries: `getLobDockApps()` / `getLobStoreApps()` (published via `PUT /dock-apps/{id}` and `/store-apps` by LOB tooling; the platform only reads).

`toTaskbarIcon(icon)` (`lib/icon-utils.ts`): Windows taskbar icons must be raster (.ico/.png) — an SVG is ignored and the runtime falls back to the stock OpenFin icon. Resolves `/favicon.ico` against the icon URL's origin (`new URL('/favicon.ico', icon).href`); on unparseable input, swaps the last path segment for `favicon.ico`. Used for every Home/Store/Dock/Notifications/browser-window taskbar icon.

---

## Lib: @macro/openfin — unified Workspace Storage subsystem

All per-user persistence in the OpenFin platform (saved workspaces + layout snapshots, saved pages, dock customization, store favorites, named preferences) plus the shared LOB registries routes through one client-side contract. Source lives in `libs/openfin/src/lib/storage/` (`storage-types.ts`, `local-storage-client.ts`, `rest-storage-client.ts`, `storage-environment.ts`, `storage-context.ts`) plus the facade `libs/openfin/src/lib/workspace-storage.service.ts`. Public exports (from `libs/openfin/src/index.ts`): types `WorkspaceStorageClient`, `StorageMode`, `StorageEnvironmentConfig`, `StorageSettings`, `ResolvedStorageEnvironment`, `LobDockApp`, `LobDockAppChild`, `LobStoreApp`; values `WELL_KNOWN_PREFERENCES`, `LocalStorageWorkspaceStorageClient`, `RestWorkspaceStorageClient` (+ `RestWorkspaceStorageClientOptions`), `resolveStorageEnvironment`, `listStorageEnvironments`, `getSavedStorageEnvironmentChoice`, `saveStorageEnvironmentChoice`, `STORAGE_ENV_QUERY_PARAM`, `STORAGE_ENV_CHOICE_KEY`, `LOCAL_STORAGE_ENVIRONMENT`, `initWorkspaceStorage`, `getWorkspaceStorage`, `getActiveStorageEnvironment`, `whenWorkspaceStorageReady`, `resolveConfigUrl`, `ClientFavoritesStore`, and the facade as `BaseWorkspaceStorageService` (Angular `@Injectable({providedIn:'root'})` subclass `WorkspaceStorageService` at `libs/openfin/src/lib/angular/index.ts`).

### `WorkspaceStorageClient` interface (`storage-types.ts`)

All methods async and user-scoped (client is constructed with a user-id supplier):

```typescript
interface WorkspaceStorageClient {
  // Saved workspaces (full snapshots; upsert by workspace.workspaceId)
  getWorkspaces(): Promise<Workspace[]>;
  getWorkspace(workspaceId: string): Promise<Workspace | undefined>;
  saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): Promise<void>;
  deleteWorkspace(workspaceId: string): Promise<void>;   // resolves even if id absent
  // Saved pages (browser "Save Page"; upsert by page.pageId)
  getPages(): Promise<Page[]>;
  getPage(pageId: string): Promise<Page | undefined>;
  savePage(page: Page): Promise<void>;
  deletePage(pageId: string): Promise<void>;
  // Dock customization, keyed by dock provider id
  getDockConfig(dockProviderId: string): Promise<DockProviderConfigWithIdentity | undefined>;
  saveDockConfig(config: DockProviderConfigWithIdentity): Promise<void>;
  // Store favorites (full-list semantics)
  getFavorites(): Promise<string[]>;
  saveFavorites(appIds: string[]): Promise<void>;
  // Named preferences (small JSON values)
  getPreference<T>(key: string): Promise<T | undefined>;
  setPreference<T>(key: string, value: T): Promise<void>;
  deletePreference(key: string): Promise<void>;
  // SHARED LOB registries — read-only from the workspace side (LOBs PUT via the API)
  getLobDockApps(): Promise<LobDockApp[]>;
  getLobStoreApps(): Promise<LobStoreApp[]>;
}
```

`Workspace`/`Page` come from `@openfin/workspace-platform`, `DockProviderConfigWithIdentity` from `@openfin/workspace`.

### Well-known preferences

```typescript
export const WELL_KNOWN_PREFERENCES = {
  lastSavedWorkspace: 'last-saved-workspace', // string — workspace id restored at startup
  themePreset: 'theme-preset',                // string — active theme preset id
  viewTitles: 'view-titles',                  // Record<viewName, title> — custom view tab titles
  dockPinnedApps: 'dock-pinned-apps',         // string[] — appIds pinned to the dock from the Storefront
} as const;
```

Any JSON-serializable value may be stored under any key; these are the ones the platform itself uses.

### LOB shared-registry types

```typescript
interface LobStoreApp {           // published via PUT /store-apps/{appId}
  appId: string;                  // registry (apps.json) apps win on collisions
  title: string;
  description?: string;           // card + app-details page
  manifest: string;               // view manifest ('view') or platform manifest ('manifest')
  manifestType: 'view' | 'manifest';
  icons: Array<{ src: string }>;  // at least one required
  images?: Array<{ src: string }>; // app-details screenshots
  publisher?: string;             // defaults to lob when omitted
  contactEmail?: string;
  supportEmail?: string;
  tags?: string[];                // route into matching business-area nav items case-insensitively; platform force-adds the lob value as a tag
  category?: string;              // FX / Rates / Commodities / Risk / Spread / Middle Office (optional; tag works too)
  lob?: string;                   // owning line of business, e.g. "Rates"
  sortOrder?: number;             // ascending; undefined last; stable
}

interface LobDockApp {            // published via PUT /dock-apps/{id}
  id: string;
  label: string;
  iconUrl: string;                // required by contract
  type: 'icon' | 'dropdown';      // icon → dock button; dropdown → dock folder
  url?: string;                   // required when type === 'icon'
  children?: LobDockAppChild[];   // required non-empty when type === 'dropdown'
  tooltip?: string;               // RESERVED: accepted + stored, NOT rendered — Dock 3.0 entries have no hover-text field
  lob?: string;                   // grouping in the dock content menu
  sortOrder?: number;
}
interface LobDockAppChild { id: string; label: string; url: string; iconUrl?: string; } // iconUrl falls back to parent's
```

### `LocalStorageWorkspaceStorageClient` (`local-storage-client.ts`)

"local" mode: browser localStorage on this machine using the exact pre-API keys, so existing user data survives the migration to the unified API. Constructor: `(getUserId: () => Promise<string> = async () => 'anonymous')`. All methods no-op gracefully when `typeof localStorage === 'undefined'` (Node tests). Keys:

| Data | localStorage key |
| --- | --- |
| workspaces | `workspace-platform-workspaces` (JSON array) |
| pages | `workspace-platform-pages` (JSON array) |
| dock config | `workspace-platform-dock:<dockProviderId>` |
| favorites | `workspace-store-favorites:<userId>` (per-user; userId from supplier, `anonymous` on throw) |
| LOB dock apps | `workspace-lob-dock-apps` (machine-shared; seed manually in dev) |
| LOB store apps | `workspace-lob-store-apps` |
| preference (well-known legacy) | `lastSavedWorkspace` → `workspace-platform-last-saved`, `themePreset` → `workspace-theme-preset`, `viewTitles` → `macro:view-titles` |
| preference (everything else) | `macro:pref:<key>` (e.g. `macro:pref:dock-pinned-apps`, `macro:pref:pages-migrated`) |

Behavior details: `getPreference` JSON-parses the stored string and on parse failure returns the RAW string cast to `T` — legacy bare-string values (last-saved id, theme preset id) predate JSON wrapping. Array reads (`readArray`) return `[]` on missing key, parse error, or non-array parse result (logged). `getDockConfig` returns `undefined` on parse failure. Deletes (`deleteWorkspace`/`deletePage`) only rewrite the array when something was actually removed. Reads swallow errors (log + `null`/`[]`); `writeJson` logs AND rethrows on failure (e.g. quota) — matching the platform's writes-throw posture.

### `RestWorkspaceStorageClient` (`rest-storage-client.ts`)

Speaks the Workspace Storage API contract (`docs/api/workspace-storage-api.openapi.yaml`); one service deployment per environment, selected purely by `baseUrl`; the user rides the `X-User-Id` header (interim scheme — phase 2 replaces it with a real identity token).

```typescript
interface RestWorkspaceStorageClientOptions {
  baseUrl: string;                    // e.g. http://localhost:3000/workspace/v1 (trailing slashes stripped)
  getUserId?: () => Promise<string>;  // default async () => 'anonymous'
  fetchFn?: typeof fetch;             // default fetch.bind(globalThis)
  timeoutMs?: number;                 // default 10_000
}
```

- **Bound fetch**: the default is `fetch.bind(globalThis)` — a bare `fetch` reference invoked as `this.fetchFn(...)` loses its Window receiver and throws "Illegal invocation" in browsers.
- **Timeout**: every request creates an `AbortController` and `setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs)`; timer cleared in `finally`.
- **User id**: resolved once and cached on the instance (`cachedUserId`); supplier throw → `'anonymous'` (also cached).
- **Headers**: always `Accept: application/json` + `X-User-Id`; `Content-Type: application/json` and a `JSON.stringify` body only when a body is passed.
- **404 semantics are per-method**: `404` on GET → resolves `undefined` (missing single resource); `404` on DELETE → resolves `undefined` (idempotent, already gone); `404` on PUT → **throws** — a 404'd PUT is a misrouted `baseUrl`, and resolving it would report a save as successful while nothing was persisted.
- Any other non-2xx throws `Error("Workspace storage request failed: <METHOD> <url> → HTTP <status> (<body text truncated to 200 chars>)")`. `204` resolves `undefined`. All failures are logged (`Logger.getLogger('RestWorkspaceStorageClient')`) then rethrown, so callers decide fatal (saves) vs degradable (reads).

Endpoint map (all ids `encodeURIComponent`-escaped): `/workspaces` + `/workspaces/{id}` (GET/PUT/DELETE), `/pages` + `/pages/{id}`, `/dock/{dockProviderId}` (GET/PUT), `/favorites` (GET returns `{appIds: string[]}`, PUT body `{appIds}`), `/preferences/{key}` (GET returns `{key, value}` — client unwraps `.value`; PUT body `{value}`; DELETE), `/dock-apps` (GET), `/store-apps` (GET). Collection GETs coalesce `undefined` to `[]`. The phase-1 reference server is `market-data-server` at `http://localhost:3000/workspace/v1`; phase 2 is Java Spring Boot + MongoDB.

### Storage environment resolution (`storage-environment.ts`)

```typescript
type StorageMode = 'localStorage' | 'rest';
interface StorageEnvironmentConfig { mode: StorageMode; baseUrl?: string; label?: string; }
interface StorageSettings { defaultEnvironment?: string; environments?: Record<string, StorageEnvironmentConfig>; }
interface ResolvedStorageEnvironment { name: string; config: StorageEnvironmentConfig; }
```

Constants: `STORAGE_ENV_QUERY_PARAM = 'storageEnv'` (provider-URL query param, e.g. `?storageEnv=uat`); `STORAGE_ENV_CHOICE_KEY = 'macro:storage-env'` — the user's picker choice, which **deliberately stays in localStorage regardless of mode**: it is the bootstrap pointer saying WHERE storage lives, so it cannot itself live behind the API; `LOCAL_STORAGE_ENVIRONMENT = { name: 'local', config: { mode: 'localStorage', label: 'Local (this machine)' } }` — always available.

`resolveStorageEnvironment(settings?, search?)` merges `local` + `settings.environments` and walks candidates in precedence order: `?storageEnv=` query param (from `search` arg or `window.location.search`) → saved user choice (`macro:storage-env`) → `settings.defaultEnvironment` → `local`. Invalid candidates — unknown name, or `mode: 'rest'` with no `baseUrl` — are skipped with a `logger.warn`, so a bad config can never brick boot; the final fallback is always local localStorage. `listStorageEnvironments(settings?)` returns all selectable environments (always including `local`) for pickers. `saveStorageEnvironmentChoice(name | undefined)` persists or (with `undefined`) clears the choice — applied on next platform start (the provider restarts the platform on switch; see `apps/macro-workspace/src/app/provider/provider.component.ts`).

The `storage` block in `apps/macro-workspace/public/local/settings.json`:

```json
"storage": {
  "defaultEnvironment": "local",
  "environments": {
    "local": { "mode": "localStorage", "label": "Local (this machine)" },
    "dev":   { "mode": "rest", "label": "DEV", "baseUrl": "http://localhost:3000/workspace/v1" },
    "uat":   { "mode": "rest", "label": "UAT", "baseUrl": "https://workspace-storage-uat.example.com/workspace/v1" },
    "prod":  { "mode": "rest", "label": "PROD", "baseUrl": "https://workspace-storage.example.com/workspace/v1" }
  }
}
```

### Storage context — module-level singleton (`storage-context.ts`)

- `initWorkspaceStorage(settings?: StorageSettings, options?: { search?; getUserId? }): ResolvedStorageEnvironment` — resolves the environment, constructs the matching client (`RestWorkspaceStorageClient` for valid `rest`, else `LocalStorageWorkspaceStorageClient`), replaces the module-level context, marks readiness, and fires all registered init listeners (each in try/catch — a throwing listener is warned, not fatal). Called once during platform boot after settings load; re-initializing replaces the context (the platform restarts on environment change). Call site: `WorkspaceService.initStorageContext()` in `libs/openfin/src/lib/workspace.service.ts` — settings first (they define the environments), then `initWorkspaceStorage(settings.storage, { getUserId })`, then storage-backed hydrations (theme preset, view titles), then platform init. The non-OpenFin browser path also resolves the storage context so storage-backed features behave consistently outside the runtime.
- `getWorkspaceStorage(): WorkspaceStorageClient` — the active client. Before init it lazily creates a local-localStorage fallback context, preserving pre-API behavior for plain browser tabs and unit tests.
- `getActiveStorageEnvironment(): ResolvedStorageEnvironment` — `local` until initialized.
- `whenWorkspaceStorageReady(timeoutMs = 5000): Promise<void>` — resolves when `initWorkspaceStorage` has run, or after the timeout as a fail-open fallback (a host that never initializes storage just gets the default local client). Early hydrators await this so they cannot race environment selection during boot.
- `onWorkspaceStorageInitialized(listener: () => void): void` — runs the listener on every (re)initialization, and immediately if already initialized. Used by the Angular `FavoritesService` to re-hydrate against the real backend even when its DI-time hydrate ran against the pre-init fallback.
- `resolveConfigUrl(fileName: string): string` — where platform config loads from: in REST mode, `${baseUrl}/config/${fileName minus '.json'}` (storefront/dock/apps/snap config served per environment by the API); otherwise `resolveEnvConfigPath(fileName)` (`libs/openfin/src/lib/config-path.ts`: `/openshift/<file>` when `?env=openshift`, else `/local/<file>`). **Bootstrap-tier rule**: `settings.json` and `entitlements.json` deliberately do NOT go through this — they define the storage environments and the user identity, so they always load from the static per-env folder (`AuthService` uses `resolveEnvConfigPath('entitlements.json')` directly).
- `resetWorkspaceStorageForTests()` — test-only: drops context, readiness state, and listeners.

The Angular config services (`libs/openfin/src/lib/angular/index.ts`) pair `resolveConfigUrl` with `configHttpWithStaticFallback(http, fileName)`: when a fetch from the storage service's `/config/*` fails, it retries the static per-env file (always deployed with the app) so a storage outage degrades to baseline config instead of an empty store/dock; if the failing URL already was the static one, the error is rethrown. This matters because the config services memoize failures — without the fallback an error would stick for the whole session.

### `ClientFavoritesStore`

`FavoritesStore` adapter (`{ load(key): Promise<string[]>; save(key, ids): Promise<void> }`, defined in `libs/openfin/src/lib/favorites.service.ts`) over the unified client, so `FavoritesService` rides the active backend. The `key` argument is ignored — user scoping is the client's job. `load` awaits `whenWorkspaceStorageReady()` then `getFavorites()`, and only then sets an internal `loadSucceeded` flag. **`save` is gated on a prior successful load**: favorites are persisted as the FULL list, so saving from a cache that never hydrated (backend outage, or the fail-open pre-init window) would silently wipe the user's stored favorites. Until a load has succeeded, saves are skipped with a warning — the in-memory toggle survives and the next hydration re-syncs the view.

### `WorkspaceStorageService` facade (`workspace-storage.service.ts`)

The single seam every platform service persists through; callers never know which backend is active (it delegates to `getWorkspaceStorage()` per call, so a context re-init is picked up transparently). **Error posture — reads degrade, writes throw**: all getters (`getWorkspaces`, `getWorkspace`, `getPages`, `getPage`, `getDockConfig`, `getLobDockApps`, `getLobStoreApps`, `getPreference`) catch, log, and return `[]`/`undefined` so a storage outage can never brick platform boot; the writes (`saveWorkspace`, `deleteWorkspace`, `savePage`, `deletePage`, `saveDockConfig`, `setPreference`) propagate exceptions so a failed save is never silently reported as success. One deliberate exception: the last-saved-workspace pointer helpers `getLastSavedWorkspaceId(): Promise<string | null>`, `setLastSavedWorkspaceId(id)`, `removeLastSavedWorkspaceId()` (thin wrappers over the `last-saved-workspace` preference) degrade on write too — a failed pointer update is logged, not thrown.

Consumers (Angular DI wrappers in `libs/openfin/src/lib/angular/index.ts`): `AppsService` (merges LOB `/store-apps` into the registry), `Dock3Service` (LOB `/dock-apps` at dock init), `StoreService` (per-user "Add to Dock" via the `dock-pinned-apps` preference), `WorkspaceOverrideService`, `WorkspaceService`.

### Pages migration flag (`workspace-override.service.ts`)

Saved pages historically lived in the OpenFin platform's DEFAULT storage (per-machine IndexedDB). The platform override (`libs/openfin/src/lib/workspace-override.service.ts`) routes page CRUD through the unified backend with a one-time import guarded by the preference `pages-migrated` (`PAGES_MIGRATED_PREF`):

- `getSavedPages(query?)` reads from unified storage; if the backend returns an empty list, `migrateLegacyPagesOnce()` runs — guarded per boot (a module-scoped boolean) AND by the persisted flag per backend. The flag is read via the **raw** `getWorkspaceStorage().getPreference` (which throws) rather than the degrading facade: an unreadable flag means the migration state is UNKNOWN, and importing then could resurrect pages the user deleted — so the attempt aborts (catch → warn → `null`) instead. On a clean run it copies each legacy page via `storageService.savePage(page)`, then sets `pages-migrated = true`.
- Any explicit page save (`createSavedPage`/`updateSavedPage`) calls `markPagesMigrated()`, persisting the flag: once the user has written pages of their own, a later empty page list (e.g. after delete-all) must never re-import stale legacy pages.
- `getSavedPage(id)` retries once after triggering `getSavedPages()` to cover the not-yet-migrated case (backend empty, legacy page opened directly).
- Dock customization has a softer fallback (no flag): `getDockProviderConfig(id)` returns the unified-storage config if present, else falls through to the platform default storage so a pre-migration dock customization still applies; saves go only to unified storage.

---

## Lib: @macro/transports — unified messaging (AMPS, Solace, NATS/JetStream)

One library owns all enterprise-messaging connectivity. Every transport (60East AMPS, Solace PubSub+, NATS core, NATS JetStream) implements a single `TransportClient` contract and yields RxJS-friendly streams of a common `TransportMessage` envelope. Framework adapters are subpath entry points: Angular root-injectable services and React hooks.

### Layout, path aliases, dependencies

```
libs/transports/src/index.ts                          → @macro/transports
libs/transports/src/lib/transport.ts                  # common contract
libs/transports/src/lib/amps/amps-transport.ts        # AmpsTransport (+ spec)
libs/transports/src/lib/solace/solace-transport.ts    # SolaceTransport
libs/transports/src/lib/nats/nats-transport.ts        # NatsTransport (+ spec)
libs/transports/src/lib/nats/nats-jetstream-transport.ts  # NatsJetStreamTransport (+ spec)
libs/transports/src/lib/angular/index.ts              → @macro/transports/angular
libs/transports/src/lib/react/index.ts                → @macro/transports/react
```

`tsconfig.base.json` aliases: `@macro/transports` → `libs/transports/src/index.ts`, `@macro/transports/angular` → `libs/transports/src/lib/angular/index.ts`, `@macro/transports/react` → `libs/transports/src/lib/react/index.ts`. `libs/transports/package.json` mirrors these as `exports` (`.`, `./angular`, `./react`).

Root export names (each transport is exported twice — modern name + legacy `*Client` alias): `AmpsTransport`/`AmpsClient`, `SolaceTransport`/`SolaceClient`, `NatsTransport`/`NatsClient`, `NatsJetStreamTransport`/`NatsJetStreamClient`; types `TransportClient`, `TransportMessage`, `MessageHandler`, `ErrorHandler`, `EventHandler`, `AmpsConnectionOptions`, `AmpsSowOptions`, `SolaceConnectionOptions`, `SolacePublishOptions`, `NatsConnectionOptions`, `NatsJetStreamConnectionOptions`, `JetStreamSnapshotOptions`.

Peer dependencies (all broker clients **optional** via `peerDependenciesMeta` — only `rxjs ^7` is unconditional): `amps *`, `solclientjs *`, `@nats-io/nats-core ^3.0.0`, `@nats-io/jetstream ^3.0.0`, `@nats-io/kv ^3.0.0`. Repo root pins `amps ^5.3.4-0.378635.60b2fcc`, `solclientjs ^10.18.2`, `@nats-io/{nats-core,jetstream,kv} ^3.4.0`. All transports log via `Logger.getLogger(...)` from `@macro/logger`. Tests: Jest (`npx nx test transports`).

### Common contract — `libs/transports/src/lib/transport.ts`

```typescript
interface TransportMessage<T = unknown> {
  topic: string;          // topic/subject received on
  data: string;           // raw string payload
  json<R = T>(): R;       // lazy JSON.parse of data
  reply?: string;         // reply-to subject (NATS request/reply)
  raw?: unknown;          // transport-native message (amps Message, solace Message, nats Msg/JsMsg/KvWatchEntry)
}
type MessageHandler<T = unknown> = (message: TransportMessage<T>) => void;
type ErrorHandler = (error: Error) => void;
type EventHandler = (event: string, details?: unknown) => void;

interface TransportClient {
  readonly transportName: string;
  connect(options: unknown): Promise<void>;
  disconnect(): Promise<void>;             // completes all subjects, tears down subscriptions
  publish(topic: string, data: string | Record<string, unknown>): void;  // objects JSON.stringify'd
  subscribe(handler: MessageHandler, topic: string): Promise<string>;    // returns subscription ID
  subscribeAsObservable(topic: string): Promise<{ observable: Observable<TransportMessage>; subscriptionId: string }>;
  subscribeAsSubject(topic: string): Promise<{ subject: Subject<TransportMessage>; subscriptionId: string }>;
  unsubscribe(subscriptionId: string): Promise<void>;
  readonly isConnected: boolean;
  onError(handler: ErrorHandler): void;
  onEvent?(handler: EventHandler): void;   // only Solace implements
  getSubscriptionIds(): string[];
}
```

Shared behavior: `connect()` throws if already connected ("Disconnect first."); every publish/subscribe method throws `'Not connected to <X>.'` when disconnected; `disconnect()` is a no-op when not connected; `unsubscribe()` completes the backing subject and is fail-soft on broker-side errors. `isConnected` reflects whether `connect()` succeeded and `disconnect()` has not run — for NATS the `closed()` promise also nulls the connection on server-side close; AMPS/Solace do not update it on silent socket loss (Solace's `DISCONNECTED` session event does null the session).

### `AmpsTransport` — 60East AMPS (`transportName: 'amps'`)

Wraps the `amps` JavaScript `Client`. Constructor: `new AmpsTransport(clientName = 'amps-client')`.

```typescript
interface AmpsConnectionOptions {
  url: string;         // e.g. ws://host:9100/amps/json
  logon?: string;      // passed to client.logonOptions() (auth token etc.)
  heartbeat?: number;  // seconds, 0/undefined = disabled
}
```

Control-message hygiene is the core value-add: every consumer-facing stream filters out AMPS control frames — `isDataMessage()` drops `group_begin`, `group_end`, `oof`, `ack`, `heartbeat` (command read from `message.header.command()` or compact `message.c`). Consumers see data only.

Methods beyond the base contract (all subscribe methods accept an extra trailing `filter?: string` — an AMPS content filter):

- `deltaPublish(topic, data)` — publish only changed fields.
- `deltaSubscribe(topic, filter?)` → `{ observable, subscriptionId }` — delta stream (changed fields per message).
- `sow(topic, filter?, options?)` → `Promise<TransportMessage[]>` — one-shot State-of-the-World query; resolves the collected array on `group_end`. A client-side safety timeout (`options.timeout ?? 10000` ms, **never sent to AMPS**) resolves with whatever was collected so far — a hung query yields a partial array, not a rejection.
- `sowWithHandler(handler, topic, filter?, options?)` — legacy callback SOW, control frames filtered.
- `sowAndSubscribe(topic, filter?, options?)` → `{ observable, subscriptionId, sowComplete: Promise<void> }` — atomic snapshot-then-live on one observable; `sowComplete` resolves on `group_end` (end of the snapshot batch).
- `sowAndDeltaSubscribe(topic, filter?, options?)` — same shape with delta live updates.
- `sowDelete(topic, filter)`, `sowDeleteByData(topic, data)`, `sowDeleteByKeys(topic, keys)`.
- `executeCommand(command)` — escape hatch for a raw `amps` `Command`; captures `subId` from the first message header; `AmpsTransport.getCommand()` (static) returns the `Command` class for building them.
- Accessors: `getSubject(subscriptionId)`, `getClientName()`, `getClient()` (underlying `amps` `Client | null`).

`onError(handler)` registers the handler on the AMPS client (works before or after `connect()`).

#### SOW options — Top N / OrderBy (PR #81)

```typescript
interface AmpsSowOptions {
  batchSize?: number;
  timeout?: number;   // client-side safety timeout for one-shot sow(); never sent to AMPS
  topN?: number;      // max initial SOW records → encoded as AMPS `top_n` command OPTION
  orderBy?: string;   // AMPS OrderBy expression, e.g. `/price DESC, /symbol ASC`
  filter?: string;
  options?: string;   // additional comma-delimited raw AMPS command options
  [key: string]: unknown;  // any other key passes through as an amps CommandParams field
}
```

`toSowCommandParams()` (private, applied to all SOW variants) converts these to `amps` `CommandParams`:

- `timeout` is deleted (local-only).
- `topN` is validated — non-integer or ≤ 0 throws `'AMPS topN must be a positive integer.'` **before** any command is issued.
- `topN` is encoded into the comma-delimited `options` command field as `top_n=N` — deliberately NOT the amps client's own `topN` command parameter, which writes the legacy `top_n` message *header* that current AMPS versions ignore for result limiting.
- If the raw `options` string already contains a `top_n=` entry (case-insensitive, whitespace-tolerant match), it is stripped so the typed value wins and the command never carries two `top_n` values. E.g. `{ topN: 25, orderBy: '/price DESC, /symbol ASC', options: 'send_oof,top_n=10,projection=[/desk,/pnl]', timeout: 5000 }` produces command params `{ orderBy: '/price DESC, /symbol ASC', options: 'send_oof,projection=[/desk,/pnl],top_n=25' }` (verified by `amps-transport.spec.ts`).
- `orderBy` (and `batchSize`, `filter`, any extra keys) pass through unchanged as command parameters.

Semantics: without a paired `skip_n`, `top_n` bounds **only the initial SOW result** of `sow_and_subscribe` — matching live updates continue to flow after the snapshot (documented in `apps/prism/README.md`). `orderBy` accepts comma-separated `/field [ASC|DESC] [TEXT]` terms and orders the SOW result set.

### `SolaceTransport` — Solace PubSub+ (`transportName: 'solace'`)

Wraps `solclientjs`. Constructor `new SolaceTransport(initOptions?: { logLevel?: 'TRACE'|'DEBUG'|'INFO'|'WARN'|'ERROR'|'FATAL' })` initializes the SolclientFactory once per page (module-level `factoryInitialized` guard, profile `version10_5`).

```typescript
interface SolaceConnectionOptions {
  hostUrl: string; vpnName: string; userName: string; password: string;
  clientName?: string;
  connectTimeoutInMsecs?: number;    // default 10000
  reconnectRetries?: number;         // default 3
  reconnectRetryWaitInMsecs?: number; // default 3000
}
```

`connect()` resolves on `UP_NOTICE` (also fires `onEvent('connected')`), rejects on `CONNECT_FAILED_ERROR` with `Solace connection failed: <infoStr>`. `DISCONNECTED` nulls the session and fires `onEvent('disconnected')`. `publish(topic, data, options?: SolacePublishOptions)` sends a DIRECT-delivery message with the payload as a binary attachment to a topic destination; of `SolacePublishOptions` (`correlationId?`, `replyTo?`, `userProperties?`) only `correlationId` is currently applied. Subscribe calls `session.subscribe(topicDestination, /*requestConfirmation*/ true, /*correlationKey*/ topic, /*timeout*/ 10000)`; IDs are `solace-sub-<n>`. Extras: `getSession()`, `getSolace()` (the `solclientjs` module for advanced use), `getSubject(subscriptionId)`.

**Gotchas (verified in source):** a single session-level `MESSAGE` listener fans every inbound message to **all** active subscription subjects — there is no per-topic routing, so overlapping subscriptions each receive every message any of them matched (the envelope's `topic` is the message's actual destination; filter client-side if topics overlap). `unsubscribe(id)` removes the broker-side topic subscription for that entry's topic, which also silences any other subscription IDs on the same topic. `onError` stores the handler but no session event currently invokes it (connection failures surface via the `connect()` rejection and logs instead).

### `NatsTransport` — NATS core (`transportName: 'nats'`)

Wraps `@nats-io/nats-core` using `wsconnect` — **WebSocket URLs only** (e.g. `ws://localhost:8224`), suitable for browser use. Constructor: `new NatsTransport(clientName = 'nats-client')`.

```typescript
interface NatsConnectionOptions {
  servers: string;
  name?: string;                  // defaults to clientName
  maxReconnectAttempts?: number;  // default -1 (retry forever)
  reconnectTimeWait?: number;     // default 2000 ms
}
```

`connect()` also wires `nc.closed()`: on server-side close the connection is nulled and, if the close carried an error, the registered `onError` handler fires — this is the only transport whose `isConnected` self-heals to `false` on remote close. `disconnect()` completes subjects, unsubscribes, then `drain()`s. Subscriptions consume the NATS async iterator; `subscribeAsSubject` completes its subject when the iterator ends; per-message handler errors in `subscribe()` are caught and logged (one bad handler call does not kill the loop). IDs are `nats-sub-<n>`. Extra: `request(topic, data, timeout = 5000)` → `Promise<TransportMessage>` (request/reply); `getConnection()`. Payloads (`Uint8Array`) are decoded via `TextDecoder`; `reply` is propagated on the envelope.

### `NatsJetStreamTransport` — JetStream snapshot + stream (`transportName: 'nats-jetstream'`)

Same `wsconnect` websocket as `NatsTransport`, plus `jetstream(nc)` and `jetstreamManager(nc)` from `@nats-io/jetstream`. Deliberately mirrors `AmpsTransport`'s snapshot+stream shape.

```typescript
interface NatsJetStreamConnectionOptions extends NatsConnectionOptions {
  stream?: string;               // default stream; else resolved from the subject via jsm.streams.find()
  deliverPolicy?: DeliverPolicy; // default for LIVE subscribe (default DeliverPolicy.New)
  bucket?: string;               // default KV bucket (currently informational)
  snapshotTimeout?: number;      // ms fallback for snapshotComplete on empty snapshots (default 5000)
}
interface JetStreamSnapshotOptions {
  stream?: string;
  deliverPolicy?: DeliverPolicy; // default LastPerSubject
  snapshotTimeout?: number;
}
```

- **Live** `subscribe`/`subscribeAsObservable`/`subscribeAsSubject`: ordered consumer via `js.consumers.get(stream, { filter_subjects: subject, deliver_policy: DeliverPolicy.New })` — new messages only. IDs `njs-sub-<n>`.
- **`snapshotAndSubscribe(subject, options?)`** → `{ observable, subscriptionId, snapshotComplete: Promise<void> }`: ordered consumer with `DeliverPolicy.LastPerSubject` delivers the last value per matching subject (the blotter snapshot) then keeps streaming live updates on the same consumer. `snapshotComplete` resolves on the first delivered message with `m.info.pending === 0` (backlog drained at delivery time), or via the `snapshotTimeout` safety timer for the empty-snapshot case. Ordered consumers are `AckPolicy.None` — no manual acks. IDs `njs-snap-<n>`.
- **`snapshotAndSubscribeKv(bucket, keyFilter = '>', options?)`**: KV-backed variant; `@nats-io/kv` is **lazily `import()`ed** so it stays an optional peer. Opens the bucket via `Kvm`, watches with `KvWatchInclude.LastValue`; snapshot-done on `entry.delta === 0` (last of the initial batch) or the first `entry.isUpdate === true`, plus the same safety timer. Envelope `topic` = the KV key. IDs `njs-kv-<n>`.
- **Publish**: `publish()` = core fire-and-forget (the contract); `jsPublish(subject, data)` → `Promise<PubAck>` for persisted writes.
- Stream resolution: per-call `stream` → connection-level `stream` → `jsm.streams.find(subject)`; throws `'No stream configured and JetStreamManager unavailable.'` if neither. `unsubscribe`/`disconnect` close consumer message iterators / KV watchers (per-entry teardown errors ignored) then `drain()`. Extras: `getConnection()`, `getJetStream()`.

### Angular adapter — `@macro/transports/angular`

Four `@Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' })` subclasses, injected with `inject()`: `AmpsTransportService` (client name `'angular-amps-client'`), `SolaceTransportService`, `NatsTransportService` (`'angular-nats-client'`), `NatsJetStreamTransportService` (`'angular-nats-jetstream-client'`). They add no API — only DI + fixed client names. **Singleton caveat** (documented in source): a root singleton's `connect()` throws once connected, so anything needing multiple simultaneous connections (e.g. the Prism blotter, one per source) must instantiate the base classes directly.

### React adapter — `@macro/transports/react`

- `useAmpsTransport(clientName?)`, `useSolaceTransport()`, `useNatsTransport(clientName?)` — each returns `{ client, connected, connect(options), disconnect }` from a shared `useTransport` hook: the client lives in a `useRef` (created once), `connected` is React state toggled by `connect`/`disconnect`, and unmount auto-disconnects (rejection swallowed). No JetStream hook exists — construct `NatsJetStreamTransport` directly.
- `useTransportSubscription(client, topic, isConnected, maxMessages = 100): TransportMessage[]` — subscribes when `isConnected && topic`, accumulates messages newest-first capped at `maxMessages`, unsubscribes on cleanup (a `cancelled` flag unsubscribes immediately if cleanup races the async subscribe).

### Consumers and conflation

The library itself never conflates; high-frequency consumers pipe messages through `ConflationSubject` from `@macro/utils`:

- `libs/prism-core/src/lib/blotter-feed.ts` (`BlotterFeed`) constructs one transport per blotter source (`new AmpsTransport('prism-<sourceId>')` etc.), maps `BlotterSource.topN`/`orderBy` into `AmpsSowOptions` for `sowAndSubscribe` (options object passed only when non-empty), uses `snapshotAndSubscribe` for `nats-js` sources, and routes streaming rows through `new ConflationSubject<string, Row>(source.conflationMs ?? 250)`. The Prism ad-hoc source dialogs (Angular + React) expose "Initial SOW Top N" (positive-integer validated) and "Order by" fields for AMPS sources only.
- `libs/openfin/src/lib/analytics-nats.service.ts` publishes OpenFin analytics events via `new NatsTransport('macro-workspace-analytics')`; the workspace Analytics Dashboard (`apps/macro-workspace/src/app/analytics-dashboard/analytics-dashboard.component.ts`) consumes them by injecting `NatsTransportService`.

### Gotchas

- AMPS `sow()` resolves partial results on its local safety timeout instead of rejecting — an empty array can mean "timed out", not "no data".
- AMPS `topN` must be a positive integer or the SOW call throws before reaching the broker; a raw `top_n=` in `options` is silently replaced by the typed value.
- Solace fan-out is not per-topic (all subjects receive every session message) and `onError` is currently never invoked; unsubscribing one ID drops the broker subscription for its whole topic.
- NATS transports are websocket-only (`wsconnect`); plain `nats://` TCP URLs are not supported.
- `@nats-io/kv` is required only if `snapshotAndSubscribeKv` is called (lazy import); all broker SDKs are optional peers, so an app bundles only the clients it uses.
- `AmpsTransport.subscribe(handler, topic, filter?)` and the Solace/AMPS `getSubject()` accessors are wider than the `TransportClient` interface — code written against the interface loses AMPS filters, SOW, deltas, JetStream snapshots, and NATS request/reply.

---

## Lib: @macro/prism-core — framework-free blotter core

Path alias `@macro/prism-core` → `libs/prism-core/src/index.ts`. Framework-free (no Angular/React imports) core of the Prism "Blotter as a Service": the data-source model, the transport→grid feed controller, plain-WebSocket/REST table clients, capital-markets column inference, roll-up config/heuristics, and the data-source store. Consumed by `apps/prism` (Angular, port 4204) and `apps/prism-react` (React, port 4205) via thin per-framework adapters. Depends on `@macro/transports` (AMPS/NATS/JetStream/Solace clients), `@macro/utils` (`ConflationSubject`), `@macro/macro-grid-format` (format specs), `@macro/logger`, RxJS, and `ag-grid-community` types only. Build: `@nx/js:tsc` → `dist/libs/prism-core`; tests: Jest (`libs/prism-core/jest.config.cts`). `src/index.ts` re-exports everything from `blotter-source`, `column-inference`, `rollup`, `blotter-feed`, `data-source-store`, `ws-table-client`, `rest-table-client`.

### Source model — `libs/prism-core/src/lib/blotter-source.ts`

```ts
export type TransportKind = 'amps' | 'nats' | 'nats-js' | 'solace' | 'websocket' | 'rest';
export type BlotterMode = 'snapshot-update' | 'append' | 'streaming';
export type ColumnMode = 'infer' | 'auto-gen';
```

Modes: `snapshot-update` = stable keyed row set, snapshot seeds the grid then live messages update rows in place by `keyField`; `append` = every message is a new immutable row, trimmed to `maxRows`; `streaming` = keyed high-frequency quotes, always conflated (`conflationMs`) before painting.

Connections are a discriminated union over the transport libraries' own option types (`transport` is the discriminant, stripped before `connect()`):

```ts
export type AmpsConn   = { transport: 'amps' } & AmpsConnectionOptions;          // { url, logon?, heartbeat? }
export type NatsConn   = { transport: 'nats' } & NatsConnectionOptions;         // { servers, name?, ... }
export type NatsJsConn = { transport: 'nats-js' } & NatsJetStreamConnectionOptions; // + stream?, deliverPolicy?, bucket?, snapshotTimeout?
export type SolaceConn = { transport: 'solace' } & SolaceConnectionOptions;     // { hostUrl, vpnName, userName, password, ... }
export type WsConn     = { transport: 'websocket' } & WebSocketConnectionOptions; // { url }  e.g. ws://localhost:3000/prism
export type RestConn   = { transport: 'rest' } & RestConnectionOptions;         // { url }  e.g. http://localhost:3000/prism/tables
export type BlotterConnection = AmpsConn | NatsConn | NatsJsConn | SolaceConn | WsConn | RestConn;
```

`BlotterSource` (full field list):

| Field | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | `string` | Stable slug; per-source transport client name (`prism-<id>`) and the `?source=` deep link |
| `name`, `description?` | `string` | Display |
| `category` | `string` | Business area (FX, Rates, Orders, Risk, …); catalog grouping |
| `transport` | `TransportKind` | |
| `mode` | `BlotterMode` | |
| `connection` | `BlotterConnection` | |
| `topic` | `string` | AMPS topic / NATS(JS) subject / Solace topic / WS or REST table name; may be empty for REST when `connection.url` IS the rows endpoint |
| `filter?` | `string` | AMPS SOW/subscribe content filter (AMPS only) |
| `topN?` | `number` | **PR #81** — max records in the initial AMPS SOW result (AMPS only; encoded as the AMPS `top_n` command option by `AmpsTransport`) |
| `orderBy?` | `string` | **PR #81** — AMPS SOW ordering, e.g. `/price DESC, /symbol ASC` (AMPS only) |
| `keyField?` | `string` | Natural key → `getRowId`; required for `snapshot-update` / `streaming` |
| `conflationMs?` | `number` | When set (or `mode === 'streaming'`), updates are conflated to this interval |
| `maxRows?` | `number` | Row cap for `append` feeds (oldest trimmed) |
| `expandArrays?` | `boolean` | Default `true`: a JSON-array payload expands to one row per element; `false` treats the array as a single record's value. Ignored (always expands) for `websocket` sources, where arrays are protocol framing |
| `columnMode` | `ColumnMode` | `'infer'` (explicit inferred columnDefs) vs `'auto-gen'` (AG Grid v36 auto-generation + `applyInferredFormats`) |
| `rollup?` | `RollupConfig` | Optional roll-up view; absent → blotter offers a `suggestRollup` suggestion |
| `origin?` | `'catalog' \| 'adhoc'` | Catalog entries are read-only; ad-hoc entries are editable/deletable |

Constant maps (all `Record<TransportKind, …>` / `Record<BlotterMode, string>`):

- `TRANSPORT_LABELS`: `amps: 'AMPS'`, `nats: 'NATS'`, `'nats-js': 'NATS JetStream'`, `solace: 'Solace'`, `websocket: 'WebSocket'`, `rest: 'REST API'`.
- `MODE_LABELS`: `'snapshot-update': 'Snapshot + Updates'`, `append: 'Append (events)'`, `streaming: 'Streaming (high-freq)'`.
- `SNAPSHOT_CAPABLE` (can deliver an initial snapshot): `amps`, `nats-js`, `websocket`, `rest` = `true`; `nats`, `solace` = `false` (live-only, start empty).
- `SNAPSHOT_ONLY` (no live stream; data changes only via `BlotterFeed.refresh()`): `rest` = `true`, all others `false`.

### BlotterFeed — `libs/prism-core/src/lib/blotter-feed.ts`

One instance per blotter, so each source gets its own connection.

```ts
export type FeedStatus = 'idle' | 'connecting' | 'snapshot-loading' | 'live' | 'error' | 'stopped';
export interface FeedState { status: FeedStatus; rowCount: number; msgsPerSec: number; error: string | null }
export interface GridOps {
  addRows(rows: unknown[]): void;
  updateRows(rows: unknown[]): void;
  deleteRows(rows: unknown[]): void;
  setInitialRowData(rows: unknown[]): void;
}
export class BlotterFeed {
  constructor(source: BlotterSource, grid: GridOps, onColumns: (rec: Record<string, unknown>) => void);
  readonly getState: () => FeedState;                    // stable reference between changes
  readonly subscribe: (fn: () => void) => () => void;    // returns unsubscribe
  start(): Promise<void>;  stop(): Promise<void>;  refresh(): Promise<void>;
}
```

**Store seam**: status is an immutable snapshot via `getState()`/`subscribe()` — `useSyncExternalStore`-shaped; the Angular app bridges it into signals, React uses it directly. **GridOps seam**: Angular `MacroAngularGrid` maps add/update/delete to its `addRows$`/`updateRows$`/`deleteRows$` Subjects and `setInitialRowData` to the method of the same name; React maps the Subjects via the grid ref and `setInitialRowData` to `rowData` prop state (the React ref has no such method).

**`start()` wiring** (status: `connecting` → `snapshot-loading` → `live`):

- Conflation: created when transport ≠ `rest`, mode ≠ `append`, and (`mode === 'streaming'` OR `conflationMs != null`) — a `ConflationSubject<string, Row>(conflationMs ?? 250)` whose conflated output calls `grid.updateRows([value])`. REST gets none (no stream; `refresh()` writes directly).
- `amps` → `AmpsTransport.sowAndSubscribe(topic, filter, options?)`. **PR #81**: builds `AmpsSowOptions` from `topN` (included when `!= null`) and `orderBy` (trimmed; empty string omitted); when both absent it calls the two-argument overload. SOW records are buffered until `sowComplete` resolves, then live messages route per mode.
- `nats-js` → `NatsJetStreamTransport.snapshotAndSubscribe(topic)` (last-per-subject snapshot, then stream).
- `nats` / `solace` → live-only `subscribeAsObservable(topic)`; every record routes immediately (no snapshot phase).
- `websocket` → `WsTableClient`: `connect(conn.url)` then `subscribeTable(topic)` (snapshot then stream).
- `rest` → `RestSnapshotClient.fetchRows(conn.url, topic)`; one-shot; a late-arriving snapshot after `stop()` is discarded (fetch cannot be aborted, but the `stopping` flag prevents seeding a stopped feed's grid).
- Snapshot-then-live transports share `consumeSnapshotThenLive(observable, completePromise)`: rows buffer while `inSnapshot`, then columns are inferred from the batch and `seedSnapshot` writes via `grid.setInitialRowData`.
- Rate meter: 1-second `setInterval` publishing `msgsPerSec` from a message-count window; skipped for `rest`.
- Error posture: transport/WS `onError` and `start()` failures patch `{ status: 'error', error }` — unless `stop()` already ran (`stopping` suppresses late connect/close errors from overwriting `'stopped'`). A failed `start()` also completes the conflation subject so its interval doesn't keep ticking.

**Routing** (`route(rec)` per live record):

- `append`: stamp a synthetic `__id` (`r<counter>`), `grid.addRows([row])`, push onto an ordered queue, trim past `maxRows` via `grid.deleteRows(removed)`.
- keyed (`snapshot-update`/`streaming`): first sighting of a key → `addRows`; thereafter → conflation subject if present, else direct `updateRows`. Key = `String(rec[keyField ?? 'id'])`.
- Payload parsing (`readRecords`): `message.json()`, falling back to `{ value: message.data }` on parse failure; array payloads expand per `expandArrays` (websocket always expands); non-object elements wrap as `{ value }`.

**Column inference trigger**: `onColumns` fires exactly once — for snapshot batches with a MERGED sample (`mergeSample`, below) so sparse fields (e.g. `yield` null on futures, numeric on cash) infer correctly regardless of row order; live-only feeds infer from their first record.

**`refresh()`** — REST only (no-op for streaming transports, while already `refreshing`, while `stopping`, or unless status is `live`/`error` — racing the initial fetch would double-insert). Re-fetches and diffs the new snapshot in place: for keyed modes it dedupes within the snapshot by key (last wins), computes adds (unseen keys), updates (existing keys), and removals (keys absent from the new snapshot — deleted via `{ [keyField]: key }` stubs, enough for `getRowId`), then replaces the key set. `append` mode deletes all current rows and re-adds the fetched set with fresh `__id`s. Errors patch `status: 'error'` without clearing the grid.

`stop()`: clears the rate timer, unsubscribes RxJS subs, completes conflation, `unsubscribe(subId)` + `disconnect()` on the transport and WS client (all failures swallowed), then patches `'stopped'`.

### WsTableClient — `libs/prism-core/src/lib/ws-table-client.ts`

Plain-WebSocket "table" protocol client over the browser `WebSocket` (injectable `WebSocketFactory`/`WebSocketLike` for tests). Protocol (all JSON text frames):

```
server→client on connect:   { type: 'tables', tables: [{ name, title?, description?, keyField?, mode? }] }
client→server:              { type: 'listTables' } | { type: 'subscribe', table } | { type: 'unsubscribe', table }
server→client on subscribe: { type: 'subscribed', table } then { type: 'snapshot', table, rows: [...] }
server→client streaming:    { type: 'update', table, row: {...} } or { type: 'update', table, rows: [...] }
server→client on problems:  { type: 'error', message }
```

API: `connect(url, timeoutMs = 10_000): Promise<void>` (rejects on failure/timeout; a mid-handshake `disconnect()` settles a pending connect); `listTables(timeoutMs = 5_000): Promise<WsTableInfo[]>` (returns cached announce, else sends `listTables` and awaits); `subscribeTable(table, snapshotTimeoutMs = 10_000)` → `{ observable: Observable<TransportMessage>, subscriptionId: 'ws-table-<table>', snapshotComplete: Promise<void> }` — mirrors the `AmpsTransport.sowAndSubscribe` / JetStream `snapshotAndSubscribe` shape so `BlotterFeed` handles all three identically; `onError(handler)`; `disconnect()`.

Lenient by design (works against envelope-less servers): a bare JSON array frame = a batch of rows; a bare object without a recognized control `type` = a single row; non-JSON text = one `{ value }` row via `BlotterFeed`'s fallback. `snapshotComplete` resolves on the `snapshot` frame, OR on the first live/bare-data frame, OR on `snapshotTimeoutMs` (servers with no snapshot support go live instead of hanging). `type: 'error'` frames reject the pending snapshot and table waiters and fire `onError`. Snapshot and live rows flow through the SAME observable; payloads are the unwrapped row object/rows array wrapped as `TransportMessage` (`json()` returns the payload as-is). Data received before any subscription (greeting frames) is dropped. A server-initiated close rejects pending waiters and fires `onError`; a client-initiated one does not.

Helper: `discoverWsTables(url, timeoutMs = 5_000): Promise<WsTableInfo[]>` — connect, read announced tables, disconnect (used by the ad-hoc source dialogs).

### RestSnapshotClient — `libs/prism-core/src/lib/rest-table-client.ts`

Snapshot-only mirror of the WS protocol over HTTP GET (injectable `FetchLike`; `RestTableInfo = WsTableInfo`):

- `listTables(url, timeoutMs = 10_000)`: `GET url` → `{ tables: [...] }` or a bare array of infos; entries without a string `name` are filtered out; anything else throws.
- `fetchRows(url, table, timeoutMs = 15_000)`: `GET url/<table>` (URL-encoded segment, query string preserved: `/api?k=v` + `t` → `/api/t?k=v`), or `GET url` when `table` is empty. Accepts a bare array (normal case), `{ rows: [...] }`, `{ data: [...] }`, or a single object (wrapped as one row); anything else throws.
- Timeout via `AbortController` stays armed through the BODY read too — a server that sends headers then stalls the body cannot hang the caller. Distinct error messages for timeout vs unreachable vs non-2xx (`<url> responded <status> <statusText>`) vs invalid JSON.

Helper: `discoverRestTables(url, timeoutMs = 10_000)` — one-shot catalog fetch for the ad-hoc dialogs. Local reference server: `http://localhost:3000/prism/tables`.

### Column inference — `libs/prism-core/src/lib/column-inference.ts`

- `titleCase(field)` — `changePercent` → `Change Percent`, `order_id` → `Order Id`.
