- `specForField(name, value, keyField?): ColumnFormatSpec | undefined` — maps one field to a `@macro/macro-grid-format` spec by name heuristics, first match wins: timestamps/`time` → datetime; `date|maturity|expiry|settle` → date; identifiers (`Id` suffix on the ORIGINAL casing so `bid`/`grid`/`mid` survive, plus lowercase `id` and an `_id` suffix — the broader `Id|ID` suffix rule exists only in rollup.ts's group-candidate filter, not here) and categoricals (cusip/isin/sedol/symbol/ticker/desk/book/trader/account/side/status/type/venue/broker/state/name/ccy/currency) → `undefined` (plain text); then numeric-only: `dv01|pv01|kr01|bpv` → signed integer with pos/neg color (dollar sensitivities per bp, NOT basis points); `bps|basis` → basisPoints ±color; `spread` → basisPoints; `pct|percent` → percent ±color; `yield|coupon|rate` → percent 3dp; `pnl|p&l|pl|realized` → number 2dp thousands ±color; `qty|quantity|size|volume|notional|amount|nominal|face` → integer thousands; bid/ask/mid/rate/price/last/spot on an FX-keyed source (`keyField` matches `symbol|pair|ccypair|ccy_pair`) → `fxRate` with `pipDecimals: 5`, `jpyConvention: true`, `symbolField: keyField`; other price-like → number 4dp; `change` → signed 4dp ±color; any other number → number 2dp.
- `inferColumns(sample, keyField?): InferResult` (`{ columns: ColDef[]; formats: ColumnFormatMap }`) — one `ColDef` per field with `headerName: titleCase(field)`; the key field is pinned left; numeric kinds (`number|integer|percent|basisPoints|currency|compact|multiplier|fxRate|treasury`) get `type: 'numericColumn'`.
- `inferColumnsFromRows(rows, keyField?, maxRows = 20)` and `mergeSample(rows, maxRows = 20)` — merge the first N rows into one representative record (first non-null value per field wins) so sparse fields still infer correctly; `BlotterFeed` runs every snapshot batch through `mergeSample`.
- `applyInferredFormats(formats): ProcessAutoGeneratedColumnDefs` — for `columnMode: 'auto-gen'`: an AG Grid v36 `processAutoGeneratedColumnDefs` callback that decorates auto-generated leaf columns with the inferred value formatters (`buildValueFormatter`) and cell styles (`buildCellStyle`), which raw auto-gen would otherwise bypass.

### Roll-ups — `libs/prism-core/src/lib/rollup.ts`

```ts
export type RollupAggFunc = 'sum' | 'avg' | 'min' | 'max' | 'count' | 'first' | 'last' | 'none';
export interface RollupConfig {
  groupBy: string[];                              // coarsest first, e.g. ["desk","book","trader"]
  aggregations?: Record<string, RollupAggFunc>;   // per-field overrides; unlisted numerics inferred by name
  enabled?: boolean;                              // open rolled up (default false)
  expandLevels?: number;                          // default 1; -1 expands everything
  grandTotal?: boolean;                           // bottom grand-total row (default true)
}
```

`'none'` excludes a numeric field from aggregation entirely (blank at group level) — e.g. a per-currency `notional` that must never be summed across books in different currencies (`notionalUsd` is the aggregatable field).

- `aggForField(field): RollupAggFunc` — name-based default: strong-average per-unit fields (`rate|yield|price|coupon|spread`) → `avg`; sums (`pnl|qty|size|flow|volume|notional|amount|nominal|face|value|exposure|dv01|delta|gamma|vega|theta|position|balance|count|fee|charge|commission` — with lookbehinds keeping `discount` out of `count`) → `sum`; remaining per-unit shapes (`px|bid|ask|mid|last|open|high|low|close|nav|strike|factor|vol…|change|pct|percent|score` — `(?<!f)low` spares `cashflow`, `(?<!ex)change` spares `exchangeFee`) → `avg`; else `sum`.
- `suggestRollup(columns, opts?: { keyField?, mode?, maxLevels? }): RollupConfig | null` — builds a suggestion from (inferred) columns: candidates are non-numeric leaves that are not `__id`, not id-suffixed, not date-like, and not the source's `keyField` on keyed modes (each group would hold one row). An 18-pattern priority list (`/desk/`, `/book/`, `/portfolio/`, `/strategy/`, `/trader/`, `/account/`, `/counterparty|cpty/`, … `/tenor/`, `/(symbol|ticker|instrument|pair)$/`, `/side$/`, `/status$/`, `/(ccy|currency)$/`) defines hierarchy order, capped at `maxLevels` (default 3); per pattern the shortest matching field name wins (`book` over `bookName`). Returns `null` if no grouping field matches. Numeric leaves become `aggregations` via `aggForField`.
- `applyRollupToColumns(defs, rollup)` — pure decorator: `groupBy` fields become hidden `rowGroup` columns with `rowGroupIndex` in hierarchy order; measures get `aggFunc` (explicit override, else name-inferred for `numericColumn` leaves; `'none'` gets none); other leaves get `enableRowGroup: true`. Missing `groupBy` fields are ignored. Recurses into column groups.
- `completeRollup(rollup, columns)` — fills a partial source-authored config with name-inferred aggregations for uncovered numeric columns. Needed because v36 auto-generated defs are bare `{ field }` objects with no `type: 'numericColumn'`, so `applyRollupToColumns`' type-based fallback never fires on the auto-gen path.
- `rollupGroupHeader(rollup)` — `'Desk / Book / Trader'`.
- `rollupGridOptions(rollup): GridOptions` — `rowGroupPanelShow: 'always'`, `groupDefaultExpanded: expandLevels ?? 1`, `suppressAggFuncInHeader: true`, `grandTotalRow: 'bottom'` unless `grandTotal === false`, and a left-pinned auto group column (min width 250) headed by `rollupGroupHeader`. Spread into `gridOptions` when the roll-up view is on.

### DataSourceStore — `libs/prism-core/src/lib/data-source-store.ts`

Framework-free repository: a read-only seed catalog merged with user-defined ad-hoc sources persisted in localStorage under key `prism.adhoc-sources.v1`.

- `subscribe(fn)` / `getAll()` are `useSyncExternalStore`-shaped; `getAll()` returns a **memoized `[...catalog, ...adhoc]` snapshot** (stable reference between changes — React identity requirement). `getAdhoc()` returns only ad-hoc entries.
- `loadCatalog(fetchJson: (url) => Promise<BlotterSource[]>, url = '/data-sources.json')` — the fetcher is injected so the lib has no HttpClient/fetch dependency; each entry is stamped `origin: 'catalog'`. Failures **degrade to an empty catalog** with a warn log (never throw).
- Lookups: `get(id)`, `byTransport(kind)`, `byMode(mode)`, `groupedByCategory()` (first-seen category order for the catalog UI).
- Ad-hoc CRUD (catalog is immutable): `addAdhoc(src: Omit<BlotterSource, 'id' | 'origin'>)` generates id `adhoc-<name-slug>-<8-char randomUUID prefix>` (index fallback without `crypto`) and returns the created source; `updateAdhoc(src)` (matched by id, `origin` forced to `'adhoc'`); `removeAdhoc(id)`. Each persists to localStorage (write failures logged, not thrown) and re-emits the merged snapshot. Corrupt/missing localStorage loads as `[]`.

---

## Lib: @macro/macro-design — design system & theming

Single source of truth for all design tokens, dark/light mode, the "macro" named theme, the AG Grid theme, and the OpenFin workspace palette. Location: `libs/macro-design`. Package name `@macro/macro-design` (version 0.0.1, `type: commonjs`), built with `@nx/js:tsc` to `dist/libs/macro-design`; tests are Jest with `testEnvironment: 'jsdom'` (`libs/macro-design/jest.config.ts`). Peer deps `ag-grid-community >=33`, `@angular/core >=18`, `react >=18` — all marked optional in `peerDependenciesMeta`, so the framework-free core can be consumed alone.

Three TypeScript entry points (aliases in `tsconfig.base.json`; matching `exports` map in `libs/macro-design/package.json`):

| Import | File | Contents |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `@macro/macro-design` | `libs/macro-design/src/index.ts` | `themeConfig`, `ThemePalette`, `ThemeConfig`, `MACRO_THEME`, `MACRO_THEMES`, `DEFAULT_THEME_ID`, `getTheme`, `getPalette`, `MacroThemeDefinition`, `ThemeMode`, `ThemeController`, `themeController`, `ThemeState`, `ThemeControllerOptions`, `buildAgGridTheme`, `AG_GRID_FONTS`, `getInitialIsDark`, `applyDarkMode`, `onSystemThemeChange` |
| `@macro/macro-design/angular` | `libs/macro-design/src/lib/angular/index.ts` | `ThemeService` (signals adapter) + re-exports of `themeController` and the types |
| `@macro/macro-design/react` | `libs/macro-design/src/lib/react/index.ts` | `useTheme()` hook, `UseThemeResult` + same re-exports |

React/Vite apps cannot use tsconfig paths at runtime, so each Vite app manually aliases `@macro/macro-design/react` in its `vite.config.ts` (e.g. `apps/macro-react/vite.config.ts` maps it to `../../libs/macro-design/src/lib/react/index.ts`).

### CSS files

Apps import the shared CSS by relative path in their global `styles.css`, BEFORE any framework CSS, in this exact order (verified in `apps/macro-angular/src/styles.css` and `apps/macro-react/src/styles.css`):

```css
@import '../../../libs/macro-design/src/lib/css/fonts.css';
@import '../../../libs/macro-design/src/lib/css/macro-etrading.css';
@import '../../../libs/macro-design/src/lib/css/macro-design.css';
```

#### `libs/macro-design/src/lib/css/fonts.css`

Three Google Fonts `@import url(...)` lines (remote, fonts.googleapis.com): Roboto (variable ital/wdth/wght), IBM Plex Mono (all weights + italics), Noto Sans (variable), Ubuntu (300/400/500/700).

#### `libs/macro-design/src/lib/css/macro-etrading.css` — the full token system

"Macro E-Trading Design System v1.0.0". Dark-oriented, trading-optimized token set. Sections:

1. **Core palette** (`:root`, raw colors — comment says "never use directly in components"): `--slate-50…950` (incl. `--slate-850`) cool blue-tinted neutrals; brand `--cerulean-50…700` ("Macro Cerulean", e.g. `--cerulean-400: #2aa6e6`, `--cerulean-500: #1685c2`); directional `--green-300…900`, `--red-300…900`; `--yellow-300…500`, `--blue-300…900`, `--purple-400/500`.
2. **Semantic tokens, dark** (`.dark` selector): surfaces `--bg-app/-canvas/-panel/-raised/-overlay/-grid-row/-grid-alt/-grid-hover/-grid-selected`; foregrounds `--fg-1…4`, `--fg-inverse`; borders `--border-1…3`, `--border-grid`; brand `--brand/-hover/-press/-soft`, `--on-brand`; market directional `--mkt-up`, `--mkt-up-bg`, `--mkt-up-bg-flash`, `--mkt-down*`, `--mkt-neutral`, `--bid`, `--bid-bg`, `--ask`, `--ask-bg`; status `--status-info/-warn/-error/-ok` (+ `-bg` variants); `--focus-ring` (double box-shadow).
3. **Semantic tokens, light** (`:root:not(.dark)` — so removing the `.dark` class IS light mode; there is no separate light class in practice, despite a stale `.theme-light` mention in the file header comment). Same token names, light values (e.g. `--mkt-up: #0f7f45`, `--brand: #1685c2`).
4. **Typography** (`:root`): `--font-sans` (Roboto stack), `--font-mono` (IBM Plex Mono stack), `--numeric-feature: "tnum" 1, "lnum" 1, "zero" 1`, sizes `--fs-10…48` (px), line-heights `--lh-tight/snug/normal`, weights `--fw-regular…bold`, letter-spacing `--ls-tight/normal/wide/allcaps`.
5. **Spacing/radii/shadows/motion** (`:root`): `--space-1…10` (2–48px), `--radius-xs…xl` + `--radius-round: 999px`, `--shadow-0…3`, easings `--ease-out/--ease-in-out`, durations `--dur-instant/fast/base/slow` + `--dur-flash: 600ms`, control/grid heights `--h-grid-row: 22px`, `--h-grid-header: 26px`, `--h-control-sm/md/lg`.
6. **Base element styles**: `html, body` get `--bg-app` background, `--fg-1` color, `--font-sans` at `--fs-13`; utility classes `.up .down .bid .ask .muted`; `.mono, code, kbd, .num` use `--font-mono` + `--numeric-feature` (`.num` also `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums`); `::selection` and WebKit scrollbar styling.
7. **PrimeNG/PrimeReact (Aura) overrides** on `:root, .p-component, body`: maps Prime surface variables (`--surface-0…700`, `--surface-ground/section/card/overlay/border/hover`) and `--primary-color`, `--primary-color-text`, `--primary-hover-color`, `--primary-active-color`, `--highlight-bg`, `--highlight-text-color`, `--text-color`, `--text-color-secondary`, `--text-muted-color` onto the macro tokens. Extra component styles: `.p-button.macro-bid` / `.p-button.macro-ask` directional buttons, `.p-tag.p-tag-{success,info,warning,danger}` status tags, `.p-toast` severity left-borders.
8. **AG Grid companion styles**: `.ag-cell.mkt-num` (tabular numerals, right-aligned), `.ag-cell.mkt-up/.mkt-down` colors, tick-flash animations `.ag-cell.mkt-flash-up/-down` (keyframes `mkt-flash-up/-down`, `--dur-flash`), `.ag-cell-data-changed` flash background. **AG Grid v36 selector renames handled with legacy fallbacks**: pinned-row containers `.ag-grid-pinned-{top,bottom}-rows` (legacy `.ag-pinned-{top,bottom}` kept), sort icons `.ag-icon-asc/.ag-icon-desc` under `.ag-header-cell-sorted-{asc,desc}` (legacy `.ag-sort-{ascending,descending}-icon` kept). Also a 2px brand selection stripe via `.ag-row-selected::before` and density hooks `[data-density="tight"|"cozy"] .ag-theme-quartz` overriding `--ag-row-height/--ag-header-height/--ag-font-size`.
9. **OpenFin hooks**: `html.openfin body` gets a 1px border; `html.openfin .macro-titlebar { -webkit-app-region: drag; }` with `no-drag` on titlebar buttons/inputs and AG Grid header/root wrapper.

#### `libs/macro-design/src/lib/css/macro-design.css` — Shadcn/Tailwind variable bridge

Maps the Shadcn UI variable set (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--card`, `--popover`, `--primary`, `--secondary`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--destructive`, `--border`, `--input`, `--ring`, `--chart-1…5`, `--radius: 0.3125rem`, `--sidebar*`) onto the E-Trading tokens, with hard-coded fallbacks so it degrades if `macro-etrading.css` is missing. `:root` = light values, `.dark` = dark values (Shadcn convention). Must be imported AFTER `macro-etrading.css`. React apps then re-export these as Tailwind theme colors via `@theme inline { --color-background: var(--background); … }` in their own `styles.css`.

### AG Grid theme — `libs/macro-design/src/lib/ag-grid-theme.ts`

```ts
export function buildAgGridTheme(isDark: boolean): Theme; // ag-grid-community Theme
export const AG_GRID_FONTS = {
  fontFamily: "'Roboto', system-ui, sans-serif",
  cellFontFamily: "'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace",
  headerFontSize: 10, headerFontWeight: 500, fontSize: 12,
  cellHorizontalPadding: 10, rowHeight: 22, headerHeight: 28,
  wrapperBorderRadius: 0,
  rowBorder: { style: 'solid', width: 1, color: '#1c2029' },
  columnBorder: false,
} as const;
```

`buildAgGridTheme` composes `themeQuartz.withPart(iconSetMaterial).withPart(isDark ? colorSchemeDarkBlue : colorSchemeLight).withParams(AG_GRID_FONTS)`; dark mode additionally applies a `DARK_TOKENS` param block mirroring `macro-etrading.css` hex values (background `#12141a`, `chromeBackgroundColor: '#181b22'`, accent `#2aa6e6`, row hover `#22262f`, selected `#1a2a3f`, menu/input colors, etc.). Light mode uses only `colorSchemeLight` + fonts. v36 API notes baked into the source: header background derives from `chromeBackgroundColor` (dedicated `headerBackgroundColor` param removed); `menuShadow` renamed `popupShadow`; there is no `headerFontFamily` — base `fontFamily` styles headers/chrome while `cellFontFamily` overrides data cells (monospaced digits stay aligned).

Consumers: both grid wrappers. `libs/macro-angular-grid/.../macro-angular-grid.ts` holds the theme in a signal, reads `document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark')` at init, and re-runs `buildAgGridTheme` from a `MutationObserver` on the root element's class attribute (zoneless-safe). `libs/macro-react-grid/src/lib/macro-react-grid.tsx` does the same with `setTheme(buildAgGridTheme(document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark')))` + a `MutationObserver`. Apps never build the grid theme directly.

### Dark-mode utilities — `libs/macro-design/src/lib/dark-mode.ts`

- `getInitialIsDark(): boolean` — precedence: localStorage key `theme` (`'dark'`/`'light'`) → `matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')` → `false`. SSR-safe (`window` undefined → `false`); also guards missing `window.matchMedia` (jsdom).
- `applyDarkMode(isDark: boolean): void` — toggles the `dark` class on `document.documentElement` AND persists `'dark' | 'light'` to `localStorage.theme`. No-op when `document` is undefined.
- `onSystemThemeChange(cb: (isDark: boolean) => void): () => void` — subscribes to the `prefers-color-scheme: dark` media query; the callback fires ONLY while `localStorage.theme` is absent (an explicit user choice mutes OS tracking). Returns a cleanup that removes the listener; returns a no-op cleanup when `window` is undefined.

### OpenFin palette — `libs/macro-design/src/lib/theme.config.ts`

```ts
export interface ThemePalette {
  brandPrimary: string; brandPrimaryActive?; brandPrimaryHover?; brandPrimaryFocused?; brandPrimaryText?;
  brandSecondary: string; brandSecondaryActive?; brandSecondaryHover?; brandSecondaryFocused?; brandSecondaryText?;
  backgroundPrimary: string; background1?…background6?;
  textDefault?; textHelp?; textInactive?;
  inputBackground?; inputColor?; inputPlaceholder?; inputDisabled?; inputFocused?; inputBorder?;
  statusSuccess?; statusWarning?; statusCritical?; statusActive?;
}
export interface ThemeConfig { dark: ThemePalette; light: ThemePalette; }
export const themeConfig: ThemeConfig;
```

`themeConfig` is the Macro E-Trading palette in OpenFin Workspace palette shape: dark brand `#2AA6E6` on `backgroundPrimary #0B0D12` (backgrounds 1–6 = the slate ramp `#12141A…#4A5060`); light brand `#1685C2` on `#ECEEF2`. Status colors match the CSS tokens (`#34D97A`/`#F5C13A`/`#FF6B64` dark, `#0F7F45`/`#C79306`/`#B82C25` light). Consumed by `@macro/openfin`: `theme.service.ts` (BehaviorSubject seeded with `themeConfig.dark`), `platform.service.ts` (falls back to compiled `themeConfig` when JSON theme presets are unavailable, feeding `buildMacroGranularTheme(dark, light)` into the workspace platform init), and `theme-preset.service.ts` (compiled never-fails fallback when a preset fails to load).

### Named theme registry — `libs/macro-design/src/lib/themes.ts`

```ts
export type ThemeMode = 'dark' | 'light';
export interface MacroThemeDefinition { id: string; label: string; palettes: ThemeConfig; }
export const MACRO_THEME: MacroThemeDefinition; // { id: 'macro', label: 'Macro E-Trading', palettes: themeConfig }
export const DEFAULT_THEME_ID: string;          // 'macro'
export const MACRO_THEMES: Readonly<Record<string, MacroThemeDefinition>>; // currently only 'macro'
export function getTheme(id?: string): MacroThemeDefinition; // unknown/omitted id → MACRO_THEME
export function getPalette(mode: ThemeMode, id?: string): ThemePalette;
```

The registry currently contains exactly one theme (`macro`); palettes are sourced from `themeConfig` so CSS tokens, the OpenFin palette, and the AG Grid theme share one set of color values.

### ThemeController — `libs/macro-design/src/lib/theme-controller.ts`

Framework-agnostic store owning the active theme id + dark/light mode and their DOM side effects.

```ts
export interface ThemeState {
  readonly isDark: boolean; readonly mode: ThemeMode; readonly themeId: string;
  readonly theme: MacroThemeDefinition; readonly palette: ThemePalette;
}
export interface ThemeControllerOptions {
  syncSystem?: boolean;   // default true — follow prefers-color-scheme when user hasn't chosen
  syncOpenFin?: boolean;  // default true — follow OpenFin platform theme inside the runtime
  defaultThemeId?: string; // default 'macro'
}
export class ThemeController {
  constructor(options?: ThemeControllerOptions);
  start(): void; stop(): void;
  readonly getSnapshot: () => ThemeState;          // stable reference until state changes
  readonly subscribe: (l: () => void) => () => void;
  setDark(isDark: boolean): void; toggle(): void;  // both PERSIST to localStorage
  setTheme(themeId: string): void;
  get currentIsDark(): boolean; get currentThemeId(): string;
}
export const themeController: ThemeController; // shared singleton used by both adapters
```

Behavior: constructor seeds `isDark` from `getInitialIsDark()`. `start()` is idempotent (only the first call does work): it applies the `.dark` class WITHOUT persisting (so an unset preference keeps tracking the source), then attaches `onSystemThemeChange` and `onOpenFinThemeChange` listeners per options. External changes (system/OpenFin) call the internal `set(isDark, persist=false)` — class toggled, no localStorage write; explicit user actions (`toggle`/`setDark`) use `persist=true` → `applyDarkMode` (class + localStorage). Listeners are notified only when state actually changed; snapshots are immutable and recomputed on change (suitable for `useSyncExternalStore`). `stop()` pops and runs all cleanups and resets `started` (adapters never call it in practice — the singleton is app-lifetime).

OpenFin sync lives in `libs/macro-design/src/lib/openfin-theme-sync.ts` (not exported from the public index): `onOpenFinThemeChange(cb): () => void` subscribes to InterApplicationBus topic `workspace:theme-changed` (payload `{ isDark: boolean }`, broadcast by the platform's `WorkspaceOverrideService.setSelectedScheme` in `@macro/openfin`) with source `{ uuid: '*' }`. Deliberately dependency-free mirror of the `@macro/openfin` helper so this lib stays platform-agnostic; reads `globalThis.fin` and returns a no-op cleanup outside the OpenFin runtime; unsubscribe errors during teardown are swallowed.

### Angular adapter — `@macro/macro-design/angular`

`ThemeService` (`@Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' })`, implements `OnDestroy`) wraps the `themeController` singleton as signals. Constructor calls `themeController.start()`, snapshots state into a private `signal<ThemeState>`, and subscribes for updates; `ngOnDestroy` only unsubscribes the signal bridge (controller keeps running). Surface: `state` (readonly signal of `ThemeState`), computed `isDark`, `mode`, `themeId`, `theme`, `palette`; methods `toggle()`, `setDark(isDark)`, `setTheme(themeId)` delegating to the controller. Apps start theming simply by injecting it: `apps/macro-angular/src/app/app.ts`, `apps/prism/src/app/app.ts`, `apps/macro-angular-fdc3/src/app/app.ts` do `protected readonly theme = inject(ThemeService)` and bind `theme.isDark()` / call `theme.toggle()` in templates. Name-collision gotcha: `@macro/openfin` exports a DIFFERENT `ThemeService` (OpenFin platform color-scheme service, used by `apps/macro-workspace`) — the import path disambiguates.

### React adapter — `@macro/macro-design/react`

```ts
export interface UseThemeResult extends ThemeState {
  toggle: () => void; setDark: (isDark: boolean) => void; setTheme: (themeId: string) => void;
}
export function useTheme(): UseThemeResult;
```

`useTheme()` calls `themeController.start()` in a mount `useEffect` and reads state via `useSyncExternalStore(themeController.subscribe, themeController.getSnapshot, themeController.getSnapshot)`; actions are `useCallback`-stable. React apps additionally call `themeController.start()` at module top of `main.tsx` (before `ReactDOM.createRoot`) so the `.dark` class is applied before first paint — `apps/macro-react/src/main.tsx`, `apps/prism-react/src/main.tsx`, `apps/capital-markets-themes/src/main.tsx` all do this.

### The `.dark` class convention

Every theming layer keys off a single `dark` class on `<html>` (`document.documentElement`), maintained only by this library:

- **PrimeNG** (all Angular apps): `providePrimeNG({ theme: { preset: Aura, options: { darkModeSelector: '.dark' } } })` (e.g. `apps/macro-angular/src/app/app.config.ts`).
- **PrimeReact 11** (React apps): `<PrimeReactProvider theme={{ preset: Aura, options: { darkModeSelector: '.dark' } }}>`.
- **Tailwind CSS 4**: `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark *));` in each React app's `styles.css` (class-based, not media-query).
- **AG Grid**: wrappers rebuild `buildAgGridTheme(isDark)` from a `MutationObserver` on the root class list.
- **CSS tokens**: `macro-etrading.css` dark block on `.dark`, light on `:root:not(.dark)`; `macro-design.css` Shadcn bridge on `:root`/`.dark`.

Apps must NOT define their own `:root`/`.dark` variable blocks or duplicate dark-mode logic — mode changes flow exclusively through `ThemeController`/`applyDarkMode`, and everything else reacts to the class.

---

## Grid stack: @macro/macro-grid-format, @macro/macro-angular-grid, @macro/macro-react-grid

Three libraries form the AG Grid 36 Enterprise stack. `@macro/macro-grid-format` is a framework-free column-format engine plus Format tool panels; `@macro/macro-angular-grid` and `@macro/macro-react-grid` are thin, line-for-line-equivalent wrappers around `ag-grid-angular` / `ag-grid-react` that de-duplicate onto it. Path aliases (`tsconfig.base.json`):

```
@macro/macro-grid-format         -> libs/macro-grid-format/src/index.ts          (core, framework-free)
@macro/macro-grid-format/angular -> libs/macro-grid-format/src/lib/angular/index.ts
@macro/macro-grid-format/react   -> libs/macro-grid-format/src/lib/react/index.ts
@macro/macro-angular-grid        -> libs/macro-angular-grid/src/index.ts
@macro/macro-react-grid          -> libs/macro-react-grid/src/index.ts
```

`macro-grid-format` has a `@nx/js:tsc` build target whose `tsconfig.lib.json` **excludes** `src/lib/angular/**` and `src/lib/react/**` — the framework panels are consumed from source via the subpath aliases only; the built package (`libs/macro-grid-format/package.json`) still declares `./angular` and `./react` exports and marks `ag-grid-community|angular|react` (>=33), `@angular/core` (>=18), `react` (>=18) as **optional** peers. The two wrapper libs have **no build target** (source-only: `macro-angular-grid` has `test`+`lint`, `macro-react-grid` has an inferred Vitest `test` via `vite.config.ts`). Both wrapper barrels re-export `FormatType`, `ColumnFormatConfig`, `ColumnFormatMap`, `ColumnFormatSpec` from `@macro/macro-grid-format` for back-compat.

Both wrappers register modules once at module load:

```ts
ModuleRegistry.registerModules([AllCommunityModule, AllEnterpriseModule,
  IntegratedChartsModule.with(AgChartsEnterpriseModule)]);
```

The full verbatim port guide (engine ordering, persistence side-channels, bake-mode design, v36 upgrade deltas) is `docs/copilot/ag-grid-36-format-panel-port.md`; a condensed version auto-loads for Copilot from `.github/copilot-instructions.md`.

### Format spec model (`libs/macro-grid-format/src/lib/format-spec.ts`)

`ColumnFormatSpec` is a JSON-serializable discriminated union over `kind` — it **never stores functions**, so it round-trips through `getGridState()`/`applyGridState()` and is rebuilt into a `valueFormatter` on restore. Kinds (`FormatKind`): `number`, `integer`, `percent`, `basisPoints`, `currency`, `compact`, `multiplier`, `treasury`, `fxRate`, `date`, `text`. All numeric kinds share `NumericModifiers`:

```ts
interface NumericModifiers {
  decimals?: number; thousands?: boolean; scale?: number;   // scale: multiply raw value (e.g. 1e-6 for $mm)
  prefix?: string; suffix?: string;
  signDisplay?: 'auto' | 'always';                          // 'always' => leading + on positives (not on 0)
  negativeStyle?: 'minus' | 'parentheses';                  // parentheses = accounting (1,234)
  colorMode?: 'none' | 'posneg' | 'negative';
  nullText?: string; zeroText?: string; locale?: string;    // locale pinned 'en-US' by default
}
```

Kind-specific extras: `currency` (`currency` ISO code, `currencyDisplay: 'symbol'|'code'|'none'`), `compact` (`notation: 'KMB'|'mmbn'`), `treasury` (`fraction: 32|64`, `plusTick`), `fxRate` (`pipDecimals`, `jpyConvention`, `symbolField` — the row field carrying the pair symbol), `date` (`dateStyle: 'date'|'datetime'|'time'`), `text` (`weight: 'normal'|'bold'|'bolder'|'lighter'`, `italic` — styles only, value untouched).

Persistence shape `ColumnFormatMap = Record<string, ColumnFormatSpec>` is a deliberately **bare** `colId -> spec` map (no `{version, formats}` envelope) so older builds still iterate it; versioning is per-entry structural migration. `migrateSpec(raw)` passes through entries with `kind`, upgrades legacy `{type: 'number'|'percent'|'bps'|'currency'|'compact', decimals}` (`bps`→`basisPoints`; currency gains `USD`/`symbol`; compact gains `KMB`), returns `undefined` for anything unrecognised; `migrateMap(raw)` drops unrecognised entries.

### Format engine (`libs/macro-grid-format/src/lib/format-engine.ts`)

Pure/deterministic; AG Grid appears as type-only imports.

- `formatValue(value, spec, rowData?)`: dispatches by kind. Non-finite numbers → `nullText ?? ''`; exact `0` → `zeroText` when set. Numeric path: `n = value * (scale ?? 1) * kindMultiplier` where percent ×100, basisPoints ×10000; defaults `defaultDecimals(kind)` (integer 0, basisPoints 1, compact 1, fxRate 5, else 2) and grouping on only for `number`/`integer`/`currency`. Auto suffixes `%`, ` bps`, and `x` (multiplier, only when no explicit `suffix`) are placed **inside** parentheses/sign; `negativeStyle: 'parentheses'` wraps the whole `prefix+core+suffix` body. Compact uses thresholds `1e12 T / 1e9 B|BN / 1e6 M|MM / 1e3 K`; `currencyDisplay: 'none'` renders a plain grouped number. `fxRate` reads the symbol from `rowData[symbolField ?? 'symbol']`; `date` parses `Date | number | string` (invalid dates render `String(value)`); `text` passes values through.
- `buildValueFormatter(spec)` → AG Grid `ValueFormatterFunc`, or `undefined` for `text` (style-only; the column keeps its own value display). The built formatter first **unwraps AG Grid's rich aggregation results**: v36's built-in `avg` yields `{ count, value }` objects on group/footer rows (so parents re-aggregate), and any object with a numeric `.value` is unwrapped before formatting — custom formatters elsewhere must do the same or aggregated cells render blank/raw.
- `previewStyle(spec, value?)` → `FormatStyleOverlay { color?, fontWeight?, fontStyle? }` — colour-by-sign resolved from `var(--mkt-up, #16a34a)` / `var(--mkt-down, #dc2626)` (macro-design tokens with hex fallbacks, so dark mode rides the CSS variables), plus font weight/italic for `text`. Drives both live cellStyle and the panel preview.
- `buildCellStyle(spec, base?)` composes the overlay **over** the column's original `cellStyle` (function or object — app `{textAlign:'right'}` survives) and returns `base` unchanged when the spec contributes no overlay (identity comparison matters to the store).

### `ColumnFormatStore` (`libs/macro-grid-format/src/lib/column-format-store.ts`)

One instance per grid, constructed `new ColumnFormatStore(() => gridApi)`. The single owner of `colDef.valueFormatter`/`colDef.cellStyle` mutations. API: `apply(colId, spec)`, `clear(colId)`, `clearAll()`, `has/get/entries/size`, `listFormattableColumns(): FormattableColumn[]` (`{colId, headerName, active}`), `serialize(): ColumnFormatMap | undefined` (undefined when empty so the key is omitted), `restore(map)` (clears then applies; missing columns picked up later by reconcile), `reconcile()`, `onChange(cb): () => void`, `setBakeMode(on)`, `setExternallyManaged(colIds)`.

Mechanics: originals (app `valueFormatter`/`cellStyle`) are captured **once** per colId; a `WeakSet` of every function the store ever produced guarantees one of its own functions is never backed up as "original". `reconcile()` re-installs formats whose installed formatter **and** installed style are no longer on the colDef (a `text` format installs only a cellStyle) — wired by the wrappers to `displayedColumnsChanged`/`newColumnsLoaded` to survive app columnDef rebuilds.

**Bake mode** (`setBakeMode(true)`, what both wrappers use): the store never touches live colDefs — it is a pure spec registry, and the wrapper bakes every format into fresh colDef copies pushed via `setGridOption('columnDefs', ...)`. Rationale (comment in source): AG Grid caches/clones colDefs, and calculated columns cache a `cellDataType` value formatter, so post-hoc `valueFormatter` mutation + `refreshCells` is ignored for them; a single baking source also kills the in-place-mutation ↔ columnDef-rebuild ↔ reconcile feedback loop. `reconcile()` is a no-op in bake mode. `setExternallyManaged` is the narrower per-column variant (calc columns only) retained for non-bake consumers.

### Registry, presets, decimal steps

`format-registry.ts`: `FORMAT_REGISTRY: Record<FormatKind, FormatKindDef>` — per kind `{ label, group ('Numeric'|'Rates'|'FX'|'Text'|'Other'), example, defaults, fields: FormatFieldDef[] }` where each field is `{key, label, control: 'stepper'|'toggle'|'select'|'text', min?, max?, options?, placeholder?}`. **Both** tool panels render controls by looping this registry, so a new knob is a one-file change. `setSpecField(spec, key, raw)` coerces via `NUMBER_FIELD_KEYS` (`decimals`, `pipDecimals`, `scale`, `fraction`) / `BOOLEAN_FIELD_KEYS` (`thousands`, `plusTick`, `jpyConvention`, `italic`) and deletes emptied optional keys. `kindsByGroup()` orders the kind picker.

`presets.ts`: `FORMAT_PRESETS: FormatPreset[]` (28 entries: `{id, label, group: 'Rates'|'FX'|'Commodities'|'Risk / PnL'|'Text'|'General', hint?, spec}`) — e.g. `rates-ust-32` (`treasury` 32nds `99-16+`), `rates-dv01-mm` (`scale: 1e-6`, suffix ` mm`), `pnl-accounting` (USD, 0 dp, parentheses, posneg colour, `zeroText: '-'`), `fx-rate` (5 dp + JPY convention). A preset seeds the editor draft; the user can tweak before applying. `presetsByGroup()` renders the gallery.

`treasury.ts`: `formatTreasury(decimalPrice, {fraction = 32, plusTick = true})` → `handle-TT[+]`, ticks zero-padded to 2, half-tick `+` when the residual fraction of a tick is `>= 0.4` (matches the macro-react app's original `formatTreasury32nd`, kept as the regression baseline); non-numbers → `''`. `fx-rate.ts`: `isJpyPair(symbol)` (case-insensitive `endsWith('JPY')`); `formatFxRate(value, spec, rowSymbol?)` — 3 dp when `jpyConvention !== false` and the row's pair ends in JPY, else `pipDecimals ?? 5`; grouping off by default.

`decimal-steps.ts` — Excel-style "Increase/Decrease Decimals" (`MIN_STEP_DECIMALS = 0`, `MAX_STEP_DECIMALS = 10`): `canStepDecimals`, `stepColumnDecimals(store, api, colId, ±1)` (kinds `treasury`/`date`/`text` are non-steppable; `fxRate` steps `pipDecimals`; others step `decimals`), and `buildDecimalStepMenuItems(store, api, colId)` → two `MenuItemDef`s (or `[]`). Columns with **no** format yet get one inferred so the first click changes only one decimal: `findNumericSample` walks `forEachNodeAfterFilterAndSort` for the first leaf row with a finite raw value (group rows skipped), reads the current display via `getCellValue({useFormatter: true})`, and `inferNumberSpec(formatted, raw)` regex-extracts decimals, thousands comma, literal prefix/suffix, and a forced `+` (en-US assumption, matching the pinned engine locale). Steps ride the store, so they persist under `columnFormats` and the panel reflects them.

### Calculated columns + Show Values As persistence side-channels

Two things AG Grid 36's native `gridApi.getState()` does **not** carry, so the wrappers persist them as extra keys next to the native state:

`calculated-columns.ts` (`CALCULATED_COLUMNS_KEY = 'calculatedColumns'`): the expression/definition of a runtime calc column is absent from `getState()` (only its column-state — order/width/sort — references the colId). `CalcColumnSchema = { colId, calculatedExpression, headerName?, cellDataType? }` (function-free). `serializeCalculatedColumns(colDefs)` extracts schemas from leaf defs carrying `calculatedExpression`; `schemaToColDef(schema)` rebuilds a colDef; `sameCalcSchema(a, b)` dedupes re-emitted calc events; `mergeCalculatedColumns(base, schemas, removed)` produces effective columnDefs — schema matching a base colId **overrides** its calc fields (edited pre-defined column), a new colId is **appended** (user-created), base leaf defs whose colId is in `removed` are **dropped**; pure and idempotent.

`show-values-as.ts` (`SHOW_VALUES_AS_KEY = 'showValuesAs'`): a column's active Show Values As mode (e.g. "% of grand total") lives in `getColumnState()[].showValuesAs`, not in `GridState` (whose `aggregation` section carries only `aggFunc`). `serializeShowValuesAs(columnState)` → `ShowValuesAsEntry[] { colId, showValuesAs }` or `undefined`; only built-in **string** modes are persisted (custom transform objects can carry functions). Restored via `api.applyColumnState({ state: entries })`.

### Format tool panel

`tool-panel-def.ts`: `FORMAT_TOOL_PANEL_ID = 'macroFormat'`, `FORMAT_TOOL_PANEL_COMPONENT = 'macroFormatToolPanel'`, `formatToolPanelDef(toolPanelParams?)` builds the `ToolPanelDef` (label "Format", icon `columns`), and `withFormatPanel(sideBar, toolPanelParams)` merges it idempotently into any `GridOptions.sideBar` shape (`SideBarDef | string | string[] | boolean | null | undefined`; defaults to `['columns','filters']`), passing `{ store }` as `toolPanelParams`.

Both panels are thin renderers over the registry/presets/store with no formatting logic: column multi-select with search filter and an "active" dot, preset gallery (seeds the draft), kind chips, registry-driven controls, a live preview (`formatValue(kindDef.example, draft, {symbol:'EURUSD'})` styled by `previewStyle`), Apply(n)/Reset, and an "Applied" list with per-column remove and Reset all. Both subscribe to `store.onChange` and the grid's `displayedColumnsChanged`.

- Angular: `MacroFormatToolPanelComponent` (`@macro/macro-grid-format/angular`), standalone, signal-driven, implements `IToolPanelAngularComp`; `FormatToolPanelParams extends IToolPanelParams { store: ColumnFormatStore }`; `refresh()` returns `true` to stay mounted across sideBar updates.
- React: `MacroFormatToolPanel` (`@macro/macro-grid-format/react`); `MacroFormatToolPanelProps = CustomToolPanelProps & { store }`. Inline styles derive every colour from the live AG theme vars (`--ag-foreground-color`, `--ag-background-color`, `--ag-border-color`, `--ag-accent-color`) with `color-mix` for muted/border/surface tones, so contrast tracks light and dark.

Registration (both wrappers do this automatically): map `FORMAT_TOOL_PANEL_COMPONENT` in `components` and pass `sideBar: withFormatPanel(userSideBar, { store })`.

### `@macro/macro-angular-grid` — `MacroAngularGrid`

`libs/macro-angular-grid/src/lib/macro-angular-grid/macro-angular-grid.ts`, selector `lib-macro-angular-grid`, standalone, host fills 100% width/height. Inputs: `columns: string | ColDef[]` (JSON string is parsed; parse errors log and yield `[]`; a single object is wrapped in an array), `rowData: unknown[]`, `gridOptions: GridOptions`, `getRowId?: (GetRowIdParams) => string`, `columnFormats?: ColumnFormatMap` (declarative seed applied on grid-ready; also re-restored when the input changes after grid-ready — saved view state overrides on restore).

Public API (component instance):
- `addRows$ / updateRows$ / deleteRows$: Subject<unknown[]>` — each emission becomes `applyTransactionAsync({add|update|remove})`; empty arrays ignored; completed on destroy.
- `applyTransaction(t: RowNodeTransaction)` — **queues** transactions arriving before grid-ready and flushes them in order on `gridReady`.
- `setInitialRowData(data)` — once-only (second call logs a warning and no-ops). Post-ready it goes through `setGridOption('rowData', ...)` (zoneless-safe: callers are WebSocket/promise callbacks where no CD is scheduled) and deliberately does **not** reassign `this.rowData` — a later CD pass would re-fire the `[rowData]` binding with the stale seed and revert every transaction since. Pre-ready it assigns `this.rowData` so the binding initializes the grid.
- `getGridApi()`, `getGridState()`, `applyGridState(state)` (below).

Default `gridOptions` (consumer options spread over them): `defaultColDef` `{sortable, filter, resizable, enableRowGroup, enableValue, enablePivot, enableShowValuesAs}`; `calculatedColumns: { applyMode: 'deferred' }` (dialog validates, applies on Apply); `getMainMenuItems`/`getContextMenuItems` append/prepend the decimal-step items; `pagination: false` (pageSize 10, selector [10,25,50,100]); `rowGroupPanelShow: 'never'`; `pivotPanelShow: 'always'` (initial-only option; strip renders only in pivot mode); v36 selection `rowSelection: {mode:'multiRow', checkboxes:false, enableClickSelection:false}`, `cellSelection: true`, `suppressCellFocus: true`, `animateRows: true`; a status bar with `agTotalAndFilteredRowCountComponent`, `agSelectedRowCountComponent`, `agAggregationComponent` (count/sum/min/max/avg) plus five custom right-aligned toggles. `mergeGridOptions()` re-applies `components` and `sideBar: withFormatPanel(userSideBar, {store})` **after** the user spread so a consumer sideBar/components is merged, never clobbered; `statusBar` defaults unless the consumer supplies one; `columnDefs`/`rowData` are always forced from the inputs (except auto-gen mode).

Status-bar toggle components (same file set, keys registered in `components`): `MacroPaginationToggleComponent` (`macroPaginationToggle`), `MacroGroupingToggleComponent` (`macroGroupingToggle` — OFF also clears active row groups via `applyColumnState({defaultState:{rowGroup:false}})`), `MacroPivotToggleComponent` (`macroPivotToggle` — syncs with external `pivotMode` changes on `columnPivotModeChanged`, `cdr.markForCheck()` for zoneless hosts), `MacroQuickFilterToggleComponent` (`macroQuickFilterToggle` — inline search box driving `quickFilterText`, focused via `afterNextRender` because under zoneless a bare `setTimeout` can fire before the scheduled render; Escape/uncheck clears), `MacroAdvancedFilterToggleComponent` (`macroAdvancedFilterToggle` — flips `enableAdvancedFilter`; AG Grid then natively disables per-column filters; syncs via the `advancedFilterEnabledChanged` event, which is cast because this AG Grid build's typings classify it as internal). These live in `libs/macro-angular-grid/src/lib/{pagination-toggle,group-pivot-toggles,filter-toggles}.component.ts`; the lib barrel exports only the grid component + format types.

Formatting/bake pipeline: the store is a single `readonly` field (never rebuilt in `mergeGridOptions`, so the tool-panel params ref stays stable and the panel isn't torn down) with bake mode on. `recomputeEffectiveColumns()` = `mergeCalculatedColumns(base, userCalcCols, removedCalcCols)` then bakes each stored spec into a colDef **copy** (`valueFormatter` + `buildCellStyle(spec, d.cellStyle)`); colId falls back to `field`. `onStoreFormatsChanged` re-bakes and pushes via `setGridOption('columnDefs', ...)` under an `applyingColumnDefs` re-entrancy guard. `onGridReady` wires: `displayedColumnsChanged`/`newColumnsLoaded` → `reconcile` (no-op in bake mode, kept for safety), `calculatedColumnCreated`/`calculatedColumnExpressionChanged`/`calculatedColumnRemoved` → tracked-schema updates, `store.onChange` → re-bake, then seeds `columnFormats`. The calc upsert handler has an **idempotency guard** (`sameCalcSchema`): feeding a user-created calc column back into columnDefs makes AG Grid re-emit the create event, and without the bail the re-feed → re-emit cycle freezes the grid. Removing a calc column also `store.clear(colId)`s its format so no dangling `columnFormats` entry resurrects on a same-colId recreation. Pushes after calc events go through `pushEffectiveColumnDefs()` (imperative `setGridOption` — AG Grid event callbacks schedule no CD under zoneless, so the `[columnDefs]` binding would never flush).

**Auto-gen guard**: `useAutoGen = !!gridOptions.autoGenerateColumnDefs && effectiveColumnDefs.length === 0` (opt-in AND no consumer columns). When true, the wrapper must stop force-binding columnDefs so AG Grid can infer them from row data — the guard is enforced at **every** columnDefs sink: the template getter `gridColumnDefs` returns `undefined`, `mergeGridOptions` omits `columnDefs`, `onStoreFormatsChanged` early-returns (pushing a baked empty set would wipe the generated columns), the calc handlers capture `wasAutoGen` **before** recompute (adding the calc column makes `effectiveColumnDefs` non-empty, which would flip the flag mid-flight), and `restoreCalcColumns` skips its push.

State persistence: `getGridState()` = `gridApi.getState()` spread with up to three side-channel keys — `columnFormats` (bare map), `calculatedColumns` (`{defs: CalcColumnSchema[], removed?: string[]}`), `showValuesAs` — each omitted when empty. `applyGridState(state)` destructures those keys out and applies **in order**: (1) `restoreCalcColumns` (resets tracked defs/removed — a saved "no calc columns" view removes runtime additions — and pushes columnDefs synchronously so the native state can bind calc column order/width/sort by colId), (2) `api.setState(gridState)`, (3) Show Values As via `applyColumnState`, (4) `store.restore(migrateMap(columnFormats))`, plus a one-shot `firstDataRendered` → `reconcile()` when some formatted colId isn't loaded yet. Pre-ready it warns and returns.

### `@macro/macro-react-grid` — `MacroReactGrid`

`libs/macro-react-grid/src/lib/macro-react-grid.tsx`: `forwardRef<MacroReactGridRef, MacroReactGridProps>`; props `{ columns?, rowData?, gridOptions?, getRowId?, columnFormats? }` (same semantics as Angular; JSON string columns, invalid JSON → `[]`).

```ts
export interface MacroReactGridRef {
  applyTransaction: (t: RowNodeTransaction) => void;   // THROWS 'Grid API not available.' pre-ready (Angular queues instead)
  getGridApi: () => GridApi | undefined;
  getGridState: () => any;                             // native state + columnFormats/calculatedColumns/showValuesAs
  applyGridState: (state: any) => void;                // silently no-ops pre-ready
  addRows$: Subject<unknown[]>; updateRows$: Subject<unknown[]>; deleteRows$: Subject<unknown[]>;
}
```

Same defaults as Angular (defaultColDef, status bar + five toggle panels implemented as local function components in the same file, decimal-step menus, deferred calc columns, v36 selection options). `sideBar`/`components` are memoized once (stable refs so the Format panel is never torn down on re-render) and passed as explicit `<AgGridReact>` props, which take precedence over anything in `gridOptions`. The store lives in a ref, created once with bake mode on. `columnFormats` prop changes after grid-ready re-`restore` via an effect (pass a **memoized** map — each new identity replaces the store's contents).

React-specific columnDefs mechanics: tracked calc columns live in refs plus a `calcVersion` counter; `buildEffective()` is the same merge+bake as Angular. A `pinnedDefsRef` makes the render **after** an imperative `setGridOption('columnDefs', built)` hand AG Grid the *same array reference* — AG Grid short-circuits on identical ref and does not re-apply columnDefs after `setState` (the React analogue of Angular's imperative-push path; called out in the port guide as the one real divergence between the wrappers). The store-change subscription and `applyGridState` both pin. Auto-gen guard mirrors Angular (`useAutoGen` + a ref so long-lived closures read the current value): `columnDefs` prop becomes `undefined`, merged options omit it, the store-change push and `applyGridState`'s columnDefs push bail.

`applyGridState` order matches Angular exactly (calc defs reset + pinned push → `setState` → `applyColumnState` for Show Values As → `store.restore` + one-shot `firstDataRendered` reconcile). Row-op subjects subscribe once the API exists; emissions before grid-ready are **dropped** (unlike Angular's queue). Unmount unsubscribes the RxJS subscription.

### Theming

Both wrappers watch `document.documentElement`'s `class` attribute with a `MutationObserver` and rebuild the theme with `buildAgGridTheme(isDark)` from `@macro/macro-design` on every `.dark` flip — Angular stores it in a `signal<Theme>` (observer callbacks schedule no CD under zoneless), React in `useState`. `buildAgGridTheme` (`libs/macro-design/src/lib/ag-grid-theme.ts`) = `themeQuartz` + `iconSetMaterial` + `colorSchemeDarkBlue|colorSchemeLight` + `AG_GRID_FONTS` (Roboto chrome/headers, IBM Plex Mono cells, `rowHeight: 22`, `headerHeight: 28`) + dark token overrides. v36 notes encoded there: header background derives from `chromeBackgroundColor` (dedicated param removed), `menuShadow` → `popupShadow`, no `headerFontFamily` (base `fontFamily` covers headers; `cellFontFamily` overrides cells). The theme is passed via the v36 theming API (`[theme]`/`theme` prop); the Angular template's `class="ag-theme-quartz"` is vestigial.

### Gotcha: filter `event.source` in consumer grid handlers

The wrappers push columnDefs imperatively (`setGridOption('columnDefs', ...)`) on format changes, calc-column events, and state restore. Those pushes fire column events (e.g. `columnRowGroupChanged`) with `source: 'gridOptionsChanged'` (and `'gridInitializing'` at startup). Consumer handlers that treat such events as user gestures misfire — e.g. the Prism blotters' `onColumnRowGroupChanged` (`apps/prism/src/app/components/blotter/blotter.component.ts`, `apps/prism-react/src/app/components/blotter.tsx`) returns early on those two sources and only reacts to `'api'` (status-bar toggle) / `'uiColumnDragged'` (panel drag), otherwise a transient empty-columns push during a feed restart would flip the roll-up view off.

### Spec coverage

`macro-grid-format` (Jest): `format-engine.spec.ts` (186 lines — null/zero text, per-kind formatting, parentheses-with-suffix-inside, sign-on-positives-not-zero, **avg `{count,value}` unwrapping**, cellStyle merge), `column-format-store.spec.ts` (223 — original capture/restore, reconcile-without-poisoning-originals, text-format style-only semantics, bake mode and externally-managed never mutating colDefs, bare-map serialize/restore), `calculated-columns.spec.ts` (106 — override/append/remove merge semantics), `decimal-steps.spec.ts` (154 — inference, bounds, fxRate pip stepping, menu-item gating), `format-spec.spec.ts` (73 — legacy migration), `treasury.spec.ts` (65), `fx-rate.spec.ts` (40), `show-values-as.spec.ts` (41). Wrappers: `macro-angular-grid.spec.ts` (833 lines, Jest — column parsing, option merging incl. sideBar/components non-clobbering, transaction queueing, subjects, setInitialRowData once-only, calc/Show-Values-As state round-trips, theme observer, destroy) plus toggle specs (`filter-toggles` 144, `group-pivot-toggles` 117); `macro-react-grid.spec.tsx` (795 lines, Vitest — mirrors the Angular suite plus **auto-gen passthrough** tests: columnDefs omitted only when auto-gen is on and no columns supplied, and pre-ready `applyTransaction` throwing).

---

## Libs: @macro/utils & @macro/logger

Two small, framework-free TypeScript libraries. Both are consumed from source via `tsconfig.base.json` path aliases (`"@macro/logger": ["libs/logger/src/index.ts"]`, `"@macro/utils": ["libs/utils/src/index.ts"]`), both build with `@nx/js:tsc` to `dist/libs/<name>` (CommonJS, `main: ./src/index.js`, `types: ./src/index.d.ts`, `*.md` copied as assets), and both test with Jest (test targets declare `{workspaceRoot}/coverage/{projectRoot}` and `{workspaceRoot}/reports/{projectName}-junit.xml` as NX outputs so the merged CI JUnit report stays complete).

### @macro/utils — RxJS conflation

- Source: `libs/utils/src/lib/rx/conflation.ts` (the library's only module); public API re-exported from `libs/utils/src/index.ts`.
- Exports: `conflateByKey`, `ConflationSubject`, `type ConflatedValue`.
- `libs/utils/package.json`: name `@macro/utils`, version `0.0.1`, MIT, dependency `tslib ^2.3.0`, **peer dependency `rxjs ^7.0.0` (non-optional)**. No publish script exists for it (unlike logger); it is built as part of `npm run build:libs`.
- Implementation credit in the source header: based on `angular-fintech/Conflation` (ConflationJS).

Conflation reduces update frequency by batching updates over a fixed time window, keeping only the **latest value per key** — the standard market-data pattern for high-frequency tickers feeding a grid.

#### Types and functions

```typescript
export interface ConflatedValue<TKey = string, TValue = unknown> {
  key: TKey;
  value: TValue;
}

export function conflateByKey<TKey = string, TValue = unknown>(
  source$: Observable<ConflatedValue<TKey, TValue>>,
  intervalMs: number
): Observable<ConflatedValue<TKey, TValue>>;
```

#### Double-buffer algorithm (`conflateByKey`)

Two `Map<TKey, TValue>` buffers and a `currentBuffer` pointer, plus an RxJS `interval(intervalMs)` timer:

1. Every `source$` emission does `currentBuffer.set(key, value)` — later values for the same key overwrite earlier ones within the window (last-write-wins).
2. On each timer tick, the pointer is **swapped** to the other buffer first, then every `[key, value]` entry of the old buffer is emitted as a separate `{ key, value }` next, then the old buffer is `clear()`ed for reuse. The swap-before-drain means writes arriving mid-flush land in the fresh buffer — no mutation-during-iteration and no lost ticks.

Semantics and edge cases:

- Emission cadence: the timer ticks every `intervalMs` unconditionally; an empty buffer simply produces no emissions that tick. Worst-case added latency for any single update is ~`intervalMs`.
- Per-tick ordering is `Map` insertion order — the order in which each key was **first** written during that window.
- Memory is bounded by the number of distinct keys, not update rate.
- Errors from `source$` propagate to the conflated observer via `observer.error`; buffered-but-unflushed values are dropped. `source$` completion completes the conflated observable immediately (no final flush).
- Teardown (unsubscribe) unsubscribes both the source subscription and the interval timer.
- The returned observable is **cold**: each subscription creates its own buffer pair, source subscription, and interval timer. Timers are not aligned across subscribers.

#### `ConflationSubject<TKey, TValue>`

`class ConflationSubject<TKey = string, TValue = unknown> extends Subject<ConflatedValue<TKey, TValue>>`. The constructor takes `intervalMs: number` and builds a private `conflated$ = conflateByKey(this.asObservable(), intervalMs)`. Producers call the inherited `next({ key, value })`; consumers read the conflated side via:

- `subscribeToConflated(observerOrNext?, error?, complete?): Subscription` — accepts a next callback (with optional error/complete callbacks, using RxJS's deprecated multi-arg `subscribe` overload), a partial observer object, or no arguments.
- `getConflatedObservable(): Observable<ConflatedValue<TKey, TValue>>` — raw access to `conflated$`.
- `pipeToSubject(targetSubject: Subject<ConflatedValue<TKey, TValue>>): Subscription` — forwards conflated values into another Subject; the subscription is stored on a single private field, so a second call **overwrites** the tracked handle (the first subscription stays live; only the last one is cleaned up by `unsubscribeFromConflated()` — keep the returned `Subscription` if calling more than once).
- `unsubscribeFromConflated(): void` — tears down the tracked `pipeToSubject` subscription, if any.
- `complete(): void` (override) — calls `unsubscribeFromConflated()` then `super.complete()`; completing the Subject also completes every conflated subscription and stops its interval timer (teardown), so `complete()` is the correct way to stop a `ConflationSubject` from ticking.
- `unsubscribe(): void` (override) — completes first (if not already stopped), then `super.unsubscribe()`.

Because the Subject multicasts into a **cold** `conflateByKey`, N conflated subscribers each get an independent buffer pair and timer (each sees all values); typical usage has exactly one conflated subscriber.

Primary in-repo consumer: `BlotterFeed` in `@macro/prism-core` (`libs/prism-core/src/lib/blotter-feed.ts`) creates `new ConflationSubject<string, Row>(source.conflationMs ?? 250)` for streaming sources, routes keyed row updates through `next({ key, value: rec })`, drains via `subscribeToConflated(({ value }) => grid.updateRows([value]))`, and calls `conflation.complete()` on stop **and on failed start** ("don't leave the conflation interval ticking on a failed start"). Snapshot-only REST sources skip conflation entirely.

### @macro/logger — pino-based SLF4J/Log4J-style logging

- Source: `libs/logger/src/lib/logger.ts`; `libs/logger/src/index.ts` exports everything (`Logger`, `LogLevel`, plus the module's helper types).
- `libs/logger/package.json`: name `@macro/logger`, version `0.0.1`, MIT, dependencies `pino ^10.1.0` + `tslib ^2.3.0`. Works in both Node.js and browsers — environment detected once at module load via `typeof window !== 'undefined'`.

#### Levels

```typescript
export enum LogLevel {
  TRACE = 10, DEBUG = 20, INFO = 30, WARN = 40, ERROR = 50, FATAL = 60,
}
```

Numeric values match pino's native levels exactly. Note: `TRACE` and `FATAL` exist in the enum and as level settings, but `Logger` exposes **no `trace()` or `fatal()` instance methods** — only `debug` / `info` / `warn` / `error`. Setting a logger's level to `FATAL` therefore silences all four methods.

#### Construction and caching

`Logger` has a **private constructor**; the only way to obtain one is the static factory:

```typescript
static getLogger(context: string): Logger  // per-context singleton, cached in a module-level Map
```

Every `Logger` is a pino child logger (`baseLogger.child({ context })`) of a single module-level base logger that is **always created at TRACE** so children can filter independently. Each instance's numeric level defaults to the module-level global level, which initializes from `process.env.LOG_LEVEL` (when `process` exists) and otherwise defaults to `'trace'`.

#### Logging methods

`debug(message: string, data?: unknown)`, `info(...)`, `warn(...)`, `error(...)` — all share the same shape. Filtering is a double gate: an explicit numeric guard in each method (`if (this.logLevel <= LogLevel.X)`) plus the pino child logger's own `level` string (kept in sync). The explicit guard is what actually filters in the browser (a code comment notes pino's browser mode doesn't auto-filter through the custom `write` hooks). The optional `data` argument is dispatched by type:

- `string` → appended to the message: `pino.debug(`${message} ${data}`)`
- `object`/array → passed as pino's merge object (pretty-printed, see below)
- other primitives (number, boolean) → wrapped as `{ value: data }`
- `undefined`/`null` → message only

#### Output format and transports

All output is rendered SLF4J-style by a shared formatter: `[yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS] [LEVEL] [context] - message`, with the context defaulting to `ROOT` when absent. Any non-standard pino fields (everything except `level`/`time`/`pid`/`hostname`/`context`/`msg`) are pretty-printed as 2-space-indented JSON on the following line(s). Gotcha: the timestamp is recomputed from the local wall clock **at format time** — pino's own `time` field (isoTime in Node config) is discarded.

- **Browser**: pino browser mode with `asObject: false` and custom `write` hooks for `info` / `error` / `warn` / `debug`, mapping to the matching `console.*` method; multi-line output (message + pretty JSON) is logged as two consecutive console calls.
- **Node.js**: a custom writable stream JSON-parses each pino line, formats it, and writes level ≥ 40 (WARN and above) to `process.stderr`, everything else to `process.stdout`. Unparseable lines fall back to raw `process.stdout.write`.

#### Level management API

```typescript
setLevel(level: LogLevel | string): void      // per-instance; recreates the pino child at the new level
getLevel(): string                            // e.g. 'DEBUG'
getLevelNumber(): number
static setGlobalLevel(level: LogLevel | string): void  // updates the default AND every cached logger
static getGlobalLevel(): string
static getGlobalLevelNumber(): number
```

String levels are case-insensitive; unknown strings fall back to `INFO`. `setGlobalLevel` never touches the base logger (it stays at TRACE) — it iterates the cache calling `setLevel` on each instance.

App usage pattern: `const logger = Logger.getLogger('MacroAngularMain');` at module scope (e.g. `apps/macro-angular/src/main.ts`), and root components call `Logger.setGlobalLevel(LogLevel.DEBUG)` at startup (`apps/macro-angular/src/app/app.ts`, `apps/macro-react/src/app/app.tsx`, `apps/macro-angular-fdc3/src/app/app.ts`).

#### Build & publish (`build:logger`)

`@macro/logger` is the repo's one npm-publishable library. Root `package.json` scripts:

```json
"build:logger": "nx build logger",
"publish:logger": "npm run build:logger && npm publish --prefix dist/libs/logger",
"publish:logger:dry-run": "npm run build:logger && npm publish --prefix dist/libs/logger --dry-run"
```

`nx build logger` (`libs/logger/project.json`, executor `@nx/js:tsc`, tsconfig `libs/logger/tsconfig.lib.json`) emits a self-contained CJS package to `dist/libs/logger` with `exports` mapping `.` to `./src/index.js` + `./src/index.d.ts`. Both libs are also part of `npm run build:libs` (`nx run-many --target=build --projects=logger,transports,openfin,macro-design,macro-angular-grid,macro-react-grid,utils`). Unit tests: `npx nx test logger` / `npx nx test utils` (Jest; conflation specs cover keyed conflation, error propagation, `pipeToSubject` cleanup, and complete/unsubscribe teardown; logger specs cover caching, level filtering, data-argument dispatch, and global-level propagation).

---

## Repo-root asset trees: design-system/ & AgenticUI/

Two repo-root trees hold **shareable/exported artifacts**, not app code. `design-system/` (~392 tracked files) is the standalone, brandable "Macro" design-system distribution — dark-default trading-UI tokens ("Macro Cerulean" brand `#2aa6e6`, IBM Plex Mono numerals, Roboto sans, green/red reserved for market direction). No app or lib imports from it: the in-repo source of truth for app theming remains `libs/macro-design` (CSS variables, `buildAgGridTheme`, dark-mode utils). A full copy of the `MacroThemeCondensed` bundle is additionally served by the workspace app from `apps/macro-workspace/public/MacroThemeCondensed/` (that copy — not the `design-system/` one — is what `tools/generate-favicons.ps1` reads). `AgenticUI/` documents the "Macro Front Office Transformation" initiative.

### design-system/MacroThemeCondensed/ — self-contained shareable browser bundle
Zero-build demo kit ("zip it, attach to email, drop into SharePoint" per `design-system/MacroThemeCondensed/README.txt`). Entry point `workspace.html` — a splittable workspace shell: draggable pane dividers, a per-pane dropdown to swap pages, toolbar presets ("Trading floor", "Theme storybooks"), pane/divider state persisted to localStorage. Its page list spans the six UI kits, theme storybooks, and foundation cards. Contents:
- `colors_and_type.css` — the token file: core palette (`--slate-*` cool blue-tinted neutrals, `--cerulean-*` brand) as raw colors, semantic tokens dark-default with a `.theme-light` override; target density 12–13px body / 11–12px grid rows.
- `fonts/` — local `.ttf`s (`Roboto-VariableFont_wdth_wght.ttf` + italic, 14 IBM Plex Mono weights, source `.zip`s) plus `fonts.css`.
- `assets/` — brand marks: `favicon.svg`, `logo-mark.svg`, `logo-lockup.svg`, `logo-lockup-light.svg`.
- `ui_kits/` — six full-page kits, each `index.html` + `README.md`: `rates-blotter/` (also `Blotter.jsx` — `PriceBlotter`, `InstrumentSidebar`, `WorkingOrders`; 22px rows, 600ms `.flash-up`/`.flash-down` tick fades), `order-ticket/` (Order + RFQ variants), `sales-cockpit/` (clients → inquiries → axes), `middle-office/` (exceptions queue, trade-capture ledger, KPI strip), `analytics/` (curves/price history/depth ladder, hand-rendered SVG charts), `admin/` (users/entitlements, venue connectivity, audit log). Shared chrome in `ui_kits/_shared/chrome.css` + `chrome.jsx` (`TitleBar`, `TopNav`, `StatusBar`, `Panel`, `Icon`).
- `themes/` — framework bridges with live storybooks: `themes/index.html` landing, `themes/ag-grid/` (`macro-theme.js` AG Grid v33 Theming-API object + `macro-overrides.css` + `preview.html`), `themes/primeng/macro-theme.css`, `themes/primereact/macro-theme.css` (each + `preview.html`).
- `preview/` — 18 foundation cards (`_preview.css` + pages: `brand-logo.html`, `type-{display,body,mono}.html`, `colors-{brand,light,market,neutrals,status}.html`, `spacing-{scale,radii-shadow,motion}.html`, `component-{buttons,inputs,grid,badges,tabs-menu,ticket}.html`).
- NOT fully offline despite README.txt's claim: the six `ui_kits/*/index.html` pages pull React 18.3.1, ReactDOM, Babel standalone, and Lucide from unpkg, and the two Prime storybooks (`themes/primereact/preview.html`, `themes/primeng/preview.html`) pull Prime themes/primeicons from unpkg. Only `workspace.html`, the `preview/` foundation cards, and the ag-grid/theme CSS storybooks are CDN-free. README.txt mentions a `dist/` folder that is not tracked here — that content is `design-system/MacroTheme/`.

### design-system/MacroTheme/ — two-file CDN distribution
Per `design-system/MacroTheme/README.md`: whole system via one `<link>` + optional `<script>`. Files: `macro.css` (~30 KB; `macro.min.css` ~19 KB) and `macro-ag-grid.js` (~5 KB; `macro-ag-grid.min.js` ~3 KB), plus `demo.html` smoke test. `macro.css` layers: (1) Google Fonts `@import` (Roboto + IBM Plex Mono); (2) all design tokens, dark default on `:root` with a `.theme-light` override block (usable scoped, e.g. a light island in a dark app); (3) base elements (`.mono`, `.num`, `.up`/`.down`, `.bid`/`.ask`, scrollbars); (4) PrimeNG/PrimeReact **Aura** overrides — buttons incl. directional `.p-button.macro-bid` / `.p-button.macro-ask`, inputs, DataTable, Dialog, Toast, Tree, Chip/Tag/Badge, density via `data-density="tight"|"cozy"` (20/32px rows; default 22px); (5) AG Grid companion CSS (`.mkt-flash-up`/`.mkt-flash-down` tick flashes, `.mkt-up`/`.mkt-down`/`.mkt-num` cell classes). Load order: Aura base theme **before** `macro.css` (Macro only overrides Aura's custom properties). The AG Grid v33 Theming API is a JS object, so it ships separately: `macro-ag-grid.js` (UMD + ESM) exposes `window.macroTheme` and `buildMacroTheme({ themeQuartz, iconSetMaterial, colorSchemeDarkBlue })`; it uses inlined JS constants (not CSS vars) — customize via `.withParams({...})`. Consumer token overrides: redefine `--brand` etc. on `:root` after `macro.css`. Versioning: pin jsDelivr tags (`@v1.0.0`).

### design-system/figma-make-share/ — kit for pasting the system into Figma Make
Figma Make works from pasted context (no library import). Files per `design-system/figma-make-share/README.md`:
- `FIGMA_MAKE.md` — the master brief ("the one that matters"): every token as concrete hex (surfaces `#0b0d12`→`#1e222a`, text `fg-1..4`, brand `#2aa6e6`, market `mkt-up #34d97a` / `mkt-down #ff6b64` + bg/flash alphas, status, light-theme overrides), type scale (10px ALL-CAPS labels → 32px display; nothing below 10px), 2px spacing base / 22px grid rows / control heights, radii/shadows/motion (600ms price-tick flash; no glassmorphism), component recipes (buttons, inputs, grid, status pills, tags, tabs, panels, drawer tickets), layout rules, terse copy rules (K/MM/BN sizes, Unicode minus, no emoji, Working/Filled/Cancelled/Rejected vocabulary), Lucide iconography (only ▲▼ and ● glyphs allowed), and an example rates-blotter prompt.
- Usage options: A — paste FIGMA_MAKE.md as first context; B — screenshot `macro-reference.html` (self-contained visual board) and say "match this"; C — paste `macro.min.css` (a separate snapshot, 18,871 bytes — not byte-identical to `MacroTheme/macro.min.css`).
- The two "sacred rules" to re-assert when output drifts: **green/red = directional only** (never success/error in the data area) and **every number is mono + tabular**. No live link back — regenerate and re-share on changes.

### design-system/CapitalMarketsIcons/ — 144-icon light+dark SVG system
144 unique icons × two themes: `dark/svg/*.svg` (144, bg `#10141e`) and `light/svg/*.svg` (144, bg `#ffffff`). Tile spec: 56px tile, 8px padding, 8px corner radius, 40px glyph, 100% opacity. Nine color-coded functional groups: Pricing `#00d4aa` (20), PnL `#34d399` (12), Risk `#f59e0b` (16), Trading `#3b82f6` (16), E-Trading `#06b6d4` (18), Middle Office `#8b5cf6` (13), Ticketing `#ec4899` (11), Back Office `#f97316` (16), Workspace `#64748b` (22). Generators:
- `generate-icons.mjs` — icon data (id + trading label, e.g. `balance` = "Greeks / DV01") sourced from the catalog page; writes the themed SVGs plus per-theme `dark/generate-png.html` / `light/generate-png.html` ("Download All PNGs" — renders the Material Icons font to canvas in a browser).
- `generate-standalone-icons.mjs` — fetches Material Symbols **Sharp** paths from `fonts.gstatic.com` to emit self-contained SVGs (no font dependency); PNGs via `sharp` if installed, else the HTML route.
Catalog/showcase page at repo path `design-system/capital-markets-icon-system-all.html` ("Capital Markets — Icon System").

### design-system/rates/ — rates e-trading branding
App icon set (256px rounded dark tile `#12141a` with red/green price-bar rects): `app-icon.svg` plus status variants distinguished by a badge at (200,200) — `app-icon-live.svg` (green `#34d97a` dot), `app-icon-halted.svg` (red `#ff6b64` square), `app-icon-alert.svg` (amber `#f5c13a` dot) — and `app-mark-flat.svg`, `favicon.ico`. `rates/toolbar/` holds 14 trading-toolbar SVGs: `buy`, `sell`, `order`, `rfq`, `cancel`, `cancel-all`, `depth`, `ladder`, `positions`, `watchlist`, `working`, `filled`, `halt`, `route`. Showcase page: `design-system/rates-etrading-icon-showcase.html` ("Rates E-Trading Icon System", with an icon-config panel).

### AgenticUI/ — Macro Front Office Transformation
`AgenticUI/README.md` ("Macro Front Office Transformation — Agentic AI for UI Modernization"): move legacy .NET front-office screens into governed Angular/React MFEs running in Macro Desktop (OpenFin Workspace) via an "agentic UI factory". Principles: prompt-first, governed build, desktop-ready. Six-stage pipeline:
1. **Intake** — capture legacy screen, workflow, success criteria (owner: Product + UX)
2. **Figma Make (AI Design)** — first-pass UI, variants, flows (AI + UX)
3. **Macro MCP Server (Govern)** — guardrails, routes, desktop hooks; stamps the governed repo (AI + Platform)
4. **MFE Libraries (Compose)** — auth, logger, ag-Grid wrapper, themes, shared components (Front-end Eng)
5. **GitHub + OpenShift (Deploy)** — repo, PR, tests, release image (Platform)
6. **Macro Desktop (Run)** — Storefront, Dock, color linking, FDC3, entitlements (Desktop + Entitlements)

Four human review gates: **UX sign-off → Security / Auth → PR quality → Desktop UAT**. In this repo the pipeline is concretely backed by `libs/mcp-core` (`@macro/mcp-core` — the macro-mcp servers' `import_figma_app` tool imports Figma Make output and adopts macro tokens; plus scaffold/register tools) and by `design-system/figma-make-share/` (the stage-2 design context). Also in the folder: binary decks `MM1.pptx`, `MM2.ppt`, `MM3.pptx`, `AgenticUIDev.pdf`, and `README - Copy.md` — a stale longer draft of README.md that additionally retains "Traditional UI Development — The Pain Points" and a Traditional-vs-Agentic "What Changes" table.

### tools/generate-favicons.ps1 and tools/extract-dependencies.js
- `tools/generate-favicons.ps1` (`pwsh -File tools/generate-favicons.ps1`): dependency-free brand-favicon generator. Parses the rect-only SVG `apps/macro-workspace/public/MacroThemeCondensed/assets/favicon.svg` directly (viewBox + `<rect>` x/y/w/h/rx/fill), rasterizes 16/24/32/48/64/128/256px frames with .NET `System.Drawing` (anti-aliased rounded-rect paths), assembles a PNG-compressed multi-resolution `favicon.ico` (Vista+/Chromium/OpenFin; needed because Windows taskbar/.lnk shortcuts can't use SVG), then writes `favicon.ico` + copies `favicon.svg` into exactly five app folders: `apps/macro-workspace/public`, `apps/macro-angular/public`, `apps/macro-angular-fdc3/public`, `apps/macro-react/public`, `apps/capital-markets-themes/public` (missing dirs skipped with a warning; prism/prism-react are not targets).
- `tools/extract-dependencies.js` (`node tools/extract-dependencies.js` from the repo root): dependency inventory. Reads `package-lock.json` (the `packages` map, recursing nested `dependencies`) and writes two files to cwd: `npm-all-dependencies.txt` (sorted unique `name@version` lines) and `npm-all-dependencies.json` (per-package `name`, `version`, `path`, `license`, `dev`, `optional`, `resolved`, `integrity`).

---

## OpenFin platform configuration (apps/macro-workspace/public)

All OpenFin platform configuration is static JSON served by the macro-workspace dev server (port 4202) and is **environment-scoped**: every config file exists twice, under `apps/macro-workspace/public/local/` and `apps/macro-workspace/public/openshift/`. The environment is selected by the `?env=` query param on the provider URL (`resolveEnvConfigPath(fileName)` in `libs/openfin/src/lib/config-path.ts`: `?env=openshift` → `/openshift/<file>`, `?env=local` or anything else/absent → `/local/<file>`). Local files use `http://localhost:42xx` URLs; openshift files use `https://{{OPENSHIFT_*_HOST}}` placeholder tokens (`OPENSHIFT_WORKSPACE_HOST`, `OPENSHIFT_ANGULAR_HOST`, `OPENSHIFT_REACT_HOST`, `OPENSHIFT_PRISM_HOST`, `OPENSHIFT_PRISM_REACT_HOST`, `OPENSHIFT_RATES_HOST`, `OPENSHIFT_LOG_SERVER_HOST`, `OPENSHIFT_STORAGE_{DEV,UAT,PROD}_HOST`) substituted at deploy time.

Config files split into two tiers (see `resolveConfigUrl()` in `libs/openfin/src/lib/storage/storage-context.ts`):

- **Storage-served tier** — `apps.json`, `dock-config.json`, `storefront-config.json`, `snap-config.json`: when the active workspace-storage environment is REST-backed, these load from the storage service's `/config/<name>` endpoints (e.g. `http://localhost:3000/workspace/v1/config/apps`) with a **static-file fallback** on outage (`configHttpWithStaticFallback` in `libs/openfin/src/lib/angular/index.ts`); in `local` storage mode they load straight from the static per-env folder.
- **Bootstrap tier** — `settings.json` and `entitlements.json`: ALWAYS load from the static per-env folder via `resolveEnvConfigPath` (they define the storage environments and the user identity, so they cannot depend on the storage API).

Launch: `npm run launch` runs `node apps/macro-workspace/launch.mjs http://localhost:4202/local/manifest.fin.json` (uses `@openfin/node-adapter` on Windows, a `fins://` link on macOS).

### `manifest.fin.json` — platform/runtime manifest (NOT the app registry)

Key contents (local):

```json
{
  "devtools_port": 9090,
  "licenseKey": "openfin-demo-license-key",
  "runtime": { "arguments": "--v=1 --inspect --enable-mesh --security-realm=macro-workspace", "version": "44.146.101.5" },
  "platform": {
    "uuid": "macro-workspace-platform",
    "name": "Macro Workspace",
    "providerUrl": "http://localhost:4202/provider?env=local",
    "autoShow": true, "allowLaunchIntoPlatform": true,
    "appLogsTimezone": "utc", "enableAppLogging": true,
    "logManagement": { "enabled": true, "url": "http://MontyUbuntu2604:8000" }
  }
}
```

- Provider window geometry: `defaultWidth/defaultHeight/minWidth/minHeight` 280, `maxWidth` 280, `maxHeight` 600, `resizable: false`, `frame: false`, `alwaysOnTop: true`, `showTaskbarIcon: false`, `cornerRounding {12,12}`, `shadow: true`, `saveWindowState: false`.
- `permissions`: `System.launchExternalProcess` enabled with an asset `srcRule` allowing `https://cdn.openfin.co/release/snap/*` (for Snap), plus `terminateExternalProcess`, `downloadAsset`, `serveAsset`, `openUrlWithBrowser` (http/https/mailto), `readRegistryValue`, `registerCustomProtocol`/`unregisterCustomProtocol`/`checkCustomProtocolState`, `launchLogUploader`; full `Clipboard` read/write (text/html/rtf/image); `webAPIs`: `clipboard-read`, `clipboard-sanitized-write`, `notifications`, `openExternal`, `fullscreen`.
- `appAssets`: `openfin-snap` 1.6.0 from `https://cdn.openfin.co/release/snap/1.6.0/snap.zip`, target `OpenFinSnap.exe`.
- `shortcut` (desktop + start-menu) and `supportInformation` blocks.
- Openshift differences: runtime args drop `--inspect`; `providerUrl` is `https://{{OPENSHIFT_WORKSPACE_HOST}}/provider?env=openshift`; `logManagement.url` is `https://{{OPENSHIFT_LOG_SERVER_HOST}}`; the `shortcut`/`supportInformation` company fields still read "OpenFin" (local says "Macro Workspace").

### `apps.json` — app registry (source of truth for store + dock + home)

Shape: `{ "apps": [ ... ] }`; each entry has `appId`, `name`, `title`, `description`, `manifest` (view-manifest URL), `manifestType`, `icons[{src}]`, `contactEmail`, `supportEmail`, `publisher` (`"OpenFin"` for all), `intents: []`, `images[{src}]` (screenshots shown on the storefront app-details page, served from `public/screenshots/`), `tags[]`, `category`. Loaded by `AppsService` (`libs/openfin/src/lib/apps.service.ts`): fail-soft (malformed payload or fetch failure degrades to an empty registry, never a dead platform), and LOB store apps from the storage API's `/store-apps` are merged in at load, registry winning on `appId` collision.

All 15 registered apps (identical in both envs apart from URL hosts and the `local/` vs `openshift/` manifest path segment):

| appId | title | category | manifestType | tags | screenshot |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `macro-workspace-view1` | Macro Workspace View 1 | Showcase | view | view, openfin, showcase | — |
| `macro-workspace-view2` | Macro Workspace View 2 | Showcase | view | view, openfin, showcase | — |
| `macro-angular-view` | Macro Angular App | Showcase | view | view, angular, market-data, showcase | — |
| `macro-angular-fx-market-data` | FX Market Data | Showcase | view | view, angular, fx, market-data, showcase | `fx-market-data.png` |
| `macro-angular-treasury-microstructure` | Treasury Microstructure | Showcase | view | view, angular, treasury, market-data, showcase | `treasury-microstructure.png` |
| `macro-angular-risk-pnl` | Risk / PnL | Showcase | view | view, angular, risk, pnl, showcase | `risk-pnl.png` |
| `macro-react-view` | Macro React App | Showcase | view | view, react, market-data, showcase | — |
| `macro-react-treasury-market-data` | Treasury Market Data | Showcase | view | view, react, treasury, market-data, showcase | `treasury-market-data.png` |
| `macro-angular-fdc3-instrument-viewer` | FDC3 Instrument Viewer | Showcase | view | view, angular, fdc3, instrument, showcase | — |
| `macro-react-commodities-dashboard` | Commodities Dashboard | Showcase | view | view, react, commodities, market-data, showcase | `commodities-dashboard.png` |
| `macro-analytics-dashboard` | Analytics Dashboard | Showcase | view | view, analytics, monitoring, showcase | — |
| `rates-desktop` | Rates E-Trading Desktop | Showcase | **manifest** | platform, rates, fixed-income, showcase | — |
| `capital-markets-themes` | Capital Markets Theme | Showcase | view | view, react, figma-make, showcase | `capital-markets-themes.png` |
| `prism-blotter` | Prism — Blotter as a Service | **Blotters** | view | view, angular, blotter, amps, nats, solace | `prism-blotter.png` |
| `prism-react-blotter` | Prism Blotter (React) | **Blotters** | view | view, react, blotter, amps, nats, solace | `prism-react-blotter.png` |

Every registry app carries category `Showcase` except the two Prism apps (`Blotters`). Business-area categories (FX / Rates / Commodities / Risk / Spread / Middle Office) are deliberately NOT used by the registry — they are LOB-owned and populated at runtime via the storage API's `/store-apps`. `rates-desktop` is the only `manifestType: "manifest"` entry (a full external platform): local `http://localhost:8080/assets/openfin/app.platform.fin.json`, openshift `https://{{OPENSHIFT_RATES_HOST}}/assets/openfin/app.platform.fin.json`.

### `dock-config.json` — Dock 3.0 favorites + content menu

Two top-level arrays. `favorites[]` is the visible dock bar; `contentMenu[]` holds the dropdown contents.
