A `favorites` entry of `type: "folder"` renders as a dock **dropdown** whose children are resolved from the top-level `contentMenu` folder with the **same `id`** (Dock 3.0 folder merging — the favorites entry itself has no `children`). `type: "item"` entries would be plain launch buttons; the current bar has none.

Current bar (both envs identical up to icon host):

- `favorites[0]`: folder `id: "showcase"`, label "Showcase", icon `icons/platform.svg`
- `favorites[1]`: folder `id: "prism"`, label "Prism Blotters", icon `icons/prism.svg`

`contentMenu` folder `showcase` contains sub-folders **Angular Apps** (`angular-apps`: Macro Angular App, FX Market Data, Treasury Microstructure, FDC3 Instrument Viewer, Risk / PnL), **React Apps** (`react-apps`: Macro React App, Treasury Market Data, Commodities Dashboard), **Workspace** (`workspace-views`: View 1 (FDC3 Broadcaster), View 2 (FDC3 Listener)), **Platforms** (`platforms`: Rates E-Trading Desktop), plus direct items Analytics Dashboard and Capital Markets Themes. `contentMenu` folder `prism` contains Prism (Angular) + Prism (React). Item shape: `{ "type": "item", "id": "cm-...", "label", "icon", "appId" }` — items reference registry `appId`s, so the registry stays the launch source of truth. Icons here are plain URLs; Dock 3.0 also accepts `{light, dark}` theme-variant objects.

At runtime `Dock3Service` (`libs/openfin/src/lib/dock3.service.ts`) merges LOB dock apps from the storage API's `/dock-apps` after the config-driven entries, fail-soft per entry with `lob:`-prefixed ids so published content can never collide with the config ids (`showcase`, `prism`) — Dock 3.0 keys folder merging purely by id, so an id collision would attach children to the wrong dropdown. Per-user 📌 dock pins arrive separately as `pin:`-namespaced favorites.

### `storefront-config.json` — storefront navigation, landing page, footer

Consumed by `StorefrontConfigService` (`libs/openfin/src/lib/storefront-config.service.ts`; cached single fetch; on load failure falls back to an EMPTY config `{navigation:{sections:[]}}` so the store still renders) and `StoreService` (`libs/openfin/src/lib/store.service.ts`).

Navigation model (`navigation.sections[]`, identical both envs):

```json
{ "id": "capital-markets", "title": "Capital Markets", "items": [
    { "id": "fx",          "title": "FX",          "category": "FX",          "tags": ["fx"] },
    { "id": "rates",       "title": "Rates",       "category": "Rates",       "tags": ["rates"] },
    { "id": "commodities", "title": "Commodities", "category": "Commodities", "tags": ["commodities"] },
    { "id": "risk",        "title": "Risk",        "category": "Risk",        "tags": ["risk"] },
    { "id": "spread",      "title": "Spread",      "category": "Spread",      "tags": ["spread"] } ] }
```

plus section `desk-library` "Desk & Library" with items **Middle Office** (`category: "Middle Office"`, tags `["middle-office"]`), **Blotters** (`category: "Blotters"`, tags `["blotters"]`), **Showcase** (`category: "Showcase"`, no tags), **All Apps** (`category: "*"` = wildcard, all apps). Matching semantics (`store.service.ts`): an app matches a nav item when its `category` equals the item's `category` (or the item is `"*"`) **OR** any app tag matches any item tag, case-insensitively — so the Capital Markets items are populated by LOB `/store-apps` publications while registry apps land under Showcase/Blotters.

Hard OpenFin caps enforced in `store.service.ts`: `MAX_NAV_SECTIONS = 3`, `MAX_NAV_ITEMS = 5` per section (excess sliced with a logged warning). A dynamic **Favorites** section (title from `navigation.favoritesTitle`, default `"Favorites"`) is prepended automatically whenever the user has favorited apps — occupying one of the 3 section slots, which is why the config defines exactly 2 sections.

`landingPage`: `topRow` "Featured" = category `Showcase` (capped at 4 apps; falls back to all apps if the row resolves empty), `middleRow` "Markets" = category `"*"` (capped at 6), `bottomRow` empty (`appIds: []`, capped at 3). Rows resolve by explicit `appIds[]` first, else `category`. `footer`: text "Macro Workspace — Capital Markets" + Support (`mailto:support@macro.example.com`) and Documentation links (max 3 links). `cardClickBehavior: "show-app-details"` — clicking a card body opens the storefront app-details page (description, `images` screenshots, publisher/contact/support) while the Launch button launches; the service default when the key is absent is `"perform-primary-button-action"`.

### `settings.json` — platform settings, browser settings, storage environments (bootstrap tier)

```json
{
  "platformSettings": { "id": "macro-workspace-platform", "title": "Macro Workspace Starter",
                        "icon": "http://localhost:4202/MacroThemeCondensed/assets/favicon.svg" },
  "browserSettings": {
    "allowDuplicatePageTitles": true,
    "indicators": { "suppressWorkspaceSwitched": false, "suppressWorkspaceSaved": false },
    "tabSearchButton": { "pageTabs": { "enabled": true }, "viewTabs": { "enabled": true } }
  },
  "storage": {
    "defaultEnvironment": "local",
    "environments": {
      "local": { "mode": "localStorage", "label": "Local (this machine)" },
      "dev":  { "mode": "rest", "label": "DEV",  "baseUrl": "http://localhost:3000/workspace/v1" },
      "uat":  { "mode": "rest", "label": "UAT",  "baseUrl": "https://workspace-storage-uat.example.com/workspace/v1" },
      "prod": { "mode": "rest", "label": "PROD", "baseUrl": "https://workspace-storage.example.com/workspace/v1" }
    }
  }
}
```

`browserSettings` maps to Workspace v24 browser options. The `storage` block defines the unified Workspace Storage environments consumed by `initWorkspaceStorage()`; openshift replaces the three REST `baseUrl`s with `https://{{OPENSHIFT_STORAGE_{DEV,UAT,PROD}_HOST}}/workspace/v1` (its `dev` label is also `"DEV"`; `defaultEnvironment` stays `local` in both).

### `entitlements.json` — demo entitlements (bootstrap tier)

Identical in both envs. `currentUser`: `{ id: "u-001", name: "Macro Trader", groups: ["fx-desk","rates-desk"], entitlements: ["fx-trader","rates-trader","general-user"] }`. `apps` maps appId → required entitlement list: FX Market Data + FDC3 Instrument Viewer require `fx-trader`; Treasury Microstructure, Treasury Market Data, rates-desktop require `rates-trader`; Commodities Dashboard requires `commodities-trader` and Analytics Dashboard `analytics-viewer` (neither held by u-001, so those two are filtered by entitlement checks). Apps absent from the map are unrestricted.

### `snap-config.json` — OpenFin Snap

Identical in both envs: `{ "enabled": true, "serverOptions": { "showDebug": false, "disableUserUnstick": false, "keyToStick": false, "disableGPUAcceleratedDragging": false, "disableBlurDropPreview": false, "autoHideClientTaskbarIcons": true } }`. Pairs with the `openfin-snap` appAsset + `launchExternalProcess` srcRule in the manifest.

### `dos.json` + `npm run dos` / `npm run dos:restore` — Desktop Owner Settings pin

`dos.json` (identical in both envs) pins the workspace system-app versions:

```json
{ "desktopSettings": { "systemApps": {
    "workspace": { "version": "24.0.19" },
    "notification-center": { "version": "2.15.0" } } } }
```

Why: Workspace 24.0.19 (HERE Core UI) is on the **Beta** channel; without the pin the RVM serves the Stable 23.2.x browser/home UI and v24 features (tab search, page pinning) silently disappear. `scripts/dos.mjs` (Windows-only; exits 0 with a message elsewhere) writes registry value `DesktopOwnerSettings` under `HKCU\Software\OpenFin\RVM\Settings` pointing at `http://localhost:4202/local/dos.json`, backing up any prior value to `scripts/.dos-backup.json`; `--restore` re-applies the backup or deletes the value if none existed. All OpenFin processes must be restarted for the RVM to re-read it.

### View manifests (`<name>.fin.json`)

Every view manifest has the identical three-key shape — only `url` varies:

```json
{ "url": "<app url>", "fdc3InteropApi": "2.0", "interop": { "currentContextGroup": "green" } }
```

FDC3 2.0 is enabled on every view and all views join the `green` context group (shared context/instrument broadcasting works out of the box). URLs — local → openshift:

| Manifest | local url | openshift url |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `view1.fin.json` / `view2.fin.json` | `http://localhost:4202/view1` / `/view2` | `https://{{OPENSHIFT_WORKSPACE_HOST}}/view1` / `/view2` |
| `macro-angular-view.fin.json` | `http://localhost:4200` | `https://{{OPENSHIFT_ANGULAR_HOST}}` |
| `macro-angular-fx-market-data.fin.json` | `:4200/fx-market-data` | `{{OPENSHIFT_ANGULAR_HOST}}/fx-market-data` |
| `macro-angular-treasury-microstructure.fin.json` | `:4200/treasury-microstructure` | `{{OPENSHIFT_ANGULAR_HOST}}/treasury-microstructure` |
| `macro-angular-risk-pnl.fin.json` | `:4200/risk-pnl` | `{{OPENSHIFT_ANGULAR_HOST}}/risk-pnl` |
| `macro-angular-fdc3-instrument-viewer.fin.json` | `http://localhost:4203/instrument-viewer` | `http://localhost:4203/instrument-viewer` (unsubstituted — stale) |
| `macro-react-view.fin.json` | `:4201/macro-react/` | `{{OPENSHIFT_REACT_HOST}}/macro-react/` |
| `macro-react-treasury-market-data.fin.json` | `:4201/macro-react/treasury-market-data` | `{{OPENSHIFT_REACT_HOST}}/macro-react/treasury-market-data` |
| `macro-react-commodities-dashboard.fin.json` | `:4201/macro-react/commodities-dashboard` | `{{OPENSHIFT_REACT_HOST}}/macro-react/commodities-dashboard` |
| `analytics-dashboard.fin.json` | `:4202/analytics` | `{{OPENSHIFT_WORKSPACE_HOST}}/analytics` |
| `capital-markets-themes.fin.json` | `:4202/MacroThemeCondensed/workspace.html` | `{{OPENSHIFT_WORKSPACE_HOST}}/MacroThemeCondensed/workspace.html` |
| `prism.fin.json` | `http://localhost:4204/` | `https://{{OPENSHIFT_PRISM_HOST}}/` |
| `prism-react.fin.json` | `http://localhost:4205/` | `https://{{OPENSHIFT_PRISM_REACT_HOST}}/` |

Gotcha: the openshift `macro-angular-fdc3-instrument-viewer.fin.json` still points at `http://localhost:4203/instrument-viewer` — the only openshift view manifest without a host token.

### Icons, screenshots, and theme presets (shared `public/` root)

- `public/icons/` — per-app SVGs referenced by `apps.json` and `dock-config.json`: `angular-app.svg`, `react-app.svg`, `platform.svg`, `platform-taskbar.svg`, `view-broadcaster.svg`, `view-listener.svg`, `fx-market-data.svg`, `treasury-market-data.svg`, `treasury-microstructure.svg`, `commodities-dashboard.svg`, `risk-pnl.svg`, `prism.svg`, `prism-react.svg`; plus icon packs `icons/capital-markets/{light,dark}/` (144 themed Material-style SVGs each + `index.json` catalog) and `icons/material/` (115 SVGs + an `index.json` catalog).
- `public/screenshots/` — 8 PNGs consumed by `apps.json` `images[]` for the app-details page: `capital-markets-themes.png`, `commodities-dashboard.png`, `fx-market-data.png`, `prism-blotter.png`, `prism-react-blotter.png`, `risk-pnl.png`, `treasury-market-data.png`, `treasury-microstructure.png`.
- Workspace-chrome theme preset JSONs at the `public/` root (env-agnostic, fetched as `/<file>` by `ThemePresetService` in `libs/openfin/src/lib/theme-preset.service.ts`): `macro-etrading-theme.json`, `default-theme.json`, `carbon-graphite.json`, `midnight-trader.json`, `executive-blue-theme.json`, `stagecoach-red-theme.json` are wired into the `PRESETS` list; `neo-quantum-theme.json` and `structured-credit-pro-theme.json` exist on disk but are not registered.

### Local vs openshift differences (exhaustive)

`dock-config.json`, `storefront-config.json`, `snap-config.json`, `dos.json`, `entitlements.json` are byte-identical after `http://localhost:4202` → `https://{{OPENSHIFT_WORKSPACE_HOST}}` substitution (dock) or entirely identical (the rest). Real differences: `manifest.fin.json` (no `--inspect`, host tokens, `?env=openshift`, "OpenFin" company strings), `settings.json` (storage REST `baseUrl` tokens), `apps.json` (manifest URLs point at `/openshift/*.fin.json` and `{{OPENSHIFT_RATES_HOST}}`), and the view-manifest `url`s per the table above (with the FDC3 instrument-viewer exception).

---

## Workspace Storage API contract (docs/api) & other docs

### `docs/api/workspace-storage-api.openapi.yaml` — the storage contract

OpenAPI **3.1.0**, `info.title: Macro Workspace Storage API`, **`info.version: 1.2.0`** (2,734 lines). This is the normative contract for the unified Workspace Storage service: phase 1 is the in-memory reference implementation mounted at `/workspace/v1` on `apps/market-data-server` (port 3000); phase 2 is a planned Java Spring Boot + MongoDB service. The canonical TypeScript client is `RestWorkspaceStorageClient` in `libs/openfin/src/lib/storage/rest-storage-client.ts`; the client-side interface is `WorkspaceStorageClient` in `libs/openfin/src/lib/storage/storage-types.ts`. `externalDocs` points at `https://github.com/CapitalMarketsMacro/macro/tree/master/libs/openfin/src/lib/storage`.

Declared servers:

```yaml
servers:
  - url: 'http://localhost:3000/workspace/v1'   # phase-1 reference (market-data-server)
  - url: 'https://workspace-storage-{env}.example.com/workspace/v1'  # env ∈ dev|uat|prod, default dev
```

One deployment exists **per environment** — the environment is selected purely by **base URL** (never a path segment, header, or query param). The platform's `settings.json` `storage.environments` block maps names to base URLs; active-env precedence at boot is `?storageEnv=` → user's saved choice → `defaultEnvironment` → `local`.

#### Path inventory (14 paths, 28 operations)

| Path | Methods | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `/health` | GET | Liveness probe; unauthenticated; returns at minimum `{"status":"ok"}` (`HealthStatus`, `additionalProperties: true`). |
| `/workspaces` | GET | List the user's saved workspaces **including full snapshots**; `[]` (never 404) when none. Optional `?query=` = case-insensitive substring filter on `title` or `workspaceId`. |
| `/workspaces/{workspaceId}` | GET, PUT, DELETE | Get / full-document-upsert / delete one saved workspace (opaque `snapshot`). |
| `/pages` | GET | List the user's saved pages including full layouts; `[]` never 404. |
| `/pages/{pageId}` | GET, PUT, DELETE | Get / upsert / delete one saved page (opaque `layout`). |
| `/dock/{dockProviderId}` | GET, PUT, DELETE | The user's dock customization (`DockProviderConfigWithIdentity`); GET 404 = never customized → platform falls back to the environment default; DELETE resets to default. |
| `/dock-apps` | GET | List LOB-published dock apps. NOT user-scoped; `[]` never 404; sorted by `sortOrder` asc, missing last, **stable** sort. Read by the platform at dock init (`icon` → dock button, `dropdown` → dock folder). |
| `/dock-apps/{dockAppId}` | GET, PUT, DELETE | Get / publish / unpublish one LOB dock app. PUT is the **validated** write path for LOB tooling (the platform only reads). DELETE removes it for ALL users. |
| `/store-apps` | GET | List LOB-published full store apps. NOT user-scoped; `[]` never 404; same stable `sortOrder` sort. Merged into the app registry at platform load — **registry (apps.json) wins on `appId` collisions**; storefront nav routes each app by `category` OR any tag, case-insensitively (FX, Rates, Commodities, Risk, Spread, Middle Office). |
| `/store-apps/{storeAppId}` | GET, PUT, DELETE | Get / publish (validated) / unpublish one LOB store app. |
| `/favorites` | GET, PUT | Per-user singleton ordered list of favorited appIds. GET **never 404s** — returns `{"appIds":[]}` when never saved. PUT replaces the whole list; order is significant and preserved. |
| `/preferences` | GET | List all of the user's `{key, value}` preferences; `[]` never 404. |
| `/preferences/{key}` | GET, PUT, DELETE | One named JSON preference. PUT body is the wrapper `{"value": <any JSON>}` so `null`/scalars round-trip unambiguously. Well-known keys: `last-saved-workspace`, `theme-preset`, `view-titles`. |
| `/config/{configName}` | GET, PUT | Per-environment platform config; `configName` enum: `settings \| apps \| dock-config \| storefront-config \| snap-config \| entitlements`. GET is NOT user-scoped/unauthenticated. PUT is a **phase-2 admin operation** — the reference implementation returns `501 Not Implemented` (problem+json). |

#### Cross-cutting conventions

- **User scoping (interim)** — security scheme `userId`: `apiKey` in header **`X-User-Id`** (opaque string; reference impl defaults to `anonymous` when absent). Applied globally EXCEPT `GET /health`, `GET /config/{configName}`, and all `/dock-apps` + `/store-apps` routes (shared across users; `X-User-Id` ignored when sent). Phase 2 swaps in OAuth 2.0 / OIDC bearer JWT (`Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`, user id from the `sub` claim); the wire contract of every route is otherwise unchanged.
- **PUT semantics** — full-document upsert keyed by the path id: `201` on create, `200` on replace, **both carry the stored representation as the body**. The body's identifier MUST equal the path id (`body.workspaceId`, `body.pageId`, `body.id`, `body.appId` respectively) else `400`.
- **Opaque vs validated documents** — `Workspace.snapshot`, `Page.layout`, and everything in `DockConfig` beyond `id` are **opaque JSON owned by the OpenFin client** (shape tracks the Workspace SDK version); servers MUST persist and return them verbatim, never bind them to typed models. `LobDockApp`/`LobStoreApp` are the opposite: fully validated on PUT with `additionalProperties: false` at the top level and inside `children[]`/`icons[]`/`images[]`, and 400 problem+json whose `detail` names the offending field.
- **ETag / If-Match** — single-resource GETs and PUT responses carry an `ETag` header (opaque revision, e.g. `"7"`). PUT/DELETE accept an **optional** `If-Match`; when present and stale the server MUST return `412 Precondition Failed`. The phase-1 client never sends `If-Match` (last-writer-wins); phase-2 servers SHOULD fully support it.
- **Error model** — every error body is **RFC 9457 Problem Details** (`application/problem+json`): `{type (default about:blank), title, status, detail, instance?}` (`ProblemDetail` schema; `title`+`status` required).
- **Client behavior servers can rely on** (implemented in `RestWorkspaceStorageClient`): single-resource GET 404 → resolves `undefined`; DELETE 404 → treated as success (idempotent end-to-end); every other non-2xx throws; **any non-204 success MUST carry a JSON body** — the client unconditionally calls `response.json()` on 2xx ≠ 204.

#### Key schemas (35 total in `components.schemas`)

- `ProblemDetail`, `HealthStatus`.
- `Workspace` — required `workspaceId`, `title`; `snapshot` opaque (`getCurrentSync().getSnapshot()`), typical serialized size **100 KB–2 MB**; free-form `metadata`. `WorkspaceSummary` — snapshot-less projection, **not returned by any phase-1 endpoint**; reserved for a future `fields=summary` list projection.
- `Page` — required `pageId`, `title`; opaque `layout`; SDK-owned `isReadOnly`.
- `DockConfig` — only `id` is contractual; everything else opaque (`additionalProperties: true`).
- `LobDockApp` — required `id`, `label`, `iconUrl`, `type: icon|dropdown`. Conditional requirements (server-enforced, deliberately NOT modeled as `oneOf`): `icon` → non-empty `url`; `dropdown` → non-empty `children[]` of `LobDockAppChild` (each requires `id`, `label`, `url`; optional `iconUrl` falls back to parent). Optional `lob` (content-menu grouping), `sortOrder` (finite number). **`tooltip` is RESERVED** — accepted and stored but not rendered (Dock 3.0 entries have no hover-text field; kept so publishers need no migration if the SDK grows tooltips). `additionalProperties: false`.
- `LobStoreApp` — required `appId`, `title`, `manifest`, `manifestType: view|manifest`, `icons` (≥1 `Image` = `{src}` only); optional `description`, `images` (details-page screenshots), `publisher` (platform defaults it to `lob` when omitted), `contactEmail`, `supportEmail`, `tags` (non-empty strings; also routed to nav), `category`, `lob` (the platform force-adds a `lob` tag as metadata), `sortOrder`. `manifestType: view` launches as an embedded platform view; `manifest` launches another OpenFin platform.
- `Favorites` (`{appIds: string[]}`), `Preference` (`{key, value}`), `PreferenceValueWrapper` (`{value}`).
- `ConfigDocument` = `oneOf` six shapes, selected by `configName`: `SettingsConfig` (`platformSettings` required + `BrowserSettings` + `StorageSettings` with `StorageEnvironment` `{mode: localStorage|rest, baseUrl?, label?}`), `AppsConfig`/`App`/`AppIcon` (registry entries incl. the enterprise `category` field), `DockSurfaceConfig` (the environment **default** dock — `DockFavoriteEntry` + recursive `DockContentEntry` = discriminated `item|folder` union — distinct from the user-scoped `/dock/{id}` resource), `StorefrontConfig` (nav sections max 5 items each — OpenFin caps nav at 3 sections × 5 items with the dynamic Favorites section occupying one slot; `cardClickBehavior: show-app-details|perform-primary-button-action`), `SnapConfig` (`keyToStick` is `boolean | 'ctrl' | 'shift'`), `EntitlementsConfig`/`EntitledUser` (appId → required-entitlement keys, **any-of** match; missing/empty entry = launchable by everyone).

#### Phase-2 implementation notes (Spring Boot + MongoDB, non-normative)

Embedded in `info.description`: collections `workspaces` (`{userId, workspaceId, title, snapshot, metadata, version, updatedAt}`, compound unique `{userId:1, workspaceId:1}`; store `snapshot` as `org.bson.Document`/`Map<String,Object>` — never typed POJOs; 100 KB–2 MB snapshots are far under Mongo's **16 MB cap** but enforce a request-size limit (e.g. 8 MB) and alert on outliers), `pages`, `dockConfigs` (unique `{userId:1, dockProviderId:1}`), `favorites` (one doc per user, unique `{userId:1}`; GET returns `{"appIds":[]}` — never 404), `preferences` (unique `{userId:1, key:1}`), `config` (one doc per name, seeded at deploy; PUT becomes the role-guarded admin write path), `dockApps`/`storeApps` (**no `userId` field** — shared like config but writable via this API, so validate on PUT). Optimistic concurrency = monotonic `version` field (Spring Data `@Version`) surfaced as the `ETag` value; compare on `If-Match`, 412 on mismatch; upserts bump atomically (`findAndModify`/`ReplaceOptions.upsert(true)`). Workspace list `query` = anchor-free case-insensitive regex with escaped input (or `$text`). PUT must return the stored representation (client requires a JSON body on non-204).

Changelog: **1.0.0** initial (workspaces/pages/dock/favorites/preferences/config/health) → **1.1.0** added `/dock-apps` → **1.2.0** added `/store-apps`. The reference implementation persists the shared LOB registries in the same debounced store file as user data, under top-level `dockApps`/`storeApps` keys (older store files without them load fine).

### `docs/api/workspace-storage-api.postman_collection.json` — contract test collection

Postman collection v2.1.0 schema, name **"Macro Workspace Storage API"** — **57 requests in 10 folders, every request carrying `pm.test` assertions** (status codes, body shapes, ETag round-trips, problem+json fields; e.g. `/health` asserts `service === 'workspace-storage-api'`, which the reference server returns). Collection-level auth is `apikey` → header `X-User-Id: {{userId}}`.

| Folder | Requests | Covers |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Health | 1 | `GET /health` |
| Workspaces | 10 | create → update (200) → list → `?query=` filter → single GET (captures ETag) → missing 404 → body/path id mismatch 400 → stale If-Match 412 → correct If-Match 204 → delete-missing 404 |
| Pages | 4 | create / list / single GET (ETag) / delete |
| Dock | 3 | PUT / GET / DELETE (reset) customization |
| Favorites | 3 | never-404 GET, PUT, round-trip GET |
| Preferences | 6 | string + object values, list, delete, deleted-key 404 |
| Config (not user-scoped) | 6 | GET apps/settings/dock-config/storefront-config, unknown name 404, `PUT /config/apps → 501` |
| Dock Apps (LOB, not user-scoped) | 10 | icon + dropdown publish, sortOrder-sorted list, two 400 validation cases (icon without url; dropdown with empty children), ETag capture, 412/204 deletes, cleanup |
| Store Apps (LOB, not user-scoped) | 10 | view + manifest publish, sorted list, two 400 cases (empty `icons`; `manifestType: 'snapshot'`), ETag capture, 412/204 deletes, cleanup |
| Scenarios | 4 | user isolation (seed as `{{userId}}`, list as `{{altUserId}}` must NOT see it), stale-If-Match PUT 412, cleanup |

Collection variables: `baseUrl` (`http://localhost:3000/workspace/v1`), `userId` (`u-001`), `altUserId` (`u-002`), `workspaceId` (`ws-postman-1`), `pageId` (`page-postman-1`), `dockProviderId` (`macro-workspace-platform-dock`), plus four ETag capture slots written by test scripts: `workspaceEtag`, `favoritesEtag`, `dockAppEtag`, `storeAppEtag`.

Running it: start the reference server (`npm run start:market-data-server`), then run the collection **top-to-bottom** — later requests reuse ETags captured by earlier ones, and every run creates what it deletes (self-cleaning). Via CLI:

```bash
npx newman run docs/api/workspace-storage-api.postman_collection.json \
  --env-var baseUrl=http://localhost:3000/workspace/v1 --env-var userId=u-001
```

### `docs/architecture.md` — Mermaid architecture overview

Four diagrams plus a "Key Conventions" and "Useful Commands" recap:

1. **System Context** (`flowchart TB`) — OpenFin RVM boots `macro-workspace` via `manifest.fin.json`; the platform fans out to Provider window, Workspace Browser/Snap, Storefront, Dock 3.0, Home; the Browser hosts eight views (macro-angular FX + Treasury Microstructure, macro-react Treasury + Commodities, FDC3 Instrument Viewer, prism, prism-react, Analytics Dashboard); views hit `market-data-server` over WebSocket (Prism via `/prism`), which fronts AMPS/Solace/NATS; the platform persists to the Workspace Storage API (local/REST); views interop over FDC3 2.0 context group `green`.
2. **Monorepo Layout** (`graph LR`) — the nine apps with ports (4200–4206, 3000) and ten `@macro/*` libs, with app→lib dependency edges (e.g. grid wrappers → `@macro/macro-grid-format`, prism apps → `@macro/prism-core`).
3. **Shared Library Dependencies** (`flowchart TB`) — lib-to-lib edges: grid wrappers wrap `macro-grid-format`, `prism-core` uses `transports`, `transports` uses `utils` (conflation), `openfin` uses `macro-design` (theme config), MCP servers use `mcp-core`, `market-data-server` implements workspace storage.
4. **Data Flow** (`sequenceDiagram`) — launch from Store/Dock → `createView()` → `TransportClient.connect()` → WebSocket to market-data-server → broker subscribe → tick data → RxJS observable → AG Grid render + `ConflationSubject` → live prices.

### Copilot guidance pair — AG Grid 36 / Format panel / Calc columns / Show Values As

- **`.github/copilot-instructions.md`** (92 lines, auto-loaded by GitHub Copilot) — the condensed rulebook: AG Grid `^36.0.0` + AG Charts `^14.0.0` are a matched pair; the two grid wrappers are line-for-line equivalent (change one → mirror the other); `@macro/macro-grid-format` has three entry points (`.` core framework-free, `./angular`, `./react` — panels excluded from tsc build, consumed from source); `format-registry.ts` is the single source of truth for both panel UIs (new numeric/boolean knobs MUST join `NUMBER_FIELD_KEYS`/`BOOLEAN_FIELD_KEYS`); **bake mode is mandatory** (`store.setBakeMode(true)` — without it, in-place mutation ↔ rebuild ↔ reconcile loops infinitely); persistence rides three side-channels absent from AG Grid's native `getState()` (`columnFormats` bare `colId→spec` map, `calculatedColumns`, `showValuesAs`); `applyGridState()` ordering is load-bearing (calc cols first → `setState` → `applyColumnState` for Show Values As → `store.restore`, which always clears first). Ends with a "top gotchas" list (silent v36 theme-param drops, `sameCalcSchema` freeze guard, pivot-only Show-Values-As modes rendering `#N/A`, `grandTotalRow: 'bottom'` naming).
- **`docs/copilot/ag-grid-36-format-panel-port.md`** (1,491 lines) — the full verbatim port guide the condensed file points to: §1 package/build boundary, §2 v36/charts-14 upgrade deltas (theme params, CSS selector renames in TWO stylesheet copies, selection API object form), §3 the complete framework-free format engine sources (`format-spec.ts`, `format-engine.ts`, `treasury.ts`, `fx-rate.ts`, `presets.ts`, `format-registry.ts`, `column-format-store.ts`, `tool-panel-def.ts`), §4 both tool-panel UIs + wrapper wiring, §5 calculated columns (+ the two freeze gotchas), §6 Show Values As, §7 persistence glue incl. the React ref-identity "pin", §8 reference consumer, §9 ordered adoption checklist, §10 verification, §11 consolidated gotcha index. Intended to make the feature set portable to another codebase.

### Other documents under `docs/`

- **`docs/prism/RELEASE-NOTES.md`** (224 lines) — "Prism — Blotter as a Service, Release 2026.07 · July 10, 2026". Narrative release notes: six transports behind one declarative source model (AMPS/NATS/JetStream/Solace snapshot+live matrix; WebSocket table protocol; REST snapshot-only with in-place diff Refresh — both lenient clients accept bare arrays/objects and `{rows}`/`{data}` envelopes), roll-up views, the IRS Risk & PnL demo book (DV01/KR01 tie-out, `dayPnl ≡ carry + rollDown + curve + newTrade + fees + residual`), a PR-numbered release timeline (#3 → #72), a compatibility matrix (AG Grid 36.0, Angular 21.2 zoneless, React 19.2, TS 6.0, OpenFin 24.0.19/FDC3 2.0), and known limitations (no feed auto-reconnect; grid layouts/format edits not yet persisted per source; sign-based conditional formatting only; AMPS demo needs a user-supplied commercial AMPS instance).
- **`docs/developer-workflow.html`** — self-contained slide-deck-style HTML ("Macro Desktop MFE — Developer Workflows") presenting the MCP automation story: Figma Make import workflow, AMPS Data Explorer / `amps_create_mfe` workflow, generated-app architecture, getting started.
- **`docs/macro-mcp-slide.md` / `.html` / `.pdf`** — single-slide content for the Macro MCP server: 11 tools (`import_figma_app`, `amps_create_mfe`, `amps_explore`, three scaffolders, `register_openfin_app`, four introspection tools), 12 `macro://` resources, 6 canned prompts.
- **`docs/statusMay2026/`** — `status.txt` (internal status-email draft announcing the platform live in UAT & PROD; describes a deployment superset with libraries not present in this repo, e.g. auth and a Deephaven adapter) plus `Macro-Desktop-Capabilities.html`/`.pdf` (capabilities deck).

Note: the API docs live under `docs/api/`; there is no other machine-readable contract in the repo — `apps/market-data-server`'s `/workspace/v1` routes and `RestWorkspaceStorageClient` are both written against this YAML.

---

## Tooling, testing & CI

### CI pipeline — `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

Workflow `CI`, single job `build-test` on `ubuntu-latest` (`timeout-minutes: 45`). Triggers: pushes to `master`, all pull requests, and `workflow_dispatch`. Steps:

1. `actions/checkout@v4`
2. `actions/setup-node@v4` with `node-version: 24` and `cache: npm`
3. `npm ci` — works with no flags because `.npmrc` sets `legacy-peer-deps=true` (see below)
4. `npm run build` → `scripts/build-ci.mjs`
5. `npm run test` → `scripts/test-ci.mjs`
6. **Verify consolidated outputs** (`if: always()`): `ls dist/apps`, `test -s coverage/lcov.info`, `test -s junit.xml` — fails the job if the merged reports are missing/empty even when earlier steps "passed"
7. `actions/upload-artifact@v4` (`if: always()`) uploads artifact **`test-reports`** containing `junit.xml` + `coverage/lcov.info`

Because steps 6–7 run `if: always()`, red builds still publish reports — `test-ci.mjs` deliberately merges whatever reports exist before exiting non-zero.

### Build entry point — `npm run build` (`scripts/build-ci.mjs`)

`build-ci.mjs` sets `process.env.CI = 'true'` in-process (a script rather than an inline env var because there is no portable inline syntax across cmd.exe and POSIX shells without `cross-env`), then runs `npm run build:apps:ci` and exits non-zero on any failure.

`build:apps:ci` in `package.json` is the **single source of truth for the CI app list/order**:

```
nx run macro-angular:build:production && nx run macro-angular-fdc3:build:production
&& nx run macro-react:build && nx run macro-workspace:build:production
&& nx run prism:build:production && nx run prism-react:build
&& nx run market-data-server:build:production && nx run macro-mcp:build:production
&& npm run build:mcp-agent
```

`build:mcp-agent` builds `macro-mcp-agent` then patches `dist/apps/macro-mcp-agent/package.json`, pinning its `amps` dependency to the exact version found in `node_modules/amps/package.json` (the `amps` client is a prerelease-versioned commercial package). Output goes to `dist/apps/<name>`. Note: `capital-markets-themes` is **not** in `build:apps:ci` (it has its own `npm run build:capital-markets-themes`). New apps must be added to `build:apps:ci` manually or CI will not build them.

### Test entry point — `npm run test` (`scripts/test-ci.mjs`)

Cross-platform runner that executes every project's unit tests with coverage and consolidates results. It sets `CI=true`, deletes stale `coverage/`, `reports/`, and `junit.xml`, then:

**Discovery / runner split.** Projects come from `npx nx show projects --with-target=test --json`. A project whose root contains a `jest.config.*` file (`/^jest\.config\.(c?[jt]s|mjs)$/`) is a Jest project; everything else (inferred `@nx/vitest` targets) is Vitest. Current split (15 projects with a `test` target):

- **Jest 30** (12): apps `macro-angular`, `macro-angular-fdc3`, `macro-workspace`, `prism`; libs `logger`, `macro-angular-grid`, `macro-design`, `macro-grid-format`, `openfin`, `prism-core`, `transports`, `utils`. Angular projects use `jest-preset-angular` 16.1.4; node libs use `ts-jest`. All extend root `jest.preset.js`.
- **Vitest 4** (3): `macro-react`, `prism-react`, `macro-react-grid` (coverage via `@vitest/coverage-v8`).
- **No test target**: `capital-markets-themes`, `market-data-server`, `macro-mcp`, `macro-mcp-agent`, `mcp-core`.

Execution: `npx nx run-many --target=test --projects=<jest…> --configuration=ci` (the `ci` configuration in `nx.json` `targetDefaults["@nx/jest:jest"]` turns on `{ci: true, codeCoverage: true}`) and `npx nx run-many --target=test --projects=<vitest…> --coverage`.

**LCOV merge.** Every `lcov.info` under `coverage/` (except the merged output itself) is concatenated into `coverage/lcov.info` with all `SF:` paths rewritten to be repo-root-relative: absolute paths are relativized; Vitest's project-root-relative paths are anchored using the `coverage/{apps|libs}/<project>/` directory prefix (with `..`/`.` segment normalization).

**JUnit merge.** Every `reports/*.xml` (named `<project>-junit.xml`) is merged into a root `junit.xml` with a synthetic `<testsuites name="macro" tests=… failures=… errors=… time=…>` root summing per-file totals. Because per-project reporters emit project-root-relative suite/class/file attributes, identical spec paths in different projects would collide — `anchorSuitePaths()` rewrites any `name`/`classname`/`file` attribute that resolves to a real file under the project root to a repo-root-relative path (mirrors the LCOV `SF:` rewrite). A bare `<testsuite>` root (no `<testsuites>` wrapper) is tolerated.

If any test batch fails, merging still happens, then the script exits 1 — so CI publishers get reports on red builds.

Other test scripts: `npm run test:all` = `nx run-many --target=test --all` (no coverage/merge); `npm run test:ci` aliases `npm run test`; `npm run test:apps:ci` is a legacy four-app chain.

### Per-runner report wiring

`jest.preset.js` (root, extended by every Jest config) wires the JUnit side-channel using the NX-provided project name:

```js
const projectName = process.env.NX_TASK_TARGET_PROJECT || 'unknown';
reporters: ['default', ['jest-junit', {
  outputDirectory: './reports', outputName: `${projectName}-junit.xml`,
  suiteNameTemplate: '{filepath}', classNameTemplate: '{filepath}',
  titleTemplate: '{classname} › {title}', addFileAttribute: 'true',
}]],
coverageReporters: ['lcov', 'text-summary'],
```

File-path-based suite/class names keep merged reports unique across projects. Per-project Jest configs set `coverageDirectory: '../../coverage/{apps|libs}/<name>'`; Angular ones use `jest-preset-angular`, `macro-design` uses a jsdom environment.

Vitest projects wire the same convention in their `vite.config.ts`/`.mts` `test` block, e.g. `apps/macro-react/vite.config.ts`:

```ts
test: {
  name: 'macro-react', watch: false, globals: true, environment: 'jsdom',
  reporters: ['default', ['junit', { outputFile: '../../reports/macro-react-junit.xml' }]],
  coverage: { reportsDirectory: '../../coverage/apps/macro-react', provider: 'v8',
              reporter: ['text-summary', 'lcov'] },
}
```

**Declared-outputs rule (NX cache).** Every one of the 15 test targets declares in its `project.json`:

```json
"outputs": ["{workspaceRoot}/coverage/{projectRoot}",
            "{workspaceRoot}/reports/{projectName}-junit.xml"]
```

NX cache replays restore only declared outputs. Since `test-ci.mjs` deletes `reports/` before every run, a cached test task with an undeclared JUnit output would silently drop that project from the merged `junit.xml`. Any new project's test target must declare both.

### NX configuration — `nx.json`

NX 23 (`nx` 23.0.0, all `@nx/*` plugins 23.0.0). Key settings:

- `defaultBase: "master"`; `workspaceLayout: { appsDir: "apps", libsDir: "libs" }`; `analytics: false`.
- `namedInputs`: `production` excludes eslint configs, `*(spec|test).*` files, `tsconfig.spec.json`, `jest.config.*`, `test-setup.*` from cache inputs.
- `targetDefaults` (all `cache: true`): `@angular/build:application`, `@nx/js:tsc`, `@nx/esbuild:esbuild` (each `dependsOn: ["^build"]`, inputs `production`/`^production`); `@nx/eslint:lint`; `@nx/jest:jest` with `passWithNoTests: true` and the `ci` configuration `{ "ci": true, "codeCoverage": true }` (its inputs include `{workspaceRoot}/jest.preset.js`).
- Plugins: `@nx/eslint/plugin` (target `lint`), `@nx/vite/plugin` (targets `build`/`serve`/`dev`/`preview`/`serve-static`/`typecheck`/`build-deps`/`watch-deps`), `@nx/vitest` (test target name `test` — this is how the React projects' test targets are inferred with no explicit executor).
- Generator defaults: `@nx/angular:application` → `e2eTestRunner: none`, eslint, css, **jest**; `@nx/angular:library` → eslint + jest; `@nx/react` application → babel, css, eslint, **vite** bundler; `@nx/react` library → **vitest**.

Command conventions: run tasks through NX (`npx nx run|run-many|affected`), never the underlying tool directly; prefix with the package manager (`npm exec nx …`) rather than a global CLI. Useful: `npx nx graph`, `npx nx affected --target=test|build`, `npx nx show project <name> --web`, `npx nx test <project>`.

### TypeScript base config & path aliases — `tsconfig.base.json`

`compilerOptions`: `target: es2015`, `module: esnext`, `moduleResolution: node`, `lib: ["es2020","dom"]`, `skipLibCheck`, `skipDefaultLibCheck`, `experimentalDecorators`, `emitDecoratorMetadata`, `importHelpers`, `sourceMap`, `declaration: false`, and **`"ignoreDeprecations": "6.0"`** — required because TypeScript is pinned at `~6.0.3` and the config still uses options TS 6.0 deprecates; without the flag `tsc` errors. Full alias list (`baseUrl: "."`):

```json
"@macro/logger":                    ["libs/logger/src/index.ts"],
"@macro/mcp-core":                  ["libs/mcp-core/src/index.ts"],
"@macro/utils":                     ["libs/utils/src/index.ts"],
"@macro/macro-angular-grid":        ["libs/macro-angular-grid/src/index.ts"],
"@macro/macro-react-grid":          ["libs/macro-react-grid/src/index.ts"],
"@macro/openfin":                   ["libs/openfin/src/index.ts"],
"@macro/openfin/theme-sync":        ["libs/openfin/src/lib/theme-sync.ts"],
"@macro/openfin/react":             ["libs/openfin/src/lib/react.ts"],
"@macro/transports":                ["libs/transports/src/index.ts"],
"@macro/transports/angular":        ["libs/transports/src/lib/angular/index.ts"],
"@macro/transports/react":          ["libs/transports/src/lib/react/index.ts"],
"@macro/macro-design":              ["libs/macro-design/src/index.ts"],
"@macro/macro-design/angular":      ["libs/macro-design/src/lib/angular/index.ts"],
"@macro/macro-design/react":        ["libs/macro-design/src/lib/react/index.ts"],
"@macro/macro-grid-format":         ["libs/macro-grid-format/src/index.ts"],
"@macro/macro-grid-format/angular": ["libs/macro-grid-format/src/lib/angular/index.ts"],
"@macro/macro-grid-format/react":   ["libs/macro-grid-format/src/lib/react/index.ts"],
"@macro/prism-core":                ["./libs/prism-core/src/index.ts"]
```

### npm install posture — `.npmrc`

```
legacy-peer-deps=true
```

TypeScript 6 is ahead of `@angular/build`'s declared peer range; legacy peer resolution keeps plain `npm ci` / `npm install` working identically on dev machines and CI without anyone remembering a flag. `package.json` also carries `overrides` pinning `shell-quote`, `esbuild`, `form-data`, `hono`, `tmp`, `piscina`, and `undici` (under `@angular/build`) — mostly security/compat pins.

### Lint & formatting

- **ESLint** (`eslint` 10, flat config): root `eslint.config.mjs` spreads `@nx/eslint-plugin` `flat/base` + `flat/typescript` + `flat/javascript`, ignores `**/dist` and vite/vitest `*.timestamp*` files, and enables `@nx/enforce-module-boundaries` as `error` with `enforceBuildableLibDependency: true` and a single permissive constraint (`sourceTag: '*'` → `onlyDependOnLibsWithTags: ['*']`) — boundaries are enforced structurally, not by tag taxonomy. Lint targets are inferred per-project by `@nx/eslint/plugin`.
- **Prettier** (`~3.6.2`): `.prettierrc` is exactly `{ "singleQuote": true }`; `.prettierignore` excludes `/dist`, `/coverage`, `/.nx/cache`, `/.nx/workspace-data`, `.angular`.
- **EditorConfig** (`.editorconfig`): UTF-8, 2-space indent, `insert_final_newline`, `trim_trailing_whitespace` (off for `*.md`, which also gets `max_line_length = off`). No `end_of_line` key is set.

### Helper scripts — `scripts/`

| Script | npm entry | Behavior |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `scripts/build-ci.mjs` | `npm run build` | CI build wrapper (above) |
| `scripts/test-ci.mjs` | `npm run test` | CI test runner + report merger (above) |
| `scripts/stop.mjs` | `npm run stop` / `npm run stop:all` | Stops dev servers via two strategies: (1) kill whatever LISTENs on ports discovered from `apps/<app>/project.json` + vite configs; (2) process-tree sweep of this repo's `nx serve`/`concurrently`/`vite`/`run-executor` node processes (critical on Windows where servers drift ports and orphan worker trees). Scoped to this repo; explicitly excludes MCP server processes so it never kills editor/agent tooling. `--with-openfin` (`stop:all`) also kills OpenFin. Node stdlib + `netstat`/`taskkill` or `lsof`/`ps`/`kill` only. |
| `scripts/dos.mjs` | `npm run dos` / `npm run dos:restore` | Windows-only (no-ops elsewhere). Sets/restores registry value `DesktopOwnerSettings` under `HKCU\Software\OpenFin\RVM\Settings` to `http://localhost:4202/local/dos.json`, backing up any prior value to `scripts/.dos-backup.json`. Needed while Workspace 24.0.19 is on the Beta channel — without the pin the RVM serves the Stable 23.2.x UI. OpenFin processes must be restarted after changing it. |
| `scripts/list-npm-packages.mjs` | — | Writes a package inventory report (from `package.json` + `package-lock.json`) into `reports/` |
| `scripts/amps-query-executions.ts` | — | Ad-hoc AMPS SOW query utility (`npx tsx scripts/amps-query-executions.ts`); queries topic `rates/executions` on `ws://MontyUbuntu2604:9008/amps/json` with `TOP_N = 10` |
| `scripts/amps-sow-query.ts` | — | Empty (0-byte) stub |

### Prism broker lab — `tools/prism-broker-lab/`

Local NATS/Solace brokers + a simulated-data feeder for the Prism blotters. Ports are chosen to match the seeded catalog `apps/prism/public/data-sources.json` so sources work with zero edits.

```bash
npm run prism:brokers        # docker compose -f tools/prism-broker-lab/docker-compose.yml up -d
npm run prism:feed           # node tools/prism-broker-lab/feeder.mjs (Ctrl-C to stop)
npm run start:prism          # http://localhost:4204
npm run prism:brokers:down   # docker compose down (add -v to wipe volumes)
```

`docker-compose.yml` (project name `prism-broker-lab`):

- **nats** — `nats:2.10-alpine` with `tools/prism-broker-lab/nats.conf`: TCP clients on **4222**, monitoring on **8222** (`http://localhost:8222`), **WebSocket on 9222** (`no_tls: true`) — browsers/OpenFin can only reach NATS over WebSocket, hence Prism and the feeder use `ws://localhost:9222`. JetStream enabled (`store_dir: /data`, `max_memory_store: 256MB`, `max_file_store: 1GB`), volume `nats-data`.
- **solace** — `solace/solace-pubsub-standard:latest` (`shm_size: 1g`, admin/admin, `system_scaling_maxconnectioncount=100`): web messaging (WS) on **8008** (`ws://localhost:8008`), SMF on **55555**, SEMP/admin UI on **8080**. Takes ~30–60 s to come up.
- **amps** — commented out: AMPS is commercial with **no public Docker image**. The catalog's AMPS entries expect `ws://<host>:9008/amps/json` (websocket/json transport) with SOW topics `fx_spot` (keyed by `symbol`) and `orders`; point them at an existing AMPS instance via `data-sources.json` or Prism's ad-hoc source dialog, or uncomment the service with a licensed image.

`feeder.mjs` publishes (fail-soft per broker via `Promise.allSettled` — one broker down never stops the others; shims `globalThis.WebSocket` from `ws` on Node < 22):

| Catalog source | Subject/topic | Cadence / mode | Key |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NATS FX Quotes | `macro.fx.quotes.<symbol>` | 250 ms streaming | `symbol` |
| NATS Analytics | `macro.analytics.events` | 1 s append | — |
| JetStream Positions | `macro.positions.<bookId>`, stream `POSITIONS` (`subjects: ["macro.positions.>"]`, `max_msgs_per_subject: 5`) | seeded + 1 s updates, last-per-subject snapshot | `bookId` |
| Solace UST Prices | `ust/prices/<cusip>` | 300 ms streaming, DIRECT delivery | `cusip` |

Field names (`bid/ask/mid`, `spread`, `changePercent`, `qty`, `pnl`, `dv01`, `yield/coupon`, `cusip/symbol/bookId`) are deliberately chosen to trigger Prism's capital-markets column inference. Env overrides: `NATS_WS`, `SOLACE_URL`, `SOLACE_VPN`, `SOLACE_USER`, `SOLACE_PASS`, `FEED=nats,js,solace` (subset). Gotcha: JetStream `LastPerSubject` is only a correct snapshot when each subject maps 1:1 to a row key. Serving Prism over `https` requires switching the catalog to `wss://` + broker TLS.

### MCP servers — `.mcp.json`

Five servers configured for coding agents:

```json
{ "mcpServers": {
    "ag-mcp":      { "command": "npx",  "args": ["ag-mcp"] },
    "primeng":     { "command": "npx",  "args": ["-y", "@primeng/mcp"] },
    "angular-cli": { "command": "npx",  "args": ["-y", "@angular/cli", "mcp"] },
    "tailwindcss": { "command": "npx",  "args": ["-y", "tailwindcss-mcp-server"] },
    "macro-mcp":   { "command": "node", "args": ["dist/apps/macro-mcp/main.js"] } } }
```

`macro-mcp` (custom scaffolding for this monorepo — apps, libs, OpenFin registration, Figma import) runs from `dist/`, so it requires a one-time `npm run build:mcp`. An additional **nx-mcp** server (NX workspace commands) is provided by the NX Claude Code plugin, not by `.mcp.json`.

### Checklist: adding a new application

1. Generate: `npx nx generate @nx/angular:application <name> --directory=apps/<name>` (defaults from `nx.json`: jest, eslint, no e2e). Pick a free serve port — 4200–4206 are taken; 3000 is the market-data-server.
2. Import shared CSS from `@macro/macro-design` in the app's `styles.css` before framework CSS.
3. OpenFin config is **environment-scoped** — do the next three in BOTH `apps/macro-workspace/public/local/` and `.../openshift/` (local = `http://localhost:42xx`, openshift = `{{OPENSHIFT_*_HOST}}` tokens): create a view manifest `<name>.fin.json` (`{"url": …, "fdc3InteropApi": "2.0", "interop": {"currentContextGroup": "green"}}`); register in `apps.json` (`appId`, `manifest` → view-manifest URL, `manifestType: "view"`, `icons`, `tags`, `category` — registry apps use `Showcase`); add a dock entry in `dock-config.json` inside the `showcase` content-menu folder (top-level `favorites[]` only for apps warranting dock real estate; folder favorites need a same-id `contentMenu` folder). Storefront nav usually needs no change. Add an icon under `public/icons/`.
4. **Register for CI**: append the app to the `build:apps:ci` script in `package.json` (otherwise `npm run build` skips it). Tests are auto-discovered by `npm run test`, but the test target's `project.json` must declare `{workspaceRoot}/reports/{projectName}-junit.xml` (plus `{workspaceRoot}/coverage/{projectRoot}`) in `outputs`, or NX cache replays silently drop it from the merged `junit.xml`.

### Checklist: adding a new shared library

1. `npx nx generate @nx/js:library <name> --directory=libs/<name> --bundler=tsc`
2. Add the alias to `tsconfig.base.json` `paths`: `"@macro/<name>": ["libs/<name>/src/index.ts"]`
3. Export the public API from `libs/<name>/src/index.ts`; declare the two test outputs as above.

---

## AI assistant & coding-agent configuration

The repo is instrumented for five different AI tooling surfaces — Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, OpenAI Codex, and generic `AGENTS.md`-reading agents — all pointing at the same instruction content and the same MCP servers.

### MCP servers — `.mcp.json`

Five stdio MCP servers are registered at the repo root (`.mcp.json`, `mcpServers` block):

| Server | Command | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `ag-mcp` | `npx ag-mcp` | AG Grid documentation search (version-aware) |
| `primeng` | `npx -y @primeng/mcp` | PrimeNG component documentation |
| `angular-cli` | `npx -y @angular/cli mcp` | Angular CLI tools, best practices, docs, examples |
| `tailwindcss` | `npx -y tailwindcss-mcp-server` | Tailwind CSS utilities and docs |
| `macro-mcp` | `node dist/apps/macro-mcp/main.js` | Custom monorepo scaffolding (apps, libs, OpenFin registration, Figma import, icon/library catalogs) |

`macro-mcp` runs from build output, so it requires a one-time `npm run build:mcp` (= `nx build macro-mcp`). An **nx-mcp** server (NX workspace queries/commands) is additionally provided by the NX Claude Code plugin (see `.claude/settings.json` below), not by `.mcp.json`.

### Agent instruction files — `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md`

- `CLAUDE.md` (285 lines, Claude Code) and `AGENTS.md` (287 lines, all other agents) are kept byte-identical apart from their headers: `AGENTS.md` prepends its own H1 plus a note "Kept in sync with `CLAUDE.md` (same content, different filename for non-Claude agents). When updating one, update both." Verify sync with `diff <(tail -n +4 AGENTS.md) <(tail -n +2 CLAUDE.md)` — empty diff confirms it (AGENTS.md body starts at line 4, CLAUDE.md at line 2).
- Content in ~3 strokes: (1) project map — app/port/command table, full `apps/` + `libs/` + `tools/` tree, key-files table; (2) critical architecture rules — `@macro/*` path aliases, single-source theming via `@macro/macro-design`, zoneless Angular conventions, OpenFin version-lockstep + per-env config layout, unified Workspace Storage API, real-time data patterns; (3) operational sections — testing/build/CI entry points, NX usage guidelines (skills-first), add-an-app/add-a-lib checklists, and a "Common Pitfalls" list.
- Convention (enforced by review practice): every docs change lands in both files in the same commit.

### GitHub Copilot — `.github/copilot-instructions.md`

Auto-loaded by Copilot (92 lines). A condensed quick-reference for the AG Grid 36 feature set: matched-pair `ag-grid ^36 / ag-charts ^14`, the two line-for-line-equivalent grid wrappers (`@macro/macro-angular-grid` / `@macro/macro-react-grid`), the framework-free `@macro/macro-grid-format` engine (bake mode is mandatory or you get an infinite reconcile loop), Format tool panel registration, calculated columns, Show Values As, the three persistence side-channels (`columnFormats`, `calculatedColumns`, `showValuesAs` — none live in AG Grid's native `getState()`), plus a "Top gotchas" list. It explicitly defers to the full verbatim port guide `docs/copilot/ag-grid-36-format-panel-port.md` (1491 lines: engine contracts, panel code, ordered port checklist §9, gotcha index §11) — read the full doc before implementing; the Copilot file is the rules-you-must-not-break summary.

### Claude Code — `.claude/`

- `.claude/settings.json`: sets `env.MCP_TIMEOUT: "60000"`; enables the NX Claude Code plugin (`enabledPlugins: { "nx@nx-claude-plugins": true }`) via an `extraKnownMarketplaces` entry sourcing GitHub repo `nrwl/nx-ai-agents-config`. The plugin supplies the nx-mcp server and the `nx-workspace` / `nx-generate` skills referenced in `CLAUDE.md`.
- `.claude/settings.local.json`: permission allowlist (WebSearch; WebFetch for `docs.adaptabletools.com` + `www.adaptabletools.com`; `Bash(grep *)`, `Bash(gh pr *)`, and one specific `node -e` package.json-inspection one-liner) and `enabledMcpjsonServers` explicitly approving all five `.mcp.json` servers.

### Cursor — `.cursor/`

- `skills/` — six skills, each a `SKILL.md` with name/description frontmatter: `nx-workspace` (workspace/project/target exploration; includes `references/AFFECTED.md`), `nx-generate` (generator discovery + scaffolding; invoke before exploring), `nx-run-tasks` (run build/test/lint/serve through nx), `nx-plugins` (find/install NX plugins), `link-workspace-packages` (fix workspace-package resolution via package-manager commands, not tsconfig hacks), `monitor-ci` (Nx Cloud CI monitoring; preferred over `gh`/`glab`).
- `commands/monitor-ci.md` — the `/monitor-ci` slash command: orchestrator for Nx Cloud CI pipeline monitoring and self-healing fixes; spawns the subagent, supports re-run continuation and `--fresh`.
- `agents/ci-monitor-subagent.md` — `ci-monitor-subagent` (frontmatter `model: fast`): polls Nx Cloud CI Attempt + self-healing status via the `ci_information` MCP tool and returns structured state; it reports only — apply/reject decisions stay with the orchestrator.

### OpenAI Codex — `.codex/`

- `config.toml`: `personality = "friendly"` plus `[mcp_servers.*]` blocks mirroring all five `.mcp.json` servers. Difference: `macro-mcp` uses an absolute **Windows** path — `node E:/MacroDesktopMFE/macro/dist/apps/macro-mcp/main.js` — so it is machine-specific (the `.mcp.json` equivalent is repo-relative `dist/apps/macro-mcp/main.js`).
- `migrate-to-codex-report.txt`: output of the Claude→Codex migration — found 2 instruction files (`CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`; no skills/commands/subagents), symlinked `AGENTS.md` to `CLAUDE.md` (they have since diverged into the two-header sync convention above), and converted the 5 MCP server entries into `config.toml`.

### VS Code — `.vscode/`

- `settings.json`: disables Codex-in-WSL (`chatgpt.runCodexInWindowsSubsystemForLinux: false`) and the Angular strict-mode prompt.
- `extensions.json`: recommends `nrwl.angular-console` (Nx Console), `esbenp.prettier-vscode`, `dbaeumer.vscode-eslint`, `firsttris.vscode-jest-runner`.
- `launch.json`: one debug configuration — "Debug market-data-server with Nx" (`npx nx serve market-data-server` with `NODE_OPTIONS=--inspect=9229`).

### This file — `llms-full.txt`

`llms-full.txt` (repo root) is the single-file, full-repo documentation for LLM consumption (llms.txt convention), regenerated from repo analysis; update it whenever a subsystem it documents changes.
